name_ja: 昇龍 by うかい亭
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 4F
geocode_note: Geocoded from 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町３番１９号 via GSI address search; 350m from Nagoya Castle center, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 西区樋の口町 / エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 4F
seats_text: 16 seats across three teppan counter sections (8 + 4 + 4)
hours_raw: Lunch Fri–Sun 11:30–14:30 (L.O. 12:30); Dinner daily except Wed 17:00–23:00 (L.O. 20:30); closed Wednesdays
cultural_comfort_note: High-end hotel teppanyaki counter with formal service and dress code (smart casual required); Japanese hospitality norms prevail but hotel setting means some English-language support is likely available.
vegan_detail: Menus center on Ukai premium wagyu beef, abalone, and Ise lobster — no vegan courses exist. The restaurant takes allergy and dietary restriction requests at reservation time, so a fully plant-based meal is not possible, but specific ingredient omissions may be arranged in advance with advance notice.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu or labeling. Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is standard in teppanyaki sauces and marinades. The restaurant states it collects allergy information at the time of reservation and cannot accommodate requests made upon arrival, suggesting some flexibility if notified in advance. No dedicated fryer — no frying occurs at teppan counters. Cross-contamination risk from shared cooking surfaces and soy-based sauces must be assumed unless the kitchen confirms specific accommodations.

bio.background: Katakiri Yuki was born in Hokkaido and joined Ukai Corporation in 2004 after culinary school. He trained at Ginza Ukai-tei and rose through kitchen ranks to earn the title "TEPPAN Cuisinier." From 2017 to 2024 he led Ukai's Taiwan expansion, heading Ukai-tei Kaohsiung and THE UKAI TAIPEI and training local kitchen staff. He returned to Japan as sous chef at Ginza Ukai-tei before being appointed head chef of Shoryu by Ukai-tei in 2025.
bio.origin: Hokkaido, Japan; Ukai Corporation (founded Hachioji, Tokyo)
bio.specialty: 「うかい極上牛」(Ukai premium wagyu) prepared tableside on the teppan; black abalone steamed with rock salt and served with liver risotto
bio.philosophy: Ukai's founding concept is "100 years of restaurant building" — building relationships through food. The restaurant aims to deliver a culinary experience that engages all five senses, blending traditional Japanese aesthetics (origami-inspired interiors) with live teppan theatre overlooking Nagoya Castle.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's black abalone dish — rock-salt steamed and finished as a liver risotto — became the standout of opening month, earning a 4.9-star mention from a Tabelog award reviewer who called the flavour "rich and profound."

Shoryu is Ukai's first consulting-model restaurant outside its owned estate, operated under a newly established subsidiary UKAIzm Corporation (founded April 2025), making this Nagoya opening a milestone for the 100-year brand.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared teppan cooking surfaces are used for all courses; no information confirms separation of gluten-containing preparations from other dishes.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is used in teppanyaki sauces and marinades; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere on official sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All courses feature wagyu beef, seafood, and animal-derived stocks; no plant-only cooking surface or preparation area is available.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant states it collects allergy and dietary restriction information at reservation time: "当店では事前にアレルギーなど召し上がれない食材を伺っております" (We ask in advance about allergies and foods guests cannot eat). Requests made on arrival cannot be guaranteed.

positives.text: No deep-frying on the teppan, which eliminates fryer cross-contamination as a concern.

caveats: Opened October 2025; limited review data as of this research (18 Tabelog reviews). Lunch service (Fri–Sun only) confirmed from official site. Chef name confirmed as 片桐 優樹 from the hotel's official restaurant page. Pricing excludes 15% service charge.

neighborhood: 三の丸（名古屋市役所前・名古屋城南）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸3-1-1 名古屋市役所東庁舎B1F
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 三の丸3-1-1 returned [136.90657, 35.180832]; approx 0.8 km from target center (35.1856, 136.8997), well within 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: Counter and table seating; exact count not published. Government cafeteria footprint, can accommodate typical lunch crowds.
cultural_comfort_note: Ticket vending machine ordering system (食券制), no English menu or signage expected; a basic command of Japanese or pointing at machine buttons is sufficient.
vegan_detail: Standard Japanese udon and soba shop; dashi is almost certainly katsuobushi (bonito) or a bonito-kombu blend — no plant-based dashi advertised. Toppings such as kitsune (sweet abura-age) may be vegan-friendly as a component, but the broth itself is the barrier. No vegan labeling or options confirmed.
gf_detail: Both core dishes — udon and soba — are wheat-based (udon = wheat flour; soba typically contains wheat flour as a binder). No gluten-free alternatives listed. Standard soy sauce used in dashi tare contains wheat. Not suitable for celiac or wheat-allergy diners.

bio.background: A small noodle counter tucked into the basement (B1F) of Nagoya City Hall's East Building (名古屋市役所東庁舎), serving weekday office workers and visitors to the civic complex. The spot is open only during the lunch window on weekdays, functioning as an affordable canteen-style destination rather than a destination restaurant.
bio.specialty: Soba and udon sets — typically a noodle dish paired with a rice bowl component for under ¥999. Daily specials rotate.
bio.origin: 名古屋市中区三の丸 (Nagoya City Hall area)
bio.philosophy: Home-style flavors at reasonable prices; the restaurant participates in Nagoya City's food-waste-reduction program by offering appropriately sized portions.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers consistently praise the price-to-volume ratio, describing it as 'コスパが大変良いお店' (outstanding value for money). Peak lunch hour brings 10–15 minute waits; off-peak visits before or just after the rush are recommended.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Both udon and soba are wheat-based; wheat flour is pervasive in this kitchen. Cross-contamination risk is effectively certain for any wheat-sensitive diner.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or GF soy sauce mentioned; standard wheat-containing soy sauce assumed in dashi tare and seasoning.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito-based dashi is standard for this style of noodle shop; no plant-based broth option confirmed.

caveats: Weekday lunch only (11:00–13:30, Mon–Fri); closed all weekends and public holidays. Cash or IC transit card only — no credit cards or QR pay. Ticket vending machine ordering. No allergen menu or dietary accommodations confirmed. Not suitable for gluten-free or celiac diners.

cuisine: 喫茶・カフェ・モーニング
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市北区金城2-5-11
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned [136.901871, 35.197063] for 愛知県名古屋市北区金城二丁目５番１１号. Approximately 1.29 km from Nagoya Castle center (35.1856, 136.8997), within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 金城・名城公園（北区）
seats_text: 82席（カウンター含む）、グループ最大10名まで対応
closed_days: 元旦（1月1日）のみ休業、ほぼ年中無休
cultural_comfort_note: A classic neighborhood kissaten with a predominantly local Japanese clientele; English menus and staff English ability are unlikely, though the relaxed atmosphere and picture-rich menu make ordering straightforward for non-Japanese speakers.
vegan_detail: Standard Nagoya kissaten menu with morning sets (toast, boiled eggs), noodle dishes, rice dishes, and coffee. No vegan options are advertised. Morning sets rely on bread, butter, and eggs. Savory dishes likely use dashi (possibly katsuo-based) and standard soy sauce containing wheat. Rice-based morning options (porridge, egg-over-rice) may be the most vegan-adjacent items, but no confirmation of animal-free preparation exists.
gf_detail: A traditional kissaten offering toast-centric Nagoya-style morning service — wheat is central to the menu. No gluten-free options are listed, and no allergen information is provided. Bread comes from Homma Bakery (local kissaten supplier). Noodle and savory dishes almost certainly contain wheat via soy sauce and noodles. No dedicated fryer information available. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: Opened in July 2011, Kinshachi Koohiiten is a large neighborhood kissaten in Nagoya's Kita Ward, built around the concept of a community living room — 'a relaxed and comfortable space.' The main Kinjo location operates as part of the Kinshachi Club complex, which also houses mahjong, shogi, and go facilities on the premises, reflecting the café's role as a genuine community anchor.
bio.specialty: Nagoya-style morning service (モーニング): a beverage order automatically includes toast and a boiled egg. Bread is sourced from Homma Bakery, a local supplier used by established kissaten across Aichi Prefecture. The house-blend coffee uses carefully selected high-grade Arabica varieties, sold whole-bean or ground.
bio.philosophy: Centered on the kissaten ideal of unhurried time: 'nonbiri, yukkuri, kutsurogi space' (relaxed, leisurely, a place to unwind). The interior features all-sofa seating — including at the counter — and large magazine shelves, embodying the Nagoya kissaten tradition of making customers feel at home for as long as they like.

anecdotes.text: The shop's distinctive exterior surprises first-time visitors, and the exclusively sofa-seating layout — even at the counter — sets it apart from typical coffee shops. A 2016 article in GOTRIP described it as emblematic of Nagoya's broader kissaten culture, a city regarded as Japan's premier coffee-shop region.
Morning service alternatives include rice porridge and egg-over-rice alongside the standard toast options, a nod to the variety that long-established Nagoya kissaten offer to retain daily regulars.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No allergen or ingredient information is published. Standard Japanese kissaten soy sauce (containing wheat) is expected in savory dishes. No gluten-free soy sauce alternatives are mentioned.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy or staff training information is available from any published source. Guests with dietary restrictions should call ahead: 052-915-7705.

caveats: Payment: credit/debit cards are not accepted; PayPay QR-code payments are accepted alongside cash. The 82-seat venue includes a large free parking lot (reported as 32–58 spaces across sources). A second location (又穂店, Mataho) exists in Nishi Ward and closes Tuesdays; the Tabelog URL refers to the main Kinjo/Kurokawa-area store only.

name_ja: ザ ガーデンラウンジ
cuisine: カフェ・ラウンジ、和菓子、プレミアムティー、ランチ・ディナーコース
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町３番１９号 returned [136.896072, 35.185513]. Approximately 317m from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), well within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町（西区）
closed_days: なし（ホテル営業日に準ずる）
seats_text: 64席（個室1室あり、4名まで）
cultural_comfort_note: Hotel lounge in a luxury property with English menu available and international staff orientation; non-Japanese visitors will be fully at ease.
vegan_detail: Vegan accommodation is listed as available (with egg/dairy-free variants noted on reservation platforms), but must be requested at the time of booking. The core menu features wagashi, French-influenced course cuisine, and matcha — many elements involve dairy or egg. Hidden animal-derived ingredients (dashi, butter in French courses) are likely unless specifically excluded on advance request. Organic matcha and some tea-side wagashi may be naturally vegan, but this is not guaranteed across all items.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or dedicated GF claim. The kitchen uses wheat extensively in French-style lunch and dinner courses (bread, sauces, pastry). Allergen accommodations — including wheat — can be requested at reservation; however, the venue states it may decline if guest safety cannot be assured. Standard soy sauce (wheat-containing) is likely used in any Japanese-style preparations. No dedicated fryer information available. Celiac diners should contact the kitchen directly in advance and understand that accommodation is best-effort, not guaranteed.

bio.background: Chef Nakanishi was born in Aichi Prefecture and began his career spending approximately nine years at La Rochelle Tokyo under iron chef Sakai Hiroyuki. He then trained at a series of Michelin-starred French establishments including Au 14 Février, Jean Sulpice, Mirazur, and Flocons de Sel. Returning to Japan in 2015, he joined SUGALABO Tokyo at its opening and served as executive chef for seven years — during which the restaurant was ranked first in the world by La Liste in 2020. In October 2025, he assumed the role of executive chef at THE GARDEN LOUNGE within the newly opened Espacio Nagoya Castle.
bio.specialty: Premium matcha and wagashi alongside French-influenced seasonal lunch and dinner courses highlighting Aichi regional ingredients (Nagoya Cochin, Atsumi salmon, local vegetables)
bio.philosophy: The lounge reimagines Owari Nagoya's deep tea-ceremony and confectionery traditions for a contemporary hotel setting — a 'new chanoyu' rooted in regional identity rather than formality.

anecdotes.text: SUGALABO, where Nakanishi served as executive chef, was ranked No. 1 in the world by La Liste in 2020 — a global restaurant ranking distinct from Michelin, based on aggregated critic and diner data.

The lounge uses organic matcha from Ishikawa Seicha in Toyota, Aichi, prepared with a state-of-the-art CUZEN MATCHA machine — positioning it as a high-spec update on the traditional tea ceremony rather than a conventional café experience.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen runs French-style courses with bread, pastry, and sauced mains — significant wheat cross-contamination risk in a non-dedicated kitchen. No dedicated GF preparation area confirmed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No wheat-free soy sauce confirmed. Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is likely used in any Japanese-style preparations on the menu.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Vegan requests require advance notice at reservation. The kitchen routinely uses dairy and egg in French-style courses, so cross-contact risk exists without explicit kitchen communication.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant requests that guests disclose all food allergies before arrival, noting that post-arrival requests may not be accommodated. Even with advance notice, the venue reserves the right to decline accommodation if guest safety cannot be assured.

positives.text: Allergy information is formally collected at reservation stage, and the reservation platforms (ikyu, TableCheck) list vegan (egg/dairy-free) as an available accommodation category.

caveats: Opened October 2025; relatively limited review history as of research date. Dietary accommodation details sourced from reservation platform listings and official allergy policy statements rather than first-hand celiac/vegan diner accounts. Service charge of 15% applies. Dress code is smart casual.

name_ja: タンメン ニュータマヤ
cuisine: タンメン・ラーメン・中華麺
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区主税町2-26 主税町ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市東区主税町二丁目２６番地 returned [136.913483, 35.180649]. Distance from Nagoya Castle target (35.1856, 136.8997): ~1,368m — within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 東区主税町・東大手駅エリア
seats_text: カウンター9席、4人テーブル×4
cultural_comfort_note: 券売機(食券機)で外で先に食券を購入するシステム。日本語のみ対応。外国語メニューや英語スタッフの案内はなし。
vegan_detail: スープは鶏ガラベースと確認。唐揚げ・餃子・麻婆豆腐など動物性食材が全面的に使われており、植物性オプションの記載なし。
gf_detail: 中太ストレートの小麦麺を使用。スープには醤油（小麦含有）が使われており、グルテンフリー対応の記載は一切なし。麺・スープ・餃子の皮・唐揚げ衣すべてに小麦が含まれる。グルテン過敏症・セリアック病の方には不適。

bio.chef_name: 森下幸雄（もりした さちお）
bio.background: 2010年2月6日創業。名古屋市東区主税町のオフィス街に立つタンメン専門店。店主の森下幸雄氏がInstagramのプロフィールで「唯一無二、極上のタンメンを日々追求しております」と語るとおり、野菜たっぷりのタンメン一本に絞った町中華。
bio.specialty: 大陸タンメン（麻婆豆腐のせタンメン）。野菜・背脂・ニンニク無料増し対応。ランチは白飯・辛もやし・高菜の無料セルフサービスあり。
bio.philosophy: 「唯一無二、極上のタンメンを日々追求」。中本スタイルの辛味系タンメンにインスパイアされつつ独自路線を確立。シンプルな空間でひたすら麺と野菜の質にこだわる。

anecdotes.text: 店外の食券機で先に食券を購入するシステムを採用。夕方の口頭注文との使い分けがユニーク。
名古屋に蒙古タンメン中本がないため、その代替として「大陸タンメン」目当てに来る常連も多い。麻婆豆腐はデフォルト辛さ控えめで、卓上の花椒などで自分好みに調整できる。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 小麦麺を使用する専門店のため、厨房全体で小麦粉・小麦麺が常時使用されている。交差汚染のリスクは高い。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: スープに醤油を使用（小麦含有の一般醤油と推定）。無添加・グルテンフリー醤油の使用についての記載なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 鶏ガラスープ・唐揚げ・餃子など動物性食材が全品に使用されており、植物性対応の分離調理は期待できない。

caveats: Tabelog lists 25 seats (9 counter + 4 tables of 4); some sources cite 17 counter-only seats — possible discrepancy between sources or a layout change. Cash-only at lunch; PayPay accepted at dinner per Tabelog. Ekiten lists Saturday as a closed day but Tabelog and multiple blogs confirm Saturday is open; Sunday is the definitive closed day. Irregular closures are noted. No official website found beyond the Instagram account.

cuisine: 日本料理・会席・懐石
address_ja: 〒460-0001 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-1-1 名古屋能楽堂1F
geocode_note: GSI address search for 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸一丁目１番 returned [136.896591, 35.182434]. Approximately 448m from Nagoya Castle target point — well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区三の丸・名古屋能楽堂内（浅間町駅徒歩約9分）
seats_text: 全112席（テーブル24席、カウンター10席、個室7室、宴会ホール42席）。蓬左亭の個室は完全予約制。
hours_raw: 10:00〜17:00（L.O. 16:30）。蓬左亭個室の夜間営業は要問合せ・完全予約制。八百彦カジュアルコーナーは11:00〜15:00。
closed_days: 定休日不明（公式情報に記載なし。直接確認を推奨）
cultural_comfort_note: Full Japanese-language service in a formal kaiseki setting; English signage is minimal and staff interaction is primarily in Japanese — comfortable for Japanese-speaking visitors, potentially challenging without some Japanese ability or advance communication.
vegan_detail: Traditional kaiseki courses are built around dashi (typically katsuobushi/bonito-based), sashimi, and grilled fish — not vegan by default. No vegan-specific course is advertised. The kitchen works with seasonal ingredients and high-end traditional technique; advance notice and a direct call may allow for some accommodation, but this is not confirmed.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese kaiseki uses wheat-containing soy sauce (shoyu) extensively in sauces, marinades, and glazes, and wheat may appear in tempura batters and noodle dishes offered in the casual hōsa area (kishimen is a wheat noodle). No gluten-free course or tamari-only preparation is mentioned anywhere in available sources. Dedicated fryer status is unknown. Celiac diners should call ahead in Japanese to assess feasibility.

bio.background: 蓬左亭は2022年3月18日、名古屋城のほど近くにある名古屋能楽堂1階に開業した個室会席レストラン。江戸時代創業・300年以上の歴史を持つ仕出し料理の名門「八百彦本店」が運営し、同じ建物内の和フードホール「蓬左＜hōsa＞」の中核を担う。「蓬左」とは、かつて名古屋城内の尾張藩文庫「蓬左文庫」に由来し、尾張名古屋の食文化を未来へつなぐという理念を屋号に込めている。
bio.origin: 名古屋市中区三の丸・名古屋能楽堂内（2022年3月開業）
bio.specialty: 季節の食材を生かした会席料理「金城」（¥3,800〜）・「蓬左」（¥5,500）・「吉祥」（¥7,700）。料理は愛知の名匠に認定された近澤料理長が監修し、前菜・お造り・焼き物・季節の鍋物・デザートで構成。
bio.philosophy: 尾張名古屋の食文化を次世代へつなぐことをテーマに、300年以上の歴史を持つ八百彦本店・菓匠花桔梗・和スイーツ中俣という3ブランドが一堂に会する。能楽堂という格調ある空間で、正統派の日本料理をカジュアルなフードホールと個室会席の両面から提供する。
roles: 個室会席レストラン（蓬左亭）
セレクトショップ（hōsa select）

anecdotes.text: 開業にあたり「蓬左」という名は、江戸時代に名古屋城内に設けられた尾張徳川家の文庫「蓬左文庫」に由来し、地域の歴史と食文化を一体に捉えるコンセプトを体現している。
料理を監修する近澤料理長は、愛知県が認定する「愛知の名匠（卓越した技能者）」に選ばれており、伝統的な日本料理の技を現代に伝える存在として紹介されている。
八百彦本店は1日約5,000食の仕出し弁当を手がける老舗で、その技術と素材選びの知見を能楽堂の会席に投影。名古屋城観光とあわせて立ち寄れる場所として地元・観光客双方に親しまれている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat-containing soy sauce is standard in kaiseki sauces, marinades, and glazes. The casual hōsa area serves kishimen (wheat noodles), meaning wheat is actively used on-site. No separate gluten-free prep area is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Traditional kaiseki cooking relies heavily on wheat-based shoyu throughout multiple courses. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned in any available source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi stock (typically bonito/katsuobushi-based) is foundational to the kaiseki menu and permeates most courses including sauces and soups. No plant-based dashi option is advertised.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu or formal allergy-handling protocol is described in any available source. Reservation-only format (call 052-253-8566) provides the best opportunity to communicate dietary needs in advance.

positives.text: The reservation-only, private-room format means diners can communicate dietary needs directly with the kitchen in advance — a structural advantage over open-seating restaurants when special requests need discussion.

caveats: Closed days are not published in any available source — confirm by phone before visiting. The private kaiseki (蓬左亭) operates on a fully reservation-only basis; walk-ins are for the casual hōsa food hall area only. Dinner hours for the private kaiseki rooms may differ from the general 10:00–17:00 posted hours — one source mentions 17:00–21:00 evening service for group reservations (min. 10 persons at ¥8,800/person). Gluten-free and vegan suitability is unconfirmed; direct advance communication with the kitchen is essential for dietary-restricted diners.

cuisine: カフェ・コーヒー・サンドウィッチ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-5-1 マジマビル
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch from 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内三丁目５番１号; coordinates 35.177959, 136.906128 fall within target box and approx. 1.0 km from Nagoya Castle reference point — within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 丸の内・市役所（中区）
seats_text: 27 seats across two floors: 1F has 3 counter seats and 4 table seats; 2F has 4 counter seats and 12 table seats (approximately). Window seating available on both floors.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu exists. Core food items — thick-cut egg sandwiches, toast morning sets, baked cheesecake, cream puffs — all contain wheat. Plain black drip coffee and espresso drinks without added syrups are naturally gluten-free, but cross-contamination risk from baked goods in a small café kitchen is unconfirmed. No dedicated GF fryer or GF labeling found. Celiac diners should stick to black coffee only and ask staff before ordering anything food-related. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) may appear in kitchen prep (the egg sandwich uses dashi-flavored wasabi mayo).
vegan_detail: No vegan menu. Black coffee and straight espresso drinks are vegan; however milk-based lattes, flat whites, and caramel lattes are standard and animal-milk is default. Food items include egg sandwiches, butter toast, cheesecake, and cream puffs — all non-vegan. No plant-milk option confirmed. Vegans can reliably order black drip coffee or straight espresso only.
cultural_comfort_note: All signage and menus are in Japanese; staff are welcoming and the café has a warm neighborhood feel, but no English-language service is documented — basic Japanese or pointing at the menu board will suffice for ordering coffee.
caveats: Hours on Tabelog and various blogs differ slightly (some list 17:30 close, one older source lists 19:00); the Tabelog listing showing 17:30 is treated as current. Additional irregular closures occur beyond Monday. A second location (Q.O.L.COFFEE BREWERS) operates in Nagoya PARCO — this record covers the original Marunouchi main store only.

bio.background: Born in Nagoya in 1982, Shima Yuya dreamed of running a coffee shop from childhood, inspired by visits with his parents. After high school basketball and culinary school, he worked at Italian restaurants and coffee shops across Aichi Prefecture, then spent years as a store manager at Kato Coffee Shop. Hungry to go deeper, he moved to Melbourne — the world's coffee capital — where he submitted 50 resumes before landing his first café job. The experience sharpened both his barista skills and his sense of what a single cup can mean. He returned to Nagoya and opened Q.O.L.COFFEE in June 2017.
bio.origin: 名古屋市中区丸の内（2017年6月開業）
bio.specialty: Self-roasted specialty drip coffee — approximately 10 bean varieties roasted on a Giesen W6A semi-hot-air roaster, served with a bean identification card; also known for thick-cut egg sandwiches with wasabi-dashi mayo.
bio.philosophy: Q.O.L. stands for Quality of Life. Shima believes that a great cup of coffee can elevate everyday living — clothing, food, shelter — and sources only world-grade specialty beans, visiting farms and judging competitions (including Cup of Excellence) to honor each producer's intention.

anecdotes.text: Shima submitted 50 resumes in Melbourne before getting his first café callback — the rejection streak only deepened his conviction that each cup matters.
A landscape architect and artist designed the café's interior, with organic shadow-casting elements that give the two-story Majima Building space its distinctive aesthetic.
Every cup of drip coffee comes with a small card identifying the bean's origin, variety, and roast profile — a practice Shima adopted to help customers build vocabulary around what they taste.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated GF kitchen or fryer confirmed. Baked goods (cheesecake, cream puffs, toast) are prepared in the same small café space as all beverages; cross-contamination cannot be ruled out.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The egg sandwich filling uses wasabi mayonnaise with dashi flavoring; standard Japanese dashi may include wheat-containing soy sauce. No GF soy sauce alternative is documented.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Milk and dairy products are used throughout the menu (lattes, cheesecake, butter toast, cream puffs); plant-milk alternatives are not confirmed available.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published allergy policy or staff allergy-handling protocol found. Reservations are accepted by phone (052-746-9134), which is the recommended channel to ask about specific dietary needs before visiting.

positives.text: Plain black drip coffee and straight espresso are naturally free of gluten, dairy, and animal products — a safe baseline for both celiac and vegan diners.

name_ja: しぼりや 丸の内店
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-7-17
geocode_note: GSI address search on 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内二丁目7番17号 returned [136.900024, 35.175873]; 1.08 km from Nagoya Castle target point, within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（丸の内駅より徒歩6分）
cuisine: フライ専門・食堂（海老フライ・白身フライ定食）
vegan_detail: A fried-food specialist: every set meal centers on breaded and deep-fried shrimp or white fish. No plant-based or egg/dairy-free options are listed. Panko breadcrumbs and frying oil shared with seafood make a vegan-safe order effectively impossible here.
gf_detail: The core menu is deep-fried foods coated in wheat-based panko breadcrumbs. Soy sauce used in Japanese set-meal condiments almost certainly contains wheat. No gluten-free alternatives or dedicated fryer are mentioned. This place is not safe for people with celiac disease or serious gluten sensitivity.
seats_text: カウンター席あり。個室なし。正確な席数は非公開。
cultural_comfort_note: A no-frills weekday lunch counter with a Japanese-only menu and ordering style typical of local teishoku spots; basic pointing or pointing at a menu picture will get you by, but staff are unlikely to speak English.

bio.background: しぼりや 丸の内店は、愛知県南知多町内海に構える老舗旅館「会席料理・お宿 しぼりや」が2026年3月13日に名古屋・丸の内に開いたランチ専門の食堂。伊勢湾に面した旅館が磨いてきた海産物の仕入れルートと揚げ物の技術を、都市のサラリーマン向けの定食として昇華させたかたちで出店した。
bio.origin: 南知多町内海（愛知県）の旅館が母体
bio.specialty: 海老フライ・白身フライ定食
bio.philosophy: 旅館の食堂の落ち着いた雰囲気をそのままに、揚げたてのフライ定食を手頃な価格で提供する。
roles: 旅館直営のランチ食堂

anecdotes.text: 南知多の旅館「しぼりや」が手がける都市型フライ専門食堂として2026年3月にオープン。旅館仕込みの揚げ物と「宿の食堂」を思わせる穏やかな雰囲気が特徴とされる。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 揚げ物専門店のため、小麦パン粉を使用したフライが主力メニュー。共用の揚げ油で複数の食材が揚げられることが強く推定され、グルテン交差汚染のリスクは高い。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 日本の定食文化における醤油・だしの使用が想定され、通常の醤油には小麦が含まれる。グルテンフリー醤油の提供は確認されていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全メニューが魚介類・動物性食材を使用したフライ定食であり、ビーガン対応の選択肢は存在しない。

caveats: 開店が2026年3月と非常に新しく、口コミ・ブログ記事がほぼ存在しない。公式SNSや独自サイトも確認できず、情報は食べログ掲載内容に依存している。営業時間・定休日はTabelog掲載情報に基づくが、変更の可能性がある。席数の正確な情報は非公開。PayPay使用可だがカード・電子マネー不可。

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 4F
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町３番１９号 returned coordinates [136.896072, 35.185513]. Approximately 0.33 km from Nagoya Castle centroid — well within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 西区樋の口町（浅間町駅徒歩約10分）
seats_text: 24席。2・4・6名用の個室あり。
cultural_comfort_note: Fine-dining venue within the Espacio Nagoya Castle hotel complex; English-language website available and international fine-dining conventions apply.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan course. The omakase format is built around French technique with Tokai-region animal proteins (seafood, meat). Allergy and dietary restrictions are collected at the reservation stage, but the restaurant explicitly notes that guests with wide-ranging allergies may not be accommodated. Vegan diners should confirm directly before booking — hidden animal-derived ingredients (butter, cream, dashi equivalents in French contexts) are inherent to the format.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu or GF-certified preparation confirmed. French fine-dining sauces, pastry components, and bread service routinely contain wheat. Staff collect allergy information prior to arrival, but the restaurant warns that broad allergies cannot always be accommodated in this omakase format. No dedicated fryer data available. Celiac diners should call 052-908-8738 (weekdays 13:30–15:00) well before booking to discuss feasibility.

bio.background: 1995年北海道生まれ。調理師専門学校を経て、南青山の「Lus」、代官山のミシュラン一つ星「Abyss」で研鑽を積んだのち、2020年にミシュラン三つ星「カンテサンス」に入店。2024年にはパリのミシュラン二つ星「レストラン・ブラン」で一年間修業。2025年帰国後、ブリアンスのシェフに就任。30歳。パティシエはブリアンス同期の松本健志（1993年熊本生まれ、カンテサンス出身、パリのジャック・ジェナンおよびマサイ・クタで修業）。
bio.specialty: 東海地方の食材を用いたオマカセ10皿コース（3ヶ月ごとに全面刷新）。冬季の「オマール・テルミドール」が代表的な一皿として言及される。
bio.philosophy: カンテサンス岸田周三シェフが提唱する「プロデュイ（食材）・キュイソン（火入れ）・アッセゾンヌマン（味付け）」の三原則を継承しつつ、名古屋という地域性を加え、素材の本来の持ち味を最大限に引き出すことを追求する。
roles: Producer / Executive Concept (岸田周三 / Quintessence)

anecdotes.text: 岸田周三シェフは東京・品川の「カンテサンス」を19年連続ミシュラン三つ星に導いた後、自身が初めて手がける地方レストランとしてブリアンスを名古屋に開業。パスカル・バルボの「アストランス」→「カンテサンス」→「ブリアンス」という系譜が記事でたびたび強調されている。
高瀬シェフはアスリートが0.1秒を削る感覚で料理の精度を追求する、と語る。「美味しさの小数点以下」を磨き続けることが自身の料理哲学だと述べている。
2025年4月、カンテサンスの看板デザート「ガトー・オー・コンテ」がブリアンスでも販売開始。唯一無二の味が名古屋でも楽しめると話題になった。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No specific cross-contamination controls for gluten have been confirmed. The kitchen prepares a multi-course French tasting menu that routinely includes pastry and bread; shared preparation surfaces are expected.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce used in a French kitchen context, but wheat appears in standard French sauces, roux, pastry, and bread service inherent to this omakase format.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen is not vegan-dedicated. Butter, cream, and other animal products are central to the French technique used throughout the omakase.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Allergy and dietary restriction information is collected from all members of the party at the reservation stage. The restaurant explicitly states that guests with wide-ranging allergies may not be able to be accommodated due to the fixed omakase format.

positives.text: Pre-reservation allergy screening is conducted for every guest — the restaurant proactively requests allergy information before confirming the booking.

caveats: Hours shown reflect the pattern reported at opening (Nov 2025); the restaurant operates on an irregular closure schedule. Confirm current hours and availability via OMAKASE platform or by calling 052-908-8738 (weekdays 13:30–15:00). Lunch service (Fri–Sun) may be subject to seasonal changes. Age restriction: 16 and over only. 15% service charge applies. Under-¥30,000 per person is not achievable here — the fixed course starts at ¥30,800 plus service.

name_ja: 担々麺専門店 麺屋•高豚 浄心店
cuisine: Tantanmen (Sichuan-style spicy sesame noodles), pork-bone broth specialty
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋3-25-14 ルミアイレブン 1B
geocode_note: GSI geocoder resolved 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋3-25-14 to [136.898071, 35.193489]. Approximately 887m north of Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), well within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 浄心（西区）
vegan_detail: All confirmed menu items are built on pork-bone (tonkotsu) broth and feature chashu pork. No plant-based broth or vegan option is listed or mentioned anywhere. Not suitable for vegans.
gf_detail: Standard wheat noodles are the core product. The broth almost certainly contains wheat via soy sauce (regular shoyu uses wheat). No gluten-free noodle option or allergen accommodation is documented anywhere. Celiac diners should not eat here.
seats_text: Counter seating only; very small shop. The parent Menya Haiton location has 10 counter seats; this branch is similarly compact.
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-only ramen specialist with no evidence of English menus or staff; ordering is straightforward (point or use ticket machine) but expect a fully Japanese-language environment.

bio.background: Menya Haiton (麺屋•高豚) is a small Nagoya-based ramen outfit that built a following with its rich pork-bone soy sauce bowls near Joshin Station. On April 18, 2025, the team opened this dedicated tantanmen branch — the third location of the group — in the basement of the Lumia Eleven building on Kaminagoya, specialising exclusively in Sichuan-style spicy sesame noodles.
bio.specialty: 麻辣担々麺 (Mala Tantanmen) — a Sichuan pepper–forward, house-chili-oil broth bowl; and 担々まぜそば (Tantan Mazesoba), a brothless mixed noodle dish. Both are original recipes created specifically for this branch.
bio.philosophy: Narrow focus, deep craft: every item on the menu is a variation on the tantanmen theme, allowing the kitchen to refine flavour and consistency around a single style.
roles: Third branch of the Menya Haiton group

anecdotes.text: The regular tantanmen is described by visitors as gentle and sesame-forward, with reviewers suggesting the 麻辣 (mala) version for those wanting more Sichuan heat.
Opened April 18, 2025 at 17:30, the opening announcement noted the branch would offer new tantanmen recipes not found at the original Menya Haiton locations.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat noodles are the core product at every station; airborne flour and shared cooking water make cross-contamination unavoidable.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is the expected seasoning base for the broth; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is documented.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth is pork-bone based; no plant-based cooking line exists. Cross-contact with pork products is inherent to every dish.

caveats: Hours from Tabelog show continuous service (11:15 onward) but the Instagram opening announcement stated a lunch/dinner split (11:30-14:00 / 17:30-22:00). The Tabelog data (30 reviews, more recent) is used as primary. Seat count is an estimate based on parent location; this branch's exact count was not published. Cash only confirmed; no electronic payments of any kind accepted.

name_ja: 名古屋とうふ 河口 金シャチ横丁店
cuisine: 豆腐料理・とうふソフト
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-2-3 金シャチ横丁 義直ゾーン
geocode_note: GSI geocode of 三の丸1-2 (chō-level); coordinates place the shop approximately 378m from Nagoya Castle — well within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 丸の内・久屋大通（名古屋城内金シャチ横丁義直ゾーン）
seats_text: Primarily a takeout counter with limited outdoor seating in front of the stall; no formal dining room.
hours_raw: 10:30〜17:00（金シャチ横丁公式サイトより）
closed_days: Not specified; follows Kinshachi Yokocho venue schedule — confirm with venue for holidays.
cultural_comfort_note: A takeout stall in a tourist-oriented market arcade next to Nagoya Castle; simple point-and-order counter that is welcoming but operates entirely in Japanese with no English menu signage noted.
vegan_detail: The core tofu products (handmade from domestic soybeans, natural sea salt nigari, and well water with no additives) are inherently vegan. The dengaku skewers use Hatcho-style miso (豆味噌 — soybeans and salt only, no wheat or dashi), which is also vegan. However, whether the tofu soft serve uses a dairy-based or fully soy-based soft-serve mix is unconfirmed — ask before ordering. No egg or fish-based dashi detected in the core menu, but confirm at the counter.
gf_detail: The shop's tofu is made from soybeans, natural sea salt nigari, and well water — no wheat. Critically, the dengaku miso sauce is Hatcho-style (八丁味噌/豆味噌), which is brewed from soybeans and salt only — no wheat, unlike most soy sauces or rice/barley miso. This is a meaningful GF positive. However: (1) the soft serve cone/wafer almost certainly contains wheat — ask for a cup; (2) no dedicated GF certification exists; (3) the shared kitchen/counter of a busy market stall carries cross-contamination risk; (4) soy sauce is not confirmed absent from all preparations. Celiac diners should inquire specifically and consider eating tofu dengaku without the cone.

bio.background: 河口とうふ (Kawaguchi Tofu) was founded in 1934 in the Owari region of Nagoya, which has long been prized for its high-quality groundwater and proximity to premium soybean-growing areas. The family has made tofu using the same core principles for over 90 years: carefully selected domestic (non-GMO) soybeans, natural sea salt nigari as the coagulant, and local well water — never additives or shortcuts. The Kinshachi Yokocho stall opened when the castle-gate food arcade launched as part of the Nagoya Castle precinct redevelopment.
bio.specialty: Tofu soft serve (豆腐ソフト) — a dense, subtly sweet soft-serve made from concentrated soy milk; the gold-leaf version (¥990) is the flagship tourist order. Tofu dengaku skewers (田楽, ¥500) — blocks of firm housemade tofu grilled and brushed with Hatcho-style sweet miso — are the deeper local eat.
bio.philosophy: No GMOs, no additives, minimal intervention: the shop's philosophy is that great tofu needs only great soybeans, pure water, and natural nigari. The Owari region's terroir is central to their identity.

anecdotes.text: The gold-leaf tofu soft serve melts quickly in warm weather — the gold leaf itself adds no flavor but covers roughly half the cone in a photogenic shimmer. Eat it fast.
Reviewers note the dengaku's miso has a 'well-balanced sweetness' that 'strongly embodies Nagoya's culinary character' — the firm, well-pressed tofu holds its shape on the skewer.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free kitchen or certification. The stall operates within a busy shared market arcade; cross-contamination from neighboring stalls (soba, tempura) cannot be ruled out.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The dengaku miso is Hatcho-style (豆味噌 / 八丁味噌), brewed from soybeans and salt only — no wheat. This is a genuine GF advantage over standard miso or soy sauce-seasoned dishes. However, no public allergen sheet confirms total absence of wheat-based soy sauce from all preparations.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No animal-derived dashi or bonito detected in the core tofu or dengaku preparations. Soft serve dairy status is unconfirmed — verify at counter.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No public information about staff allergy training or allergy protocols. Phone: 052-231-8810 for advance inquiry.

positives.text: Hatcho miso (豆味噌) used in dengaku contains only soybeans and salt — no wheat, unlike most soy sauces and many other miso types. Tofu itself is made from soybeans, nigari, and water with zero additives.

caveats: Target listing uses 川口 (Kawaguchi with 川) but the correct shop name is 河口 (Kawaguchi with 河) — same pronunciation, confirmed as the same business at this Tabelog URL. Hours (10:30–17:00) sourced from the Kinshachi Yokocho official listing; Tabelog and other sources show slightly varying times (10:00–17:30). Venue-wide closed days were not published; seasonal and holiday closures likely — call ahead. Soft serve dairy content unconfirmed; celiac and vegan diners should inquire directly before ordering.

cuisine: カフェ・和菓子・プレミアムティー・フレンチ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 returned lat 35.185513 / lng 136.896072. Approximately 330m from Nagoya Castle; well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町（西区）
seats_text: 64席（個室1室あり）
closed_days: 定休日なし（ホテル営業に準ずる）
cultural_comfort_note: Luxury international hotel lounge with bilingual (Japanese/English) online booking via TableCheck; staff at a five-star property are accustomed to foreign guests.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. The lounge serves French-influenced lunch and dinner courses with meat, fish, and dairy throughout. Wagashi (Japanese sweets) and premium tea form the cafe side of the offer — some traditional wagashi are naturally plant-based (adzuki, mochi), but this varies by item and is not guaranteed. Guests with vegan needs must contact the restaurant in advance; the hotel states allergen accommodations may be declined if safety cannot be ensured. Hidden animal-derived ingredients (butter, egg, dashi) are present throughout the kitchen.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or certified GF items. The lunch and dinner courses include bread service and flour-based French preparations (cakes, sauces). Shrimp fry and crab cake are listed menu items — all likely wheat-battered. The hotel requests allergy notification at booking time and states requests on arrival may not be accommodated. There is no mention of a dedicated fryer, GF soy sauce, or GF alternatives. Soy sauce (wheat-containing) is likely present in kitchen. Celiac diners should call in advance and be prepared for the possibility of refusal.
caveats: Opened October 2025; relatively new establishment with limited independent reviews. Dietary details sourced from hotel booking platforms and official site language — always confirm directly by phone before visiting for allergy needs. Service charge of 15% applies. Smart casual dress code required.

bio.background: Chef Nakanishi is from Aichi Prefecture and spent roughly nine years at La Rochelle Tokyo, the flagship restaurant of Iron Chef Hiroyuki Sakai. He then went to France to stage at multiple Michelin-starred establishments including Mirazur (Menton) and Flocons de Sel (Megève). Returning to Japan in 2015, he co-opened SUGALABO in Tokyo, serving as executive chef for seven years — the restaurant ranked first worldwide in La Liste 2020, the French government's global dining guide.
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区（2025年10月1日開業）
bio.specialty: フレンチ技法を軸にしたプリフィックス・ランチ／ディナーコース、および尾張名古屋の茶道文化にちなんだ和菓子とプレミアムティーのカフェタイム
bio.philosophy: The lounge frames itself as a modern reinterpretation of the Owari Nagoya tea ceremony tradition: elevated hospitality (もてなし) expressed through locally sourced organic matcha from Ishikawa Seicha in Toyota, paired with carefully chosen Nagoya confectionery. On the dining side, the menu leans on Aichi prefecture produce — Nagoya Cochin chicken, regional vegetables — channelled through Nakanishi's classical French training.

anecdotes.text: The CUZEN MATCHA machine at the lounge mills whole organic tea leaves to order, a process the hotel describes as placing the ancient tea ceremony in a contemporary, globally sustainable frame — whole-leaf matcha being recognised internationally as an exceptional zero-waste drink.

A Tabelog reviewer who visited in October 2025 described the lounge's floor-to-ceiling windows framing Nagoya Castle as creating 'a sense of departure from daily life' — an atmosphere of quiet luxury befitting a special-occasion tea stop.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on dedicated fryer or separated GF prep areas. The kitchen handles fried items (shrimp fry, crab cake) as standard menu courses, making cross-contamination likely in a shared kitchen environment.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: French-Japanese kitchen with standard soy sauce likely in use; no mention of tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternatives on any published menu or policy page.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Lunch and dinner courses include butter, egg, and dairy throughout; no plant-only prep station is indicated. Shared kitchen means vegan cross-contact is a real risk.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The hotel states allergen accommodations require advance notice at booking time. Requests made upon arrival may not be fulfilled. Even with prior inquiry, the restaurant may decline to accommodate based on customer safety priorities.
TableCheck booking platform notes allergy requests should be made during reservation; phone reservations (052-908-8930, 10:00–18:30) recommended for allergy concerns.

positives.text: Five-star hotel environment with professional kitchen staff trained in allergen awareness; the hotel explicitly references a structured allergen accommodation policy rather than ignoring dietary needs.

name_ja: トラットリア ドン・アントニオ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区白壁2-1-19 中川ビル1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市東区白壁二丁目１番１９号; result [136.910553, 35.183098] is within target box and 1.025km from Nagoya Castle — well inside the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 白壁 (Shirakabe), Higashi Ward — 2-min walk from Meitetsu Seto Line Higashioote Station
closed_days: 火曜日（年末年始も休業）
seats_text: 40席（テーブルのみ）、半個室あり、貸切20〜50名対応
cultural_comfort_note: Owner Antonio speaks fluent Japanese and is known for circulating among tables and chatting warmly with guests — multiple reviewers call him approachable and fun to talk to.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Standard Italian trattoria — pasta is egg-based, sauces typically use butter, cream, or meat stock. A spinach pizza is noted among vegetarian-leaning options, but hidden animal products (eggs, dairy, anchovies in sauces) are the norm here. Ask staff directly; the owner's fluent Japanese makes communication easy.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or dedicated GF options listed anywhere. All pasta is handmade wheat-based; pizza uses wheat dough. No dedicated GF fryer information available. Cross-contamination risk is high in a pasta-focused kitchen. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

bio.chef_name: Antonio Settembrini (セッテンブリーニ・アントニオ)
bio.background: Antonio was born in southern Italy in 1960 and relocated to Milan as a child in 1963, where he developed an early passion for the kitchen through exposure to restaurant life. He graduated from a culinary school in Milan in 1975 and subsequently trained across multiple regions of Italy to deepen his craft. He arrived in Japan in 1985 and in 1989 became the exclusive chef at the Brazilian Embassy in Tokyo — a post that sharpened his ability to cook at a formal, diplomatic level. In October 2009, he opened Trattoria DON-ANTONIO in Nagoya's Shirakabe neighbourhood, bringing decades of Italian regional cooking to the historic district near Nagoya Castle.
bio.specialty: Handmade pasta (including peperoncino and pappardelle), pre-order Florentine T-bone steak (bistecca), seasonal Italian wine including Novello
bio.philosophy: Authentic Italian flavors centered on seasonal ingredients, served in a casual and welcoming trattoria atmosphere adapted thoughtfully for Japanese diners.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers frequently note that Antonio circulates among tables speaking perfect, natural Japanese — one guest wrote 'オーナーのアントニオさんが気さくで日本語ペラペラで楽しかった' (Owner Antonio is personable, speaks perfect Japanese, it was a great time).
Before opening in Nagoya, Antonio served as the exclusive chef for the Brazilian Embassy — an unusual chapter that bridges Italian culinary tradition with high-level diplomatic hospitality.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on dedicated GF fryer or separate cooking surfaces. This is a pasta-and-pizza kitchen where wheat flour is in constant use — cross-contamination risk should be assumed high.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce noted in menu descriptions; this is Italian cuisine so soy sauce is not expected. Wheat risk comes from pasta dough, pizza crust, and possible flour-dusted prep surfaces.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Standard Italian trattoria kitchen — butter, dairy, eggs, and meat stocks are routine. No vegan-safe prep protocols mentioned anywhere.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The Italian owner speaks fluent Japanese and is known for personal, attentive service; direct communication about dietary needs should be feasible. No formal allergy protocol is documented.

positives.text: Owner Antonio's fluent Japanese and warm, table-side manner make it easier than at many restaurants to explain dietary needs directly to the person who runs the kitchen.

caveats: Dinner courses (¥5,000–5,999) require reservation; lunch (¥1,000–1,999) is walk-in friendly. No gluten-free or vegan menu exists — this is a conventional Italian trattoria. Chef's exact birthplace varies slightly across sources (Calabria in search summary, Acireale/Sicily and Calabria used interchangeably in fetched pages); the consistent facts are southern Italy, 1960, Milan 1963, culinary school 1975, Japan 1985, Brazilian Embassy 1989, Nagoya opening October 2009.

neighborhood: 浄心（西区）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区城北2-29
geocode_note: GSI returned chō-level coordinates for 西区城北町; no street-number precision available. Computed distance from Nagoya Castle (~1.43km) is within the 1.61km target radius.
seats_text: Five counter seats only; no tables, no private rooms.
closed_days: 不定休 (irregular closures; no fixed day off)
cultural_comfort_note: Five-seat counter shop with no English signage or menus; ordering is simple (one item in three sizes) but the environment is entirely Japanese-language.
vegan_detail: The menu is shoyu ramen only, served with pork chashu. Broth is assumed to be pork or chicken-based. No plant-based or vegan options are listed or documented anywhere.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese shoyu (soy sauce) contains wheat, and ramen noodles are wheat-based. No gluten-free alternatives, no allergy accommodation, and no staff allergy-handling procedures are documented anywhere. Unsafe for celiac or wheat-sensitive diners.

bio.specialty: Shoyu ramen — a single-item menu available in regular (¥500), half (¥600), and large (¥700) sizes, all including chashu pork.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers describe the shop as having an old-fashioned Chinese noodle shop feel — yatai-style and simple, discovered by chance when a customer noticed a ramen flag while parking nearby.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (shoyu) contains wheat; this is the defining flavor of the broth. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is offered.

caveats: Hours are the same daily per Tabelog but closures are irregular (不定休) with no fixed day off — call ahead (090-5451-6763) before visiting. Smoking is permitted at all seats. Geocoordinates are chō-level precision only.

cuisine: カフェ、カレー（世界料理、昭和レトロ、豆カレー）
neighborhood: 那古野（西区）/ 国際センター駅・丸の内駅周辺
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野2丁目14-1 なごのキャンパス 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned 那古野二丁目 level; coordinates fall within target box and 1.49 km from Nagoya Castle — inside the 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 45席（テーブル席中心）。20〜40名のプライベートイベント貸切も対応。
cultural_comfort_note: No English menu confirmed, but the international concept and tourist-written blog coverage suggest a welcoming, relaxed atmosphere; basic Japanese helps.
vegan_detail: The bean keema curry (34-ingredient legume curry) is the menu item most likely to be vegan — it is described consistently as vegetable- and legume-based with blended spices. Breakfast staples (toast, anko paste made with domestic azuki and raw sugar) may be vegan depending on butter use. The rotating "world bowl" and meat-lunch options are not vegan. No menu officially carries a vegan label; hidden dashi or bonito is unconfirmed but possible in some dishes. Ask about individual items.
gf_detail: No gluten-free designation exists. The bean keema curry avoids obvious wheat ingredients, but soy sauce (likely wheat-containing regular shoyu) is probable in Japanese-style dishes. Toast, Napolitan pasta, and other items contain gluten. No dedicated fryer information available. Celiac diners should inquire directly and approach with caution.

bio.background: Hirokazu Ochiai is CEO of Holidays Co., Ltd., the company behind OHAGI3, a popular ohagi (red bean sweet) specialty shop in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya. During his university years he reportedly made approximately three million takoyaki at commercial facilities around the world — an experience that shaped his belief that food could cross cultural borders.
bio.origin: なごのキャンパス, 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野, opened October 28, 2019
bio.specialty: 34-ingredient bean keema curry; Nagoya-style ogura (azuki) toast; rotating world-cuisine bowls
bio.philosophy: Ochiai compares his approach to the Momotaro fairy tale in reverse: rather than fighting the demons, he wants to offer them kibi dango and live together happily — expressing the idea that sharing food dissolves barriers. The cafe's sub-theme, 'world relay kitchen,' draws from poet Shuntaro Tanikawa's work Morning Relay, evoking the image of morning light passing around the globe from kitchen to kitchen.

anecdotes.text: The entrance to yoake displays a large cooking pot salvaged from the old Nagono Elementary School cafeteria — a quiet nod to the thousands of school lunches once prepared in the same room.
Organic matcha and hojicha are served in old-fashioned milk bottles, a deliberate reference to the building's school-lunch heritage.
The homemade azuki paste targets a brix of 47–49 degrees — a restrained sweetness designed so children (and adults) could eat it every day rather than as an occasional treat.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about cross-contamination controls is publicly available. The kitchen serves toast, pasta, and other wheat-containing items alongside the bean curry.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned anywhere; standard wheat-containing shoyu is assumed in Japanese-style dishes and rotating world bowls.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No information on shared equipment between vegan and non-vegan dishes. Meat lunch, chicken ramen, and other animal-protein items are prepared in the same kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu or documented allergy protocol has been found in any source reviewed. Phone inquiries at 052-526-2288 are recommended before visiting.

positives.text: The 34-ingredient bean keema curry is built around legumes and vegetables, with no meat listed among its components — the most likely candidate for a plant-based, lower-allergen option on the menu.
The operator's stated philosophy of food that is 'kind to both the body and the earth' suggests an openness to dietary needs, though this has not translated into documented menu labeling.

caveats: Hours vary and the restaurant keeps irregular closures — confirm by phone (052-526-2288) before visiting. Multiple sources give slightly different closing times (17:00 vs 21:00 on weekdays); the Tabelog and official-site figures of 17:00 weekdays / 18:00 weekends are used here as most reliable. No vegan or GF labeling exists on any confirmed menu; the bean keema curry assessment is based on ingredient descriptions, not allergen documentation. Celiac diners should not assume safety without direct confirmation from staff.

cuisine: 広東料理・中華旬菜 (Cantonese-style Chinese, seasonal ingredients)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋1-10-23
geocode_note: GSI address-search matched 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋1-10-23 → [136.897034, 35.193928]. Distance to target center (35.1856, 136.8997): 957 m, well within 1.61 km.
neighborhood: 西区 上名古屋 / 浄心駅 徒歩約8分
seats_text: 6 two-person tables
cultural_comfort_note: Small owner-operated counter with 12 seats and no English signage; Japanese only, but the calm, unhurried atmosphere makes communication manageable with basic phrases.
vegan_detail: The menu is built around Cantonese technique: seafood (shrimp, squid, mussels), oyster sauce, and egg appear across documented dishes. Some vegetable-forward items may exist but no plant-based options are advertised. Ask before ordering — assume most dishes contain animal products.
gf_detail: Standard Chinese cooking relies heavily on wheat-based soy sauce and wheat-thickened ankake (starch sauces). The signature ankake noodles are made from fresh wheat noodles. No gluten-free claims or dedicated protocols were found anywhere. Celiac diners should inquire directly and treat this as a high-risk environment unless staff can confirm specific dishes are prepared without wheat soy sauce and wheat starch thickening.

bio.background: Opened January 7, 2026 in a quiet residential block of Nishi Ward, a short walk from Joshin Station. The owner-chef previously served as head chef at Nangoku Shuka (南国酒家), the upscale Cantonese restaurant inside the Nagoya Tokyu Hotel — one of Nagoya's long-running prestige Chinese dining rooms. After years leading that kitchen, he stepped out to run his own twelve-seat room on his own terms.
bio.specialty: Salt-based ankake noodles — fresh noodles steamed, boiled, then pan-fried, topped with white hair scallions, shrimp, squid, mussels, bok choy, and cabbage in a light salt ankake sauce. The white scallion garnish deliberately echoes the restaurant's name: clouds above.
bio.philosophy: Seasonal Chinese cooking (旬菜) at honest prices — course-style generosity at lunch-counter rates, with three appetizers, soup, main, and almond tofu dessert for ¥1,500.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers have noted that on Saturdays the restaurant fills to its twelve seats almost the moment the door opens — arriving ten minutes before opening already earns you first place in line, and by 1 PM lunch can be sold out.

One reviewer described the lunch set as 'Michelin-caliber value' — three appetizers including gizzard in oyster sauce and radish pickled in Shaoxing wine, a gentle tofu-and-enoki egg soup, crispy white-fish in black-vinegar oil sauce, the signature salt ankake noodles, and almond tofu, all for ¥1,500.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination controls. The kitchen is small (12 seats implies a very compact cooking space) and serves wheat noodles and wheat-thickened sauces as core menu items — shared cookware and high cross-contact risk should be assumed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-based soy sauce is almost certainly in use throughout the menu; ankake sauces are wheat-thickened; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternatives are mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Oyster sauce, seafood stock, and egg are core components across multiple dishes; no separation between vegan and non-vegan prep is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy or allergen menu has been documented publicly. Given the small, owner-operated format, direct inquiry by phone (070-1652-0808) before visiting is the only reliable path.

positives.text: Owner-chef format means the person who cooked at a top hotel Cantonese kitchen is in the room — direct communication about ingredients is more feasible than at chain restaurants.

caveats: Lunch payment is cash-only; dinner accepts VISA/Mastercard/JCB/AMEX/Diners and IC/QR payments. Tiny 12-seat room fills very quickly on weekends — arriving at opening recommended. No English menu or English signage confirmed. Opened January 2026 so review depth is still shallow.

name_ja: 居酒屋ふた村 外堀店
cuisine: 居酒屋・串揚げ・おでん
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区東外堀町32-2 鈴木ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocoder matched 愛知県名古屋市東区東外堀町３２番地; coordinates place this approximately 987m from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 東区東外堀町・名古屋城駅エリア
seats_text: カウンター席あり。貸切最大20名。個室なし。
cultural_comfort_note: A small local izakaya with a Japanese-language-only environment; no English signage or English-speaking staff is indicated — gesturing and pointing at the menu should work for ordering basics.
vegan_detail: No vegan-declared menu items. Oden broth is almost certainly bonito- and/or kombu-based dashi; kushiage (deep-fried skewers) are coated in panko and almost certainly fried in shared oil with meat. No plant-based-only options confirmed. Edamame or simple salad might be available but cannot be guaranteed animal-product-free without asking staff directly.
gf_detail: This is a standard izakaya built around kushiage (panko-battered deep-fried skewers) and oden. Kushiage batter contains wheat flour and panko; wheat is also present in soy sauce used throughout. Oden broth likely contains soy sauce. No gluten-free declarations, dedicated fryer, or allergen menu found. Cross-contamination risk is high in a kushiage-specialist kitchen. Celiacs should not eat here without direct confirmation from staff — assume not safe.

bio.background: Opened in August 2015 as a branch of the Futamura izakaya group, situated in a low-rise commercial building (Suzuki Building 1F) in Nagoya's Higashi Ward, roughly a seven-minute walk from Higashi-Oote Station on the Meitetsu Seto Line. The main Futamura location is in the nearby Izumi neighbourhood.
bio.specialty: Kushiage (panko-battered deep-fried skewers) and oden; the lunch set is noted for value, though portions have drawn mixed comments from reviewers.

anecdotes.text: A reviewer on 4travel described the establishment as 'a typical izakaya featuring an extensive menu of fried skewers that were quite delicious,' noting they wanted to return for the lunch service.
A TripAdvisor reviewer described the interior as bright and comfortable, and easy to enter as a solo diner.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Kushiage is the restaurant's primary dish — skewers coated in wheat flour and panko breadcrumbs fried in shared oil. Cross-contamination with gluten is near-certain in the frying environment.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce (which contains wheat) is a staple condiment and likely used in oden broth and marinades throughout the menu. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternatives mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Kushiage items include meat and seafood skewers fried in shared oil; oden broth is almost certainly animal-based (bonito/kombu dashi). No separation of plant-based cooking confirmed.

positives.text: The restaurant is entirely non-smoking, which reduces one ambient irritant for sensitive diners.

caveats: Official website (futamura-sotobori.com) was unreachable at time of research (connection refused); no menu detail, allergen chart, or chef profile could be retrieved from it. Hours sourced from Tabelog and corroborated by multiple aggregators — confirm directly before visiting as hours may have changed since listing. Seat count (20) reflects the stated private-hire maximum, not confirmed total capacity.

cuisine: そば・うどん・きしめん・中華そば
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間2-8-14
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI address search for 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間2-8-14. Result: [136.887802, 35.185982]. Approximately 1.08km from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) — within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町駅周辺（名古屋城から約1.1km）
hours_raw: 月〜木・土・日・祝 11:00-14:00、17:00-20:00 / 金曜定休（タベログには月〜木・土 11:00-13:00のみと記載あり。情報源により異なるため要確認）
seats_text: テーブル席・小上がり席あり。具体的な席数は非公開。
cultural_comfort_note: 昭和の雰囲気が残る地元密着の大衆食堂。英語メニュー・英語対応はなく、日本語でのコミュニケーションが基本です。
vegan_detail: そば・うどん・きしめんのだしは動物性（かつお節・煮干し等）がベース。中華そばも鶏・豚系スープが一般的。植物性出汁や純菜食メニューの設定はなく、ビーガン対応は期待できません。
gf_detail: 小麦を主体とする麺料理（うどん・きしめん・中華そば）が主力。そばも提供しているが純そばかどうか不明。つゆ・だしの醤油には小麦が含まれる。調理器具・茹で釜は共有の可能性が高く、交差汚染リスクがある。グルテンフリー対応の表明なし。セリアック病・小麦アレルギーの方には不適。

bio.background: 名古屋市西区浅間の旧美濃路沿いに位置する、昭和の雰囲気を残す大衆麺処。浅間町駅から徒歩約5分、名古屋城にも近い住宅街に店を構える。
bio.specialty: 中華そば（鮮やかな黄色い麺と優しいだしのスープが特徴）、きしめん、味噌煮込みうどん、丼物（かつ丼・天丼など）

anecdotes.text: 壁一面に貼られた豊富なメニューに驚く客も多く、麺類から丼物まで幅広く揃える。地元客に長年親しまれてきた昔ながらの食堂スタイル。
中華そばを目当てに遠方から訪れるファンもおり、ブログで「知人の勧めで訪問」という口コミが見られる。

gf_cross_contamination.text: うどん・きしめん・中華そば等の小麦麺と同一の調理環境（茹で釜など）でそばを提供している可能性が高く、グルテン交差汚染のリスクがあります。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: つゆ・だしに使用される醤油は一般的に小麦を含みます。アレルギー対応醤油の使用は確認されていません。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全メニューのだしが動物性（かつお・煮干し等）ベースと考えられ、植物性食材のみの料理でも同一設備での調理による接触が生じます。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応・グルテンフリー対応についての情報は一切公表されていません。来店前に電話（052-531-5130）で直接確認することを強く推奨します。

caveats: 営業時間はタベログ（月〜木・土 11:00-13:00のみ）とエキテン・その他（11:00-14:00 + 17:00-20:00のディナー帯あり）で大きな食い違いがあります。情報が古い可能性が高く、来店前に必ず電話で確認してください。公式Webサイトは見つかりませんでした。

name_ja: カフェ ド カオス キヅキ
cuisine: フランス惣菜・コーヒースタンド
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-2-16 ＆tree
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 那古野一丁目２番１６号 returned [136.892761, 35.176167]. Distance to Nagoya Castle target (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1,224m — within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 那古野・円頓寺エリア（西区）
seats_text: 6席（カウンター席のみ）
cultural_comfort_note: A 6-seat counter-only cafe with no phone number listed and cash-only payment; English signage or service is not confirmed, so basic Japanese phrases will help.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or labeling. The French delicatessen menu centers on quiche, hot sandwiches, and pastries — all wheat-based — alongside pâté and mousse. No dedicated GF fryer or prep area is mentioned. Celiac diners should inquire directly before ordering; the safest bet is plain coffee with no food items.
vegan_detail: The menu is a French charcuterie and pastry format: pâté de tête (pork head), chicken liver mousse, homemade sausage, cream cheese brûlée, and dairy-based coffee drinks. No plant-based or vegan options are listed or implied anywhere in reviews. Vegan diners will find nothing suitable here beyond black coffee.

bio.background: Cafe de Chaos KIZKI opened on January 17, 2026 inside the &tree building at the edge of Nagoya's old Nagono merchant district. It is a sister shop to Bistro & Cafe KIZKI, a well-regarded French bistro that opened in May 2023 in Higashi Ward and earned a place on the French EAST 100 Restaurants list for 2025. The cafe is run by the wife of the original bistro's owner-chef, making it a true family enterprise.
bio.specialty: French delicatessen coffee stand — specialty coffee drinks paired with handmade French inzarui (prepared foods): quiche, pâté, mousse, hot sandwiches, and cream cheese brûlée
bio.philosophy: Bringing the flavors of a French bistro kitchen into a compact, everyday coffee-stand format — an approachable daytime counterpart to the evening dining of the original Bistro KIZKI.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers noted the soft serve was 'surprisingly delicious' for a place primarily known for its French savory bites and specialty coffee.

caveats: Opened January 2026 — still very new with only 16 Tabelog reviews at time of research. No official standalone website found; Instagram presence is shared with the parent bistro (@kizki.bistro_cafe). No dietary accommodation data exists for this location. Hours and menu subject to change given its newness.

cuisine: 居酒屋・炉端炭火焼き・海鮮・日本料理
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内1-4-29
geocode_note: GSI address search for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内一丁目４番２９号 returned coordinates 136.895432, 35.176788. Distance to Nagoya Castle target point is approximately 1,054 meters — within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（丸の内駅8番出口より徒歩1分）
seats_text: カウンター8席、テーブル24席、掘りごたつ8席、合計40席
cultural_comfort_note: 丸の内のサラリーマン向け居酒屋で英語メニューや英語対応スタッフの情報はなく、基本的に日本語のみの環境と思われる。
vegan_detail: 炉端炭火焼き中心のメニューは魚介・鶏・牛肉が主体。おばんざい5種盛りや長芋バターなど植物性に近い品もあるが、だし（鰹・昆布）や醤油ベースの調理が標準的で、ヴィーガン対応の明示はない。注文前に確認が必要。
gf_detail: 炭火焼き主体のメニューは素材焼きが多く、グルテンフリーの可能性がある品も含まれるが、醤油タレ（小麦含有）・だし・タレ類の使用が広く想定される。グルテンフリー対応の明示や専用フライヤーの情報はなく、セリアック対応は確認が必須。

bio.background: 2026年4月24日オープンの新店。名古屋・丸の内のオフィス街に位置し、備長炭を使った炉端炭火焼きと日本酒のペアリングをコンセプトに掲げる。
bio.specialty: 炉端炭火焼き（銀鮭イクラ乗せ・脂のった鯖・黒毛和牛など）と鮭いくら丼
bio.philosophy: 「こだわりの炉端炭火焼き×日本酒で乾杯」をテーマに、素材の味を活かした炭火焼きとおばんざいで地元サラリーマンの日常を支える食堂居酒屋。

anecdotes.text: 2026年4月開店直後からTabelogに複数のレビューが寄せられ、炭火焼き魚を主体としたメニューと日本酒の品揃えが評価されている。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油タレや煮物に小麦入り醤油が使用されている可能性が高い。グルテンフリー醤油の使用に関する情報は公開されていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 鰹・昆布ベースのだしが調理全般に使われていると推定され、植物性食材の料理にも動物性だしが入る可能性がある。

positives.text: 炭火焼き素材（魚・肉・野菜）は焼くだけの調理も多く、タレや調味料を別出しで依頼できる可能性がある。来店前の電話確認を推奨。

caveats: 2026年4月24日オープンの新店のため、レビュー・情報量が限られる。グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応の公式情報はなく、来店前の電話確認を強く推奨。公式ウェブサイトは確認できなかった。

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内1-10-17
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned [136.895432, 35.174011] for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内一丁目１０番１７号; approximately 1.35km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（丸の内駅 2分）
seats_text: 40席、テーブル席のみ
cultural_comfort_note: A no-frills weekday lunch spot catering to local office workers; English is unlikely to be spoken and there is no English menu.
vegan_detail: A conventional Japanese soba-ya. The tsuyu broth is almost certainly made with katsuobushi (bonito flakes) dashi; tempura batter contains wheat and eggs. No vegan options have been identified in any source.
gf_detail: Traditional Japanese soba shops typically blend buckwheat with wheat flour (ni-hachi style, ~20% wheat), so the noodles themselves are not gluten-free. The tsuyu dipping sauce uses regular soy sauce (contains wheat). Tempura is battered in wheat flour. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodation has been mentioned in any source. This restaurant is not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.specialty: ミニ天丼＋ミニそばセット（mini tendon and mini soba set）

anecdotes.text: The restaurant displays a wall chart showing the price progression of soba from the Meiji period onward, prompting diners to compare the cost of noodles and rice across different historical eras.
A blogger described the shop as having a 'THE SOBAYA' aesthetic — a spot with straightforward Showa-era character that fits naturally into Nagoya's castle-adjacent business district.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on cross-contamination controls. As a conventional soba shop with tempura on the menu, shared cooking surfaces and wheat-containing ingredients are highly likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard soy sauce (containing wheat) is used in the tsuyu; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative has been mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Katsuobushi-based dashi is standard in this style of restaurant; no plant-based dashi option has been identified.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling procedures or allergy-aware staff information found in any reviewed source.

caveats: No official website found. Hours and details sourced from Tabelog, Hot Pepper, and Hitosara listings — confirm by phone (052-231-4478) before visiting. Dietary assessments are based on standard practices for this cuisine type, not confirmed directly with the restaurant. Weekday lunch only; closed weekends and holidays.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区天神山町8-2
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区天神山町８番２号; approx 1.31 km from Nagoya Castle target center, within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 浄心 / 天神山町 (Nishi Ward)
seats_text: Table seating; exact count not published. Parking for 3 cars at rear.
cultural_comfort_note: A neighborhood regular's kissaten with no English signage or menu; basic Japanese (or pointing at the menu board) is sufficient to order.
vegan_detail: Standard kissaten menu: morning sets include buttered toast and boiled egg; lunch sets feature karaage (fried chicken), miso soup, and similar Japanese home-cooking staples. No plant-based alternatives mentioned anywhere. Hidden dashi (bonito/katsuo) in miso soup is likely.
gf_detail: Toast-centric morning service and deep-fried lunch dishes (karaage uses wheat-based batter) mean wheat is central to the menu. Shared kitchen with no GF awareness indicated. Soy sauce in cooking almost certainly contains wheat. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.specialty: Morning set — coffee with thick-cut half-toast, potato salad, and boiled egg, all under ¥1,000.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers describe a steady stream of neighborhood regulars filling the shop each morning, with the atmosphere of a well-worn living room — the kind of place locals rely on before their commute.
Lunch plates reported by visitors include karaage, macaroni potato salad, rice, miso soup, pickles, and fruit — substantial home-cooking at kissaten prices.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Karaage (wheat-battered fried chicken) is a standard lunch item, indicating a shared fryer environment with no GF separation.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Kissaten cooking in Japan routinely uses wheat-containing soy sauce in marinades and dressings; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives are indicated.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Eggs, dairy (butter on toast), and animal broths are integral to every service period; no vegan separation possible.

caveats: No official website exists. Weekend hours are morning-only (07:00-11:00); visitors arriving after 11:00 on Saturday or Sunday will find it closed. Payment is QR code (PayPay) or cash only — credit cards not accepted. No GF or vegan information exists anywhere online; this record's dietary ratings are inferred from the standard kissaten menu type.

cuisine: Creative Japanese / charcoal-grilled omakase (創作和食・炭焼き割烹)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-26-14
geocode_note: GSI geocode confirmed: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野一丁目２６番１４号 → [136.891541, 35.17424]. Distance to Nagoya Castle approx 1.46 km, within 1.61 km threshold.
neighborhood: 那古野（西区）/ 国際センター駅 徒歩5分 / 四間道歴史地区
seats_text: 14 seats total: 10 counter seats and 4 private-room seats. Counter can be reserved exclusively for a group.
cultural_comfort_note: Counter omakase in a historic machiya setting; no English menu confirmed, and communication is at the chef's counter — Japanese ability or a patient companion will help considerably.
vegan_detail: Fixed omakase courses built around seafood and charcoal-grilled proteins — octopus, eel, sweetfish, bonito, sardine, and other seasonal fish are staples. Vegan guests cannot be accommodated in the standard course format; no plant-based course alternative is known to exist.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or dedicated GF claims found anywhere. Standard Japanese wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu) is used throughout. Dashi likely contains bonito, which is GF-safe, but soy sauce cross-contact in marinades, sauces, and charcoal-basting is near-certain. The fixed omakase format offers limited substitution. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant before booking to ask about soy-sauce-free preparations; success is not guaranteed.

bio.background: Chef Iizumi Yudai graduated from Hattori Nutrition College (服部栄養専門学校), then sharpened his craft at respected establishments in Tokyo before training at the Michelin one-star charcoal-kaiseki restaurant 炭火割烹 白坂 in Akasaka, Tokyo. He subsequently returned to Nagoya to open 四間道 松 in the historic Shikemichi district.
bio.specialty: Charcoal-grilled omakase drawing on Aichi-region seasonal ingredients — Toyohama octopus, Chita-grown vegetables, Biwa Lake eel, Kisarazu bonito — woven with touches from Mie and Gifu prefectures and occasionally further afield.
bio.philosophy: Creativity without pretension: the kitchen employs inventive pairings (young sweetfish with 12-month aged Comté, corn-and-clam chawanmushi) but executes them with restraint, letting ingredient quality speak rather than showmanship.

anecdotes.text: A food writer who visited in summer attempted to pre-order rare wild char and sweetfish through Instagram before the visit, only to find they were unavailable due to poor weather conditions — a reminder of how tightly the kitchen's menu tracks actual seasonal availability rather than a fixed list.
The restaurant's pairing of young sweetfish (ayu) with 12-month aged Comté cheese drew particular praise: an unconventional East-meets-West combination that reviewers said avoided gaudiness entirely.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols has been published. The charcoal-grill station likely handles items that have been basted or marinated with wheat-containing soy sauce, posing a cross-contact risk for celiac diners.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-based Japanese soy sauce (shoyu) is used in sauces and marinades across the omakase menu. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative has been mentioned in any source reviewed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi-based preparations and fish/seafood are integral to every course; vegan cross-contact throughout the meal is unavoidable given the format.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published information on staff allergy training or allergy communication protocols. As a reservation-only counter restaurant, dietary concerns should be raised at time of booking via the online reservation platform or by phone (052-562-7475).

positives.text: Reservation-only format with advance booking means there is an opportunity to communicate dietary needs before arrival, unlike walk-in restaurants.

caveats: Chef name (飯泉雄大) and training history sourced from a secondary search summary referencing Ikyu.com; the Tabelog and sushi-blog pages did not name the chef directly. Confirm at booking. Hours include a third-Sunday closure each month that is easy to miss. No official website exists — Instagram (@shikemichi.matsu) is the closest thing to an official channel.

name_ja: シャンブル ダンファン
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区花の木3-6-14 天神山ビル 1F
geocode_note: GSI address search on 愛知県名古屋市西区花の木三丁目６番１４号 returned [136.887421, 35.191479]. Approx 1.30km from Nagoya Castle centroid — within 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 西区花の木 (浄心駅徒歩7分)
seats_text: 10-seat counter only; maximum party of 10; private hire up to 20 available
hours_raw: 火～土 18:00～22:00（一斉始まり）、日・月 定休、不定休あり
cultural_comfort_note: Reservation by phone in Japanese, intimate 10-seat counter where the chef works in an open kitchen; English communication cannot be assumed.
vegan_detail: Tasting menus are built around animal proteins — foie gras, lobster, pigeon, venison, eel, bonito, sea urchin. No vegan course or plant-based alternative is offered or documented. Hidden animal broths throughout.
gf_detail: No gluten-free information published anywhere. The tasting menu includes pastry dishes (eel in pastry, sesame tuile) and likely wheat-based thickeners/sauces standard in French cuisine. Soy sauce used in Japanese-influenced French kitchens typically contains wheat. No dedicated fryer data available. Reservation-only format means requests could potentially be raised at booking, but cross-contamination risk in a small 10-seat kitchen is not documented.

bio.background: Chef Koshiyama trained at La Vagabonde, one of Nagoya's most celebrated and hardest-to-book French restaurants, based in Tsurumai. His wife, who serves as pastry chef, also comes from a renowned establishment. The couple opened Chambre d'enfants on August 1, 2023, in a quiet residential pocket of Nishi Ward near Joshin Station.
bio.specialty: Innovative multi-course tasting menu showcasing seasonal Japanese ingredients in a French contemporary idiom — signature pairings have included cherry and foie gras terrine with sesame tuile, sea urchin and pork trotter croquette, and summer venison loin with venison jus.
bio.philosophy: The kitchen leans away from classical French technique and toward innovation: bold, sometimes unexpected ingredient combinations that still cohere into a harmonious whole across the full course. The name itself — French for 'children's room' — signals the owners' intent to make the space feel personal and warm rather than formally intimidating.

anecdotes.text: The couple's young child sometimes wanders out from the back kitchen into the dining room, acting as an unofficial mascot. One reviewer wrote that the child 'occasionally peeked out, which was adorable and soothing' — a moment that perfectly captures the warmth the name promises.

Despite being tucked into a residential backstreet of Nishi Ward — not a typical fine-dining address — the restaurant was selected for the Tabelog French EAST '100 Best' list in 2025, less than two years after opening.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No information published on whether soy sauce used in sauces or glazes contains wheat. Standard in Japanese French kitchens to use conventional wheat-bearing soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Every documented dish contains animal products. No plant-based courses exist. Cross-contact with animal fats, stocks, and proteins throughout the kitchen is near-certain.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy or dietary restriction handling policy is published on any platform. The reservation-only format (phone: 052-990-4513) theoretically allows guests to raise dietary needs at booking, but no accommodation has been confirmed by any source.
