caveats: Chef's given name not confirmed in any source — only family name Koshiyama (腰山) appeared in aggregator copy. Hours marked irregular as the listing notes 不定休 (occasional unscheduled closures). No official website found; official_url left blank. Price data from Tabelog suggests ¥20,000–¥29,999 dinner; some review sources cite a lower ¥11,000–¥15,000 figure, suggesting possible course pricing tiers. All hours represent last-known data; verify before visiting.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区堀端町1-32
neighborhood: 浅間町 / 浄心
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区堀端町１番３２号 → [136.896194, 35.187405]. Approximately 0.38 km from Nagoya Castle target point, well within 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 11 seats total: 5 counter, 6 table
closed_days: 月曜・火曜（第1月曜は営業）
cultural_comfort_note: Tiny 11-seat counter-and-table shop run by a couple with no English signage noted; basic Japanese for ordering is helpful.
vegan_detail: All broth bases use animal-derived ingredients: Nagoya Cochin and Kyoto red chicken are core to both the soy and salt broths, with dried herring (niboshi) and bonito flakes (katsuobushi) also in the stock. No plant-based option exists on the menu.
gf_detail: The soy sauce base is described as Aichi Prefecture tamari soy sauce blended with other soy sauces — tamari is traditionally wheat-free but the blend composition is unconfirmed. Noodles are 100% domestic wheat and not gluten-free. No dedicated GF handling, no allergen menu, and no cross-contamination protocol has been documented anywhere. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant before visiting.

bio.background: Opened May 9, 2024 by what appears to be a married couple. No prior restaurant or career background has been published. The owner also maintains a personal Instagram account (@tsukasaorange).
bio.specialty: 地鶏醤油らぁ麺 (jidori shoyu ramen) — a clear chicken-based soy broth made from Nagoya Cochin and Kyoto red chicken, served with house-made wheat noodles and multiple chashu preparations varying by cooking technique
bio.philosophy: Emphasis on carefully selected ingredients (厳選素材) and handcrafted noodles; broth is kept clean and sharp rather than heavy or greasy, letting the natural sweetness of premium local poultry come through.

anecdotes.text: On opening day, May 9, 2024, customers were already queuing twenty minutes before the doors opened, and the shop sold out that first service.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The soy sauce kaeshi is described as Aichi Prefecture tamari soy sauce blended with multiple other soy sauces. Tamari is typically wheat-free but the full blend is unconfirmed; wheat content cannot be ruled out.

caveats: Hours are irregular: first Monday of the month is lunch-only (11:00-14:00); all other Mondays and Tuesdays are closed. Shop closes early when broth sells out. Cash only — no cards, IC cards, or QR payments accepted. Parking for 3 cars available east of the entrance. A name-registration queuing system operates from 10:30 before opening.

neighborhood: 那古野（西区）/ 国際センター駅 徒歩5分
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-36-36
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-36-36 → [136.892349, 35.174305]. Approx 1.42 km from Nagoya Castle center, within 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 26席（個室4〜8名対応あり）
cultural_comfort_note: 予約はオンラインまたは電話で対応。英語メニュー・英語スタッフの明記はなく、日本語対応が基本と考えられる。
vegan_detail: ベジタリアンメニューの設定あり（要問い合わせ）。ただし看板料理の野菜テリーヌは和風だし（鰹・魚介系）で野菜をポシェする調理法のため、ヴィーガンには非対応の可能性が高い。動物性だしの使用を避けたい場合は予約時に要確認。
gf_detail: グルテンフリー対応の明記なし。フレンチのコース料理のため小麦粉を使用したソース・パン・デザートが含まれる可能性が高い。醤油の使用は料理の性質上低いが、だし調理・テリーヌの結着材など小麦由来成分のリスクがゼロではない。セリアック病・重篤なグルテン不耐の方は予約時に詳細確認が必須。

bio.chef_name: 松浦仁志（まつうら ひとし）
bio.background: 1970年愛知県生まれ。祖母の手作り弁当をきっかけに料理の道へ。愛知万博フランス館レストランで三ツ星相当の現場を経験後、フランスへ渡り研鑽を積む。帰国後はCeleste（セレスト）、白雲館などを経て、2013年9月に独立開業。
bio.specialty: 農園野菜のテリーヌ（18種の無農薬野菜を和風だしでポシェし、シチリア産塩のみで味付けして成形）
bio.philosophy: 「テリーヌ」の語源であるterre（大地・土）が、建物の土蔵という素材とつながる。三重県の有機農園産野菜、三河湾の鮮魚、名古屋コーチンなど地域の食材を最大限に生かし、手を加えすぎずに素材の持ち味を引き出すフレンチを追求する。

anecdotes.text: 築約380年の土蔵を内覧した瞬間に「ここでレストランをやろう」と直感。床の張り替えから漆喰塗りまで自ら改修工事に携わり、今も毎年メンテナンスを続けている。
野菜テリーヌは毎月替わりで月替わりの旬食材を使用し、テリーヌの名称にある terre（大地）と土蔵の壁材「土」を重ねて、場所と料理をひとつのストーリーにしている。
地域の夏祭りにも積極参加し、近隣農家と連携した「トマト祭り」を企画。那古野の名物となるものを自分の手でつくりたいと語っている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテンフリー対応に関する公式記載なし。フレンチのコース形式のため、小麦を含むパン・ソース・デザートとの共用厨房環境が想定される。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: フレンチが主体のため通常の醤油使用は低いと考えられるが、野菜テリーヌのポシェに和風だし（鰹ベース）を使用しており、だし系調味料に小麦が含まれる可能性を排除できない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 野菜テリーヌのだし調理に動物性（鰹・魚介）が使用されており、ヴィーガン非対応の可能性が高い。ベジタリアン対応は問い合わせ次第で可能な旨が記載されているが、ヴィーガン対応の明記はない。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応に関する問い合わせ先としてinfo@shikemichi.jpが案内されており、予約時に個別相談を受け付けている模様。ただし公式にアレルギー対応メニューの設定があるという記載はなし。

positives.text: オーナーシェフが食材の質に強いこだわりを持ち、三重県産有機農園野菜・三河湾の鮮魚・名古屋コーチンなど産地を明示した厳選素材を使用。メニューの透明性が高い。

caveats: 火曜のランチ営業は行っておらず夜のみ。第3日曜は定休。時間帯はTabelog掲載情報に基づくが、公式サイトの表記と若干の差異あり（公式はランチ11:30-15:00、ディナー18:00-22:00と記載）。予約必須（オンライン推奨）。グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応の明記はなく、重篤なアレルギーがある場合は必ず事前にinfo@shikemichi.jpへ問い合わせること。

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-22-9
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned [136.891541, 35.17543] for 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野一丁目２２番９号. Approx 1.35 km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 西区那古野（四間道）/ 国際センター駅2番出口から徒歩5分
seats_text: 8-seat U-shaped counter; diners face the chef during preparation
closed_days: 月曜日定休、年末12月30日〜年始1月4日休業
cultural_comfort_note: A refined counter-only tempura restaurant conducted in Japanese; no English menu or staff language noted — some prior Japanese or confident pointing experience will help.
vegan_detail: Standard tempura restaurant with no published vegan menu. Traditional dashi likely contains katsuobushi (bonito flakes). Vegetable tempura items exist — including mountain vegetables and avocado — but no vegan-declared preparation. Ask well in advance if ordering a vegetable-only course.
gf_detail: Tempura batter is made with wheat flour — the core product is not gluten-free. No gluten-free batter option is documented. Soy-based dipping sauce (tentsuyu) typically contains wheat soy sauce. No dedicated fryer information found. Celiac diners should not assume safety here without direct confirmation from the kitchen.

bio.background: Owner Kuwahara Sachihide ran a long-established tempura restaurant in Sakae before opening this branch in the historic Nagono/Shikemichi district. He chose the neighbourhood deliberately, drawn by its revival energy and diverse clientele — families, tourists, and locals — distinct from the suit-and-tie crowd of Sakae.
bio.specialty: Nori-wrapped yuba and uni (sea urchin) tempura — the house signature — alongside seasonal vegetable tempura and a kakiage rice bowl finish to courses.
bio.philosophy: Head chef Tone, with 38 years of tempura experience, uses a thin, delicate batter and a proprietary blend of light corn oil to achieve a crisp, non-greasy result. The restaurant breaks convention by pairing tempura with wine — champagne, Sauvignon Blanc, Bordeaux — rather than defaulting to beer or sake, hosting seasonal wine-tasting events.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi (branch of the Shogetsu group; Sakae original location predates this Shikemichi branch)

anecdotes.text: Kuwahara described his move to Nagono: 'The old building in Sakae had aging issues, but mainly I wanted to ride the wave of new shops opening in this area.' He expressed hope the restaurant would be beloved across generations and contribute to revitalizing the Shikemichi streetscape.

A blogger visiting in 2017 noted the Kyoto-like townhouse exterior and the U-shaped counter seating 13–14 guests, observing the seaweed-yuba-uni tempura as 'exceptionally flavorful' and the dinner course with wine coming to just over ¥10,000 — 'reasonably priced for a tempura restaurant.'

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is the sole product of this restaurant; wheat-flour batter is used for all items in shared oil. No gluten-free fryer or batter alternative is documented anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tentsuyu dipping sauce is standard at tempura restaurants and typically contains wheat-based soy sauce. No tamari or GF soy sauce option is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All items are fried in the same oil as seafood (shrimp, uni, etc.). Vegetable tempura shares the fryer with fish and shellfish.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy or allergen menu is published. Reservation-first model (phone ahead) means dietary requests can be communicated in advance at 070-8960-4649 or 052-485-4410.

positives.text: Counter seating directly faces the chef, making real-time communication about ingredients more practical than in a table-service setting.
Dinner courses are prix-fixe and made-to-order at the counter, which in principle allows ingredient substitutions if discussed when booking.

caveats: Hours discrepancy noted: the official website lists dinner as 17:00-21:00 (Tue-Sat only), while an older search snippet mentioned 21:30 closing — Tabelog's current listing (17:00-21:00 L.O.) is used as authoritative. Sunday dinner was listed as unavailable on the official site and Tabelog. Independent flag set false: Shogetsu operates four Nagoya branches (Sakae, Central Park, Shikemichi, Jingu Mae), so it is a small local chain rather than a single independent shop; MOM_AND_POP retained because all branches are owner-operated, small-scale, and deeply local.

cuisine: French / Innovative (フレンチ・創作料理)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区幅下1-9-1 シャトル浅間 1F
neighborhood: 西区 幅下 / 浅間町駅から徒歩3-4分
geocode_note: Coordinates from GSI AddressSearch for 愛知県名古屋市西区幅下一丁目９番１号. Approx 642m from Nagoya Castle target center — well within 1.61km radius.
seats_text: 10 seats total: 6 counter seats + 2 two-person tables
cultural_comfort_note: Intimate 10-seat counter-focused restaurant run by a single chef; Japanese is the working language and reservations require allergy/restriction communication in advance — English speakers should use a translation tool or ask a Japanese-speaking friend to call ahead.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. French cuisine with seasonal Japanese ingredients; dishes include foie gras, duck, fish, and dairy. The restaurant states that allergy and dietary restrictions must be communicated at reservation time — same-day changes cannot be accommodated. There is no published vegan course. Hidden animal-based ingredients (butter, cream, dashi-style stocks) are likely throughout. Vegans should inquire thoroughly before booking.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or labeling found. Classic French techniques mean wheat flour, roux-based sauces, and pastry elements are standard. The kitchen explicitly asks guests to declare allergies at reservation time, suggesting some accommodation may be possible, but no dedicated GF protocol or fryer information is published. Japanese soy sauce (contains wheat) is commonly used in fusion French cooking. Celiacs should contact the restaurant before booking — do not assume safety.

bio.background: Chef Ishida Katsuhisa opened grâce à… independently on October 13, 2025, after building his career at several notable Nagoya establishments including Chez Wada Koraibridge (closed), Bistro da Ange, Auberge de l'Ill Nagoya (a prestigious Hiramatsu-group French restaurant), and Luxe GRANDIR (closed).
bio.specialty: Seasonal multi-course French cuisine integrating premium Japanese regional ingredients — dishes have included Iwate abalone with escargot butter croquette, Hokkaido duck with Mikawa mandarin sauce, Noto stone bream with takenoko, and Hungarian foie gras mousse with Aomori apple.
bio.philosophy: The restaurant name grâce à means 'thanks to' in French — the concept centers on gratitude: each dish is an expression of appreciation to customers, producers, suppliers, and the mentors who shaped the chef's career.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi, Japan. Opened October 13, 2025 in a compact 10-seat space in the Shuttle Asama building, Nishi Ward, a few minutes' walk from Asama Station.

anecdotes.text: One early reviewer noted the restaurant opened 'five months ago by a chef from a 百名店 (100 Famous Restaurants)-recognized establishment' — a reference to Ishida's tenure at Auberge de l'Ill Nagoya, part of the Tabelog 100 Famous French Restaurants list.

A blog post from early 2026 detailed a dinner featuring snow crab and celeriac mousse, abalone with sea urchin liver sauce, and lily root blancmange — all reflecting the chef's hallmark of layering French technique over seasonal Japanese produce.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free kitchen or cross-contamination protocol is published. Classic French preparations routinely use wheat flour in sauces, pastry, and coatings.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No mention of tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternatives. French-Japanese fusion cooking at this level commonly incorporates regular soy sauce (which contains wheat) in marinades and sauces.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Animal products (butter, cream, meat stocks, seafood) are central to the cuisine; vegan cross-contact is highly probable given the 10-seat kitchen with no vegan-specific protocol.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant explicitly requires allergy and dietary restriction disclosure at the time of reservation ('ご予約時にアレルギーなどNG食材を必ずお伝えください'). Same-day menu changes are not accepted.

positives.text: Pre-reservation allergy declaration policy means the chef is made aware of restrictions before cooking begins, giving the best chance of accommodation within a French tasting-menu format.

caveats: Restaurant opened October 2025 and has fewer than 10 published reviews as of early 2026. No official standalone website found — Tabelog and Hot Pepper Gourmet are the primary sources. Dietary safety details are inferred from cuisine type and the published allergy-declaration policy; no specific GF or vegan accommodation has been documented in reviews. Hours and reservation policy should be confirmed directly before visiting as this is a very new, single-chef operation.

neighborhood: 名古屋城周辺
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間1-4-14
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間一丁目４番１４号 returned [136.891571, 35.184872]. Approx. 810m southwest of Nagoya Castle — within 1.61km radius.
seats_text: 18 table seats, 18 raised platform seats, plus tatami seating on the second floor; parking for 6 cars nearby
cultural_comfort_note: A long-running neighbourhood soba shop; menus and staff communication are in Japanese only, with no indication of English service.
vegan_detail: Not vegan. Signature toppings include herring (ニシン), salmon roe (イクラ), and quail egg (鶉卵). The dipping broth is almost certainly fish-based (bonito/katsuo dashi), and standard wheat soy sauce is used throughout. No vegan alternatives are listed anywhere.
gf_detail: No gluten-free claim is made. The noodles use a blend of first- and second-grade buckwheat flour; the ratio of buckwheat to wheat binder is unconfirmed — they may not be 100% buckwheat (juwari). The tsuyu dipping sauce almost certainly contains wheat-based soy sauce. Tempura is on the menu and shares kitchen space, creating cross-contamination risk. Anyone with celiac disease should call ahead (052-531-2814) before visiting and cannot assume safety.

bio.background: A neighbourhood soba shop that has stood near Asama-cho Station in Nagoya's Nishi Ward for at least four decades — the restaurant appears in Nagoya dining guides from the mid-1980s. It is run as a small family business, reputedly by an elderly couple assisted by a single server.
bio.specialty: Tenzaru Seisei Soba — cold buckwheat noodles presented across five stacked lacquer trays, accompanied by eight distinct condiments: wasabi, shredded nori, quail egg, salmon roe, herring, matsumae pickles, mountain vegetables, and grated nameko mushroom, alongside a tempura assortment and a light, subtly sweet dipping broth.
bio.philosophy: Quality everyday soba made with carefully selected first- and second-grade buckwheat flour; strongly textured, aromatic noodles served with an unusually generous range of condiments at an accessible price.
bio.origin: Nishi Ward, Nagoya; active since at least the mid-1980s

anecdotes.text: The restaurant has been listed in Nagoya print guidebooks since the mid-1980s, making it one of the longer-standing soba spots in this part of the city.
The eight-condiment presentation — ニシン, イクラ, 鶉卵, 松前漬け, 山菜, 滑子颪, 刻み海苔, 山葵 — served across five stacked trays is the dish most frequently photographed and cited by visitors.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is a core menu item; no separate fryer is indicated. Shared frying oil is a realistic cross-contamination risk for anyone avoiding gluten.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No wheat-free soy sauce is mentioned on any listing. Standard (wheat-containing) soy sauce is almost certainly used in the tsuyu dipping broth and any seasoned dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (bonito) is the expected base for the tsuyu broth at a traditional soba restaurant of this type; no plant-based alternative is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy is described on any source. Japanese-only communication is expected; calling ahead at 052-531-2814 is strongly advised for anyone with dietary restrictions.

positives.text: Buckwheat (soba) noodles are naturally gluten-free in their pure form; the restaurant's emphasis on quality flour is noted, though the exact buckwheat-to-wheat blend ratio is undisclosed.

caveats: No official website found. Hours sourced from Tabelog and directory listings; confirm before visiting as a small family shop may have irregular closures. Noodle flour blend (buckwheat-to-wheat ratio) and dashi base are not publicly disclosed — celiac diners must call ahead. Dinner closing time varies between sources (20:00 on Tabelog, 21:00 on Ekiten); 20:00 treated as primary.

name_ja: 明日のためのしじみばか 泉店
name_en: Ashita no Tame no Shijimibaka Izumi-ten
cuisine: しじみ出汁・海鮮・おでん (freshwater clam broth, seafood, oden, izakaya)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区泉1-17-3 オレンジタウン1F
geocode_note: GSI address-search returned coordinates for 名古屋市東区泉1-17-3. Calculated distance from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1.45km — within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 泉/久屋大通 (Higashi Ward)
hours_raw: 月〜土 17:00〜23:00（L.O. 23:00）、日曜定休（一部情報では年中無休との記載あり）。ランチ営業あり（一部情報：11:30〜13:30）。年末年始休業（1/1〜1/4または1/5まで）。
closed_days: 日曜・祝日（情報源により異なる場合あり）、年末年始
seats_text: 28席（カウンター5席、テーブル5卓、個室あり）
cultural_comfort_note: A local izakaya with Japanese-language menus and no noted English support; basic Japanese or a translation app is useful here.
vegan_detail: The restaurant's defining ingredient is shijimi (freshwater clam) dashi, used throughout oden and hot pot preparations. Oysters feature prominently. No plant-based or vegan menu track exists; hidden animal-derived dashi is virtually guaranteed in every dish. Anyone avoiding seafood and shellfish will find almost nothing suitable without direct kitchen negotiation.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese izakaya with no documented gluten-free options. The oden broth is shijimi (clam) based, but wheat-containing soy sauce (醤油) is standard in Japanese oden broth and grilled skewers. Grilled items (備長炭焼き) may use marinades with wheat soy sauce. No dedicated GF menu, fryer, or allergy menu was found across any source. Celiacs should call ahead; safe options cannot be confirmed without direct kitchen inquiry.

bio.background: The main branch of Shijimibaka opened in September 2019 in Nagoya's Nishiki entertainment district (Sakae), founded by representative Tsuda with a mission the name encapsulates: shijimi (freshwater clams) are rich in ornithine, which supports liver function and recovery — so tonight's izakaya dinner becomes fuel for tomorrow rather than a drain on it. The group crowdfunded in late 2020 to expand into e-commerce (shijimi shoyu, ponzu, vending machines). The Izumi/久屋大通 branch opened in May 2023, 5 minutes from Hisaya Odori Station, and draws a strong office-worker lunch crowd alongside its evening izakaya trade.
bio.origin: 名古屋市中区錦 (main branch); 名古屋市東区泉 (this branch, opened May 2023)
bio.specialty: しじみ出汁おでん（clam-broth oden）, especially the げんこつ大根 (giant daikon slow-cooked over two days in clam broth), and the しじみエスプレッソ (concentrated clam essence as an amuse-bouche).
bio.philosophy: The brand positions shijimi as a health-supporting food: its ornithine content is thought to aid liver recovery and sleep quality, so the concept frames indulgent izakaya dining as genuinely restorative — 'tomorrow's vitality, tonight.'

anecdotes.text: The main-branch shijimi dashi earned a Japan Food Selection Gold Award, and the restaurant parlayed that recognition into a crowdfunding campaign to sell bottled shijimi shoyu and build what it called 'the world's first shijimi dashi vending machines' for entertainment districts.
The signature げんこつ大根 (fist daikon) takes two full days of preparation in clam broth before it reaches the table.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated GF preparation area or fryer documented. Standard izakaya kitchen — cross-contamination from wheat via soy sauce, marinades, and shared cooking surfaces must be assumed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Shijimi dashi broth is the restaurant's specialty, but conventional wheat-containing soy sauce (醤油) is standard in Japanese oden broth and yakitori marinades. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative was documented anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Shijimi (clam) dashi is the foundational stock used across virtually all dishes. Oysters are a featured menu item. No plant-only cooking zone exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy questionnaire, or staff training documentation was found in any source. Guests with serious food allergies should call ahead (052-228-3531) before visiting.

positives.text: The core broth is made from shijimi (freshwater clams), not from wheat-containing or fermented products — so the broth base itself may be inherently wheat-light, though the soy sauce used to season it almost certainly contains wheat unless tamari is specifically requested.

caveats: Hours are inconsistent across sources: Tabelog and Gnavi indicate Mon–Sun 17:00–23:00 with no regular closed day; HotPepper and the owst.jp listing indicate lunch service (11:30–13:30) and some sources cite Sunday/holiday closure. Call ahead or check the restaurant's Instagram (@shijimibakaizumi) before visiting. The restaurant is named identically on reservation platforms as both "泉店" and "久屋大通店" — both refer to the same physical location at 泉1-17-3.

name_ja: ひつまぶし 登河 那古野本店
cuisine: ひつまぶし・炭焼うなぎ・かしわ（奥三河地鶏）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-2-11
neighborhood: 中区那古野（四間道エリア）
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-2-11 → [136.892975, 35.176479]. 1,184m from Nagoya Castle centroid — within 1.61km radius.
seats_text: 30席。2〜4名個室、4〜6名個室、4〜8名VIPルームあり。
cultural_comfort_note: 予約・注文はほぼ日本語のみ対応；英語メニューや英語スタッフの情報は確認されていない。
vegan_detail: メニューは炭焼うなぎ・かしわ（鶏）・名古屋コーチン卵・鰹だしが基本で、植物性のみの選択肢は存在しない。
gf_detail: うなぎの蒲焼きタレは醤油（小麦含有）ベースで、ひつまぶしのお出汁・たれにも小麦が含まれる可能性が高い。グルテンフリー対応メニューや除去可否の案内は確認されていない。セリアック病・小麦アレルギーの方には不向き。

bio.background: 2020年11月、名古屋・那古野の四間道エリアに開業。築約90年の古民家を改装した建物に、樹齢約320年と伝わる日本庭園を擁する。同エリアにある日本料理店「那苑（なくおん）」と敷地を共有している。運営は祭りグループ（Matsuri Group）で、2021年7月に栄店・池下店も開業している。
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区那古野（四間道エリア）
bio.specialty: 炭火焼きひつまぶし・中黄金うな重（うなぎ×名古屋コーチン卵の厚焼き玉子の組み合わせ）
bio.philosophy: 「お客様全員が満足できるものだけを追求し、提供する」（大森シェフ）。季節ごとに最上質のうなぎを仕入れ、備長炭で焼き上げる。米は岐阜県産「にこまる」を使用。

anecdotes.text: 店名「登河（とがわ）」は、うなぎが川を遡上する力強さ（登＝登る）と、海から川へと泳ぐ本質（河）を重ねた命名。四間道の歴史的な町並みに溶け込む古民家の佇まいと、樹齢320年の日本庭園を眺めながら食事できる空間が特徴。
看板メニュー「中黄金うな重」は、炭焼きうなぎと名古屋コーチン卵の厚焼き玉子を組み合わせたもので、濃厚なうなぎと軽やかな玉子の「相乗効果」が生まれるとして人気を集めている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: うなぎの蒲焼きタレ・ひつまぶしの出汁・かけ汁はいずれも醤油（小麦）ベースと推定される。グルテンフリー対応の代替タレや除去オプションは公式・口コミいずれでも確認できない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: ひつまぶしの蒲焼きタレは醤油・みりん・砂糖ベースで、一般的なうなぎのタレには小麦由来成分が含まれる。店舗での小麦不使用醤油使用については情報なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全メニューが動物性食材（うなぎ・鶏・卵・鰹だし）を使用しており、植物性食材のみの調理ラインは存在しない。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応・スタッフによる除去対応についての公式案内は確認されていない。来店前に電話（052-526-0063）で直接確認を推奨。

positives.text: 完全禁煙。個室あり（2〜4名・4〜6名・VIP 4〜8名）。テイクアウト・デリバリー（Uber Eats）にも対応しており、持ち帰りで自宅での成分確認が可能。

caveats: グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応の公式情報なし。蒲焼きタレ・出汁の小麦含有は業界標準から推定。セリアック病・重篤な小麦アレルギーには不適。営業時間・定休日は季節により変動の可能性があるため、来店前に要確認。

name_ja: カフェ キャメッロ
cuisine: カフェ・コーヒー・サンドウィッチ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋4-10-10
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋四丁目１０番１０号; approx 1.18 km from Nagoya Castle target point, within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 浄心（西区）
closed_days: 年中無休（臨時休業あり）
seats_text: 16席（カウンター6席・テーブル・ソファ席）＋2階レンタルスペース6席
cultural_comfort_note: Small independent neighborhood cafe with no English signage noted; basic Japanese helpful for ordering, but the relaxed atmosphere and simple menu make it approachable.
vegan_detail: No vegan-designated menu items. The food menu centers on house-baked focaccia and ciabatta sandwiches with fillings such as smoked salmon, chicken fillet, and mozzarella; desserts include scones made with fermented butter, brownies, and flan cake. Dairy and meat are standard throughout. No evidence of dairy-free or egg-free alternatives. Evening bar service includes wine and whiskey. Ask staff if any drink or simple item can be prepared without animal products.
gf_detail: The cafe's identity is built around house-baked wheat bread (focaccia, ciabatta) — gluten is central to the menu. No gluten-free bread alternative or GF-designated item is mentioned on the official site, Tabelog, HotPepper, or any blog. Coffee drinks may be wheat-free in isolation, but cross-contamination risk is high in a bread-focused kitchen. Not appropriate for celiac diners.

bio.background: Caffe CAMMELLO opened on 10 March 2020 in the quiet residential neighbourhood of Kaminagoya, a 10-minute walk from Joshin Station on the Tsurumai Line. The café's Wix profile is registered under the username tanakateruyuki38, pointing to an owner named Tanaka Teruyuki, though this is not confirmed on-record. The name Cammello is Italian for camel, rendered in a logo that appears on the glass door.
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区上名古屋, Nagoya
bio.specialty: Hand-dripped coffee from carefully selected single-origin beans; house-baked focaccia and ciabatta sandwiches
bio.philosophy: The cafe describes itself as a refined adults' hideaway (大人の隠れ家) where guests are invited to pick up a favourite book and listen to quietly flowing jazz — coffee, sandwiches, and desserts crafted with care as the backdrop to unhurried time.

anecdotes.text: The seasonal fruit open sandwich — layered with whipped cream, vanilla ice cream, hazelnut spread, and Edam cheese — illustrates the kitchen's playful approach to a café staple.
Baked goods including scones, brownies, and macarons are sold at Ion Town Meisei in addition to the café, extending the kitchen's reach into the neighbourhood.

gf_cross_contamination.text: House-baked wheat bread (focaccia, ciabatta) is the core product; flour is actively used throughout the kitchen. Cross-contamination risk for gluten is high and no GF protocols are documented.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Butter, dairy, and eggs appear throughout the baked-goods and sandwich menu; no vegan-safe preparation areas noted.

caveats: Hours sourced from HotPepper (most recent listing) and cross-checked against Tabelog; some earlier sources list different Mon-Tue start times (13:00 vs 08:00) — confirm directly or via SNS before visiting. Owner identity inferred from Wix account username only, not confirmed in any publication. No dietary accommodation data found for GF or vegan.

neighborhood: 中区 丸の内／久屋大通 (Nakaku Marunouchi/Hisaya-odori, City Hall area)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区（住所非公開）
geocode_note: Address intentionally undisclosed by restaurant; geocoded to chō-level centroid of 中区丸の内 via GSI API. Confirmed within ~1.23km of Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), inside 1.61km target radius. Tabelog and tenpodesign.com both place the restaurant in the Marunouchi/Hisaya-odori corridor of Nakaku.
seats_text: Counter-only seating; typically one couple (or very small party) per evening. Private use available for up to 20 people by arrangement.
hours_raw: 完全予約制・不定期営業。予約がある日のみ営業。営業時間18:00頃。
closed_days: 不定休（予約制のため予約がない日は休業）
cultural_comfort_note: Entirely referral-only with no public address or phone number; all communication is in Japanese and requires an introducer's name and contact information — not accessible without an existing Japanese-speaking connection to the restaurant.
vegan_detail: No vegan or vegetarian information published anywhere. The omakase is built around seasonal seafood, land animals (Ena chicken, Miharu beef, eel), and chef-grown vegetables. Dashi almost certainly uses katsuobushi or similar fish-based stock. No sources mention vegan accommodation. With advance notice via your introducer, a custom course may be negotiable, but nothing is confirmed.
gf_detail: No gluten-free information published anywhere. Japanese cuisine at this level routinely uses wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu) in marinades, sauces, and simmered dishes. No dedicated GF fryer or GF menu noted. Cross-contamination risk is unquantifiable. With celiac disease or severe wheat allergy, inquire through your introducer before booking — there is no public channel to ask.

bio.background: Wada trained for 16 years across three Japanese restaurants in Nagoya before making an unexpected detour into farming. He returned to the kitchen carrying both the discipline of a seasoned itamae and the hands-on knowledge of someone who has grown his own food. He opened Shunzen Wada in June 2016 in Nagoya's Marunouchi district.
bio.specialty: Seasonal omakase built on chef-grown vegetables and hyper-local sourcing — signature dishes have included charcoal-grilled natural ayu in a ceramic bowl, bowl of grated taro with shark fin, and Nagara River sweetfish.
bio.philosophy: The restaurant exists to maximize the flavour of ingredients at their peak. Wada explains each dish's ingredients and technique to diners as they eat, turning the counter into something reviewers have called 'Wada Theater.' Every detail — cuisine, sake, space — is under his direct control.

anecdotes.text: Reservations through December 2026 were nearly full by early 2026, with the next opening date for January 2027 bookings announced for March 1, 2026 — remarkable for a restaurant that serves essentially one party per night.
Gault&Millau awarded Shunzen Wada a score of 14/20, recognising the chef's focus on vegetables he cultivates personally and his practice of narrating each dish's story to diners.

The interior was designed by Torii Yoshinori of Torii Design Office and featured in the January 2018 issue of 商店建築 (Shotenkenchiku) magazine, with handmade Japanese paper art, custom calligraphy for the sign, and Koizumi Lighting's bespoke lighting scheme.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No published information on soy sauce type used. At this style of Japanese omakase, wheat-containing soy sauce is standard unless a chef explicitly uses tamari or GF alternatives — none mentioned anywhere.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy is publicly documented. Given the referral-only, single-party-per-night format, dietary needs would need to be communicated through the introducer at the time of booking.

positives.text: One chef, one small party per night — if dietary needs are communicated in advance through an introducer, the format theoretically allows for fully customised preparation. No shared fryer risk from other tables.

caveats: Address is intentionally withheld by the restaurant as part of its philosophy; the geocode is a chō-level centroid for 中区丸の内. Hours are shown as Saturday evening only as a best approximation — in reality the restaurant operates on irregular days whenever reservations exist, almost always evenings. The entry cannot be booked without a Japanese-speaking introducer. GF and vegan safety ratings are both 'ask' because zero dietary accommodation information exists publicly; do not treat this as a safe venue for celiac diners without direct pre-booking confirmation through an introducer.

cuisine: 和食・割烹・天婦羅 (Kaiseki/Kappo, Tempura)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 4F
neighborhood: 西区 樋の口町 / 浅間町
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町３番１９号 returned [136.896072, 35.185513]. Approximately 0.33 km from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), well within the 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 8-seat kappo counter + 6-seat tempura counter; intimate counter-only dining
hours_raw: Breakfast 8:00– (weekends/holidays) / 10:00– (weekdays, reservation required); Tempura lunch 11:30–14:00 (LO 13:00); Kappo lunch 12:00–14:30; Dinner (kappo) 17:30–01:00 (food LO 00:00); Tempura dinner 17:30– / 20:30– by reservation
cultural_comfort_note: High-end kappo counter inside a luxury hotel; counter dining is intimate and paced by the chef — basic Japanese or prior kaiseki experience helps, though hotel staff may assist in English.
vegan_detail: Standard kaiseki and tempura menus are built around seafood, fish dashi (bonito/kombu), and meat — no plant-based course is offered. Hidden animal-derived elements (katsuo dashi, fish-based broths) appear throughout. Inquire well in advance; same-day modifications are not possible.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu exists. Wheat is embedded in standard preparations: soy sauce (koikuchi) in kappo seasoning, tempura batter (wheat flour) for the tempura counter, and likely wheat-containing dashi or sauces. The restaurant asks that allergy concerns be raised before the visit and explicitly states it may decline to accommodate requests for safety reasons. Not suitable for celiac without advance discussion and acceptance of uncertainty.

bio.origin: Tokyo; kappo chef 大島一真 (Oshima Kazuma) from Shima City, Mie Prefecture
bio.background: Chef Inaba Masanobu built his career at some of Japan's most prestigious hotel kitchens: he joined Grand Hyatt Tokyo's sushi restaurant 六緑 as sous chef, then led the opening of Conrad Tokyo's Japanese restaurant 風花, becoming executive chef in 2010. In 2016 he moved to Aman Nemu in Ise-Shima as executive chef, where his breakfast program was cited by US magazine Brides as one of the world's best hotel breakfasts. He received the Cultural Affairs Agency Director's Award in 2014 for promoting Japanese cuisine internationally. In 2021 he opened Ginza Inaba in Tokyo under his own name. Owari Sui Inaba opened October 1, 2025 as the Nagoya outpost inside Espacio Nagoya Castle, with Kazuma Oshima heading the kappo counter.
bio.specialty: Seasonal omakase kappo courses; a signature asagohan (morning kaiseki) featuring handmade tofu, chef-rolled tamagoyaki prepared at the counter, and charcoal-grilled fish; tempura as a parallel counter experience
bio.philosophy: 融通無碍 — adaptability and total hospitality. Inaba frames cooking as 温故知新 (honoring tradition while embracing innovation), freely incorporating luxury ingredients like truffle without sliding into fusion. Oshima's approach centers on reading the room: timing, temperature, and each guest's appetite shape the pace of the meal.
roles: Concept chef: 稲葉正信 (Inaba Masanobu)
Kappo counter chef: 大島一真 (Oshima Kazuma)

anecdotes.text: Chef Inaba's breakfast at Aman Nemu was named one of the world's best hotel breakfasts by US magazine Brides — a distinction that directly shaped the morning kaiseki concept at Owari Sui Inaba.
Chef Oshima described his concept of luxury as 'consistent execution of fundamentals,' concluding that true hospitality is created through sensory engagement rather than spectacle.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The tempura counter uses wheat-flour batter as a core element of every course; cross-contamination with shared kitchen equipment is highly likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is used throughout the kappo menu for seasoning, sauces, and marinades — no gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish dashi (bonito/kombu) is foundational to the kappo cuisine style; plant-based dashi is not indicated anywhere in the menu descriptions.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant requires allergy disclosure before the visit and states it may decline to accommodate requests in order to prioritize guest safety; same-day changes are not possible.

positives.text: Allergy information is formally collected at reservation stage, giving the kitchen advance notice to plan or decline gracefully.

caveats: Hours vary by section (kappo vs tempura) and day type (weekday vs weekend/holiday); the listed split hours are a composite. Fully reservation-only — walk-ins not accepted. A 15% service charge applies. Children under middle-school age not admitted. Irregular closure days require confirmation before visiting.

cuisine: 喫茶・和洋折衷・わらびもち
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-7-2 桜華会館 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 名古屋市中区三の丸一丁目7番; coordinates fall ~783m south-southwest of Nagoya Castle, well within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 丸の内・三の丸（中区）
seats_text: 61 seats total: 9 counter, 52 table
cultural_comfort_note: A classic government-district kissaten with no English menu or signage; comfortable for Japanese speakers, but very approachable with pointing — counter seating and a simple menu mean ordering is straightforward.
vegan_detail: Standard kissaten menu centered on coffee, toast, sandwiches (ham, tuna, egg), pilaf, yakisoba, and curry — all likely contain animal products. The signature 珈琲わらびもち includes whipped cream and kinako; the 珈琲大福 contains cream filling. No vegan-labeled or plant-based items identified. Dashi base in soup items unconfirmed but almost certainly fish-based in a traditional kissaten. Ask at the counter if you need clarification.
gf_detail: This is a traditional Showa-era kissaten with no GF awareness documented. Toast, sandwiches, pilaf, yakisoba, curry, and spaghetti all contain wheat. The 珈琲わらびもち is made from warabiko (bracken starch) which is inherently GF, but it is prepared in a kitchen handling wheat throughout and no dedicated equipment or protocols are in place. Standard soy sauce (wheat-containing) is expected in savory dishes. Cross-contamination risk is high. Not appropriate for celiac diners.

bio.background: 喫茶さくら operates as the direct-managed café arm of 倉田コーヒー (Kurata Coffee), a specialty coffee roaster from Nagoya's Nishi Ward with over 50 years of history. The café has occupied the ground floor of the 桜華会館 (Oukakkaikan) — a civic hall in Nagoya's government district near the Aichi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine — for at least 30 years, serving the salarymen and civil servants of the surrounding ministerial precinct. It is the only kissaten in this otherwise officiated stretch of Sanomaru.
bio.origin: 名古屋市中区三の丸、桜華会館 1F
bio.specialty: 珈琲わらびもち — a house invention pairing the soft, jiggly texture of traditional bracken-starch warabimochi with the roastery's own bitter coffee, topped with kinako, fruit, and whipped cream; and 珈琲大福, a coffee-cream filled mochi with a moderately sweet character.
bio.philosophy: Unchanged Showa hospitality: generous portions at sub-¥1,000 prices, a complimentary candy on the way out, and coffee quality anchored by a veteran roaster's beans.
roles: Directly managed café of Kurata Coffee roastery

anecdotes.text: The café's 1980s–90s glassware is still in daily service, and the wooden sugar dispensers have been worn smooth by decades of use — details that food writers single out as signs of an authentic, unchanged kissaten.
A blogger notes that complimentary handmade ice cream arrives with every meal order, a hospitality gesture that has become part of the café's identity.
Reviewers describe it as 'the area's only coffee shop' — a solitary outpost in an administrative district otherwise devoid of independent cafés.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No GF protocols documented. The kitchen handles wheat continuously across toast, sandwiches, yakisoba, curry, and spaghetti. Cross-contamination risk for celiac diners is high.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard (wheat-containing) soy sauce is expected in savory dishes such as yakisoba and pilaf; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives have been documented.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All cooking takes place in a shared kitchen with animal products throughout the menu; no vegan-separated prep documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling procedures, allergen menus, or English-language communication capability have been documented. Phone reservations are accepted (052-203-1900), which may allow advance allergy enquiry in Japanese.

positives.text: The 珈琲わらびもち uses warabiko (bracken starch), which is naturally gluten-free as an ingredient — though kitchen cross-contamination applies.

caveats: All-smoking venue; visitors sensitive to smoke should be forewarned. Hours listed as daily 08:00–17:00 but irregular closures apply — call ahead (052-203-1900) before visiting. No digital payment accepted (cash only). The address is 三の丸 (Sanomaru) rather than 千代田, but both fall within the same government-district precinct adjacent to Nagoya Castle and within the target radius. No official website found.

name_ja: 中国料理 柳城 NAGOYA CASTLE
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 4F
geocode_note: GSI address-search on 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 returned [136.896072, 35.185513]; approximately 0.33 km from Nagoya Castle center (35.1856, 136.8997), well within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町駅周辺（名古屋城から約0.33km）
seats_text: 60席（全席個室：6名室×2、8名室×2、12名室×1、2名テーブル）
cultural_comfort_note: ラグジュアリーホテル内の高級広東料理店で、スタッフの接客は丁寧だが英語対応の記載はなく、基本的に日本語でのやり取りが前提となる。
vegan_detail: ヴィーガン・ベジタリアン対応の明示はなし。広東料理の性質上、鶏・豚・魚介を多用するコース料理が中心。隠し出汁（鶏ガラ・干し貝柱等）が多用される可能性が高い。事前に電話で要相談（052-908-8930）。
gf_detail: グルテンフリー対応の明示なし。広東料理には醤油（小麦含有）・XO醤・点心の皮（小麦粉）など小麦を多用する料理が多い。アレルギー対応は「事前申告により可能な範囲で対応するが、安全を優先して断る場合もある」との案内あり。コース料理が主体のため個別変更は難しい可能性。要事前電話確認。
caveats: ・営業時間・定休日は公式サイト変更の可能性あり。来店前に電話で要確認（052-908-8930）。・高額コース料理（ランチ¥8,000〜、ディナー¥15,000〜）が主体。・一部プレミアムコースは5日前・4名以上での予約が必要。・「柳城」の漢字は龍城ではなく柳城が正式。

bio.background: エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスルの開業（2025年10月1日）とともに4階にオープンした高級広東料理レストラン。名古屋観光ホテルの姉妹店「柳城」ブランドの新展開として誕生。
bio.specialty: 広東式クリスピーポーク・活魚料理・特級点心師による手作り点心
bio.philosophy: 「日本の食材の鮮度」と「伝承された技」を融合させ、広東料理の真髄を表現する。愛知県産食材（名古屋コーチン・岡崎おうはん等）を積極的に取り入れ、地元の旬の味わいを広東調理法で昇華させる。
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区樋の口町（名古屋城隣接のエスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル内）
roles: ヘッドシェフ: 内藤勝博

anecdotes.text: 内藤シェフは国際広東料理コンクールでトップ10入りした初の日本人シェフ。東海地方の市場に自ら足を運び、季節の鮮魚を直接仕入れる。
超高級コース「佛跳牆（フォーティャオチャン＝坊主も垣根を飛び越える）」¥50,000〜は、希少乾物を数日かけて煮込む伝統的な広東スープ料理。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテンを含む食材（点心の皮、醤油、XO醤等）を日常的に使用するオープンキッチン環境と思われ、交差汚染リスクは否定できない。専用調理ラインに関する情報はなし。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 広東料理のコースが主体であり、蒸し料理・炒め物・タレ類に醤油（小麦含有）が広く使われると考えられる。グルテンフリー醤油への切り替え対応は未確認。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全コース料理に肉・魚介・鶏ガラ出汁が含まれると推定され、ヴィーガン対応の別調理ラインは確認されていない。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー・食べられないものは事前申告により可能な範囲で対応するが、安全確保を優先して断る場合もあるとの案内がある。来店前の電話相談（052-908-8930）を推奨。

positives.text: ラグジュアリーホテル内レストランのため、スタッフのコミュニケーション対応レベルが高く、事前相談に対して丁寧に応じてもらえる可能性が高い。

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内1-10-11 シーアイマンション丸の内1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch for 名古屋市中区丸の内1-10-11; coordinates fall within the target box and approximately 1.29km from Nagoya Castle, within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（丸の内駅近く）
seats_text: 約70席。カウンター、テーブル、個室（掘りごたつ）あり。離れ（完全個室、要予約）は別棟。
cultural_comfort_note: Traditional soba izakaya with Japanese-language menus and staff; no English signage mentioned, though the restaurant states it tries to accommodate guest preferences and dietary needs on request.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu exists. Standard soba tsuyu (dipping broth) is made with katsuo (bonito) dashi, which is not vegan. Fish-based dashi is standard throughout Japanese-style side dishes. The restaurant states it tries to accommodate dietary preferences on request, so advance inquiry is worth attempting, but hidden fish stock in most dishes makes vegan eating here very difficult without explicit pre-arrangement.
gf_detail: Soba noodles are made from Hokkaido buckwheat flour (naturally gluten-free grain), but the tsuyu dipping sauce and hot broth contain wheat-based soy sauce (koikuchi shoyu) as standard. Tenkasu (tempura scraps) are offered as a broth add-in and contain wheat. No dedicated gluten-free menu or allergen labeling was found. The kitchen handles wheat extensively. Cross-contamination risk is significant. Those with celiac disease should contact the restaurant in advance; mild gluten sensitivity may be able to negotiate a plain mori soba with separate accompaniments, but nothing is guaranteed.

bio.background: そば処 桔梗 is an established soba izakaya in Nagoya's Marunouchi business district, drawing a loyal weekday crowd of office workers and sake enthusiasts. The restaurant operates a main floor and a separate annex (Hanare) — a 160-year-old historic building offering fully private, reservation-only dining in a space that feels worlds away from the surrounding city.
bio.specialty: Hand-pulled soba made fresh each morning from Hokkaido buckwheat flour, prized for its fragrant aroma and smooth texture. Served with Shinshu-origin spicy grated daikon. The lunch mori soba and two-color soba set are signature offerings, while evenings expand into seasonal seafood sourced daily from Yanagibashi Central Market.
bio.philosophy: The kitchen pursues 'truly delicious food' through daily sourcing from Yanagibashi Market, adapting the menu to what is freshest. Staff aim to accommodate individual guest preferences — including dietary needs — within the traditional Japanese framework.

anecdotes.text: A regular diner noted that warm kake soba mutes the buckwheat's signature fragrance, and recommends ordering cold mori soba to fully appreciate the freshly-milled Hokkaido flour — a distinction the kitchen itself seems to share, given how prominently it features the noodles' aroma in its own description.
The Hanare annex, housed in a 160-year-old building a short walk from the main restaurant, is available for fully private group reservations — a rare offering in Nagoya's downtown core.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen handles wheat flour extensively — tsuyu broth uses wheat soy sauce, tenkasu (tempura scraps) are offered as a standard broth topping, and wheat-containing dishes are prepared throughout service. Cross-contamination risk for celiac diners is high and unquantified.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese koikuchi soy sauce (wheat-containing) is the expected base for tsuyu dipping broth and hot soba broth at this style of establishment. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere in available sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (katsuo/bonito) is the standard stock for soba tsuyu and hot broths in Japanese soba restaurants. Side dishes sourced daily from Yanagibashi Market are likely prepared with fish dashi or other animal products as a matter of course.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant states on its website that it tries to accommodate guest preferences and dietary needs to the extent possible ('お客様の好み・苦手なものなども出来る限り対応させていただいており'). Advance contact by phone (052-201-1559) is recommended for any dietary restriction.

positives.text: Soba noodles themselves are made from 100% Hokkaido buckwheat flour — buckwheat is naturally gluten-free. Plain soba noodles without broth would be free of gluten at the grain level, though kitchen cross-contamination cannot be excluded.

caveats: Hours sourced from Tabelog and official site show slight discrepancy (Tabelog: L.O. 21:30 evening; official site: L.O. 22:00 evening) — official site figure used. Seat count varies across sources (70 seats on HotPepper vs. 80 on Tabelog including Hanare annex); 70 used for main location only. No gluten-free or vegan accommodation details confirmed; all dietary notes are inferred from standard Japanese soba-izakaya practice. Direct contact strongly advised before visiting with any dietary restriction.

name_ja: トラットリア タピーロ
cuisine: イタリアン (Italian) — stone-oven pizza and handmade pasta
neighborhood: 名古屋市北区柳原 (Yanagihara, Kita Ward) / 名城公園駅から徒歩8分
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市北区柳原4丁目4-4 中杉ビル1F-D
geocode_note: GSI address search on 愛知県名古屋市北区柳原四丁目４番４号 returned [136.908585, 35.188316]. Distance to Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) ≈ 0.79 km — within the 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 40 seats total — counter seating, table seating, and a semi-private room that accommodates 10–15 guests for group bookings
cultural_comfort_note: A neighbourhood Italian trattoria; some basic Japanese will help, as no English menus or English-language outreach were found, though the relaxed atmosphere is approachable.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Stone-oven pizzas and handmade pastas are the focus; cheese and meat feature prominently across the menu. A Margherita pizza could be ordered without cheese, but dairy and animal products are integral to the cuisine. No vegan-specific items were found in any source. Diner should call ahead to discuss options.
gf_detail: No gluten-free options are listed or advertised anywhere. The kitchen's signature dishes — stone-oven pizza and handmade pasta — are wheat-based. No dedicated fryer, no GF pasta alternative, and no allergy-accommodation language was found across Tabelog, Hotpepper, Gnavi, or the official site. Wheat cross-contamination is essentially certain given the kitchen focus. Not suitable for celiac diners without direct confirmation from the restaurant.

bio.background: Trattoria TAPIRO opened on 15 October 2025 as the second venture from the operator of Caffe CAMMELLO (浄心). The project was born when the owner (known publicly as Kain/カイン) learned that a talented Italian chef he admired was leaving their previous position and decided to launch a partnership rather than lose access to their cooking.
bio.specialty: Stone-oven (石窯) pizza — crispy exterior with a chewy, airy interior — alongside handmade pasta and a curated all-Italian wine list
bio.philosophy: The restaurant aims to be a neighbourhood イドコロ (a place that feels like yours) — welcoming for everyday meals, dates, and celebrations alike, operating through three modes: lunch trattoria, evening restaurant, and after-22:00 wine bar.
bio.origin: Yanagihara shopping district, Kita Ward, Nagoya. Opened October 2025.

anecdotes.text: The name TAPIRO comes from the Italian word for tapir (バク), an animal associated in Japanese folk belief with good dreams and a sense of security — reflecting the owner's wish for guests to feel safe and at ease.
The owner launched a crowdfunding campaign on CAMPFIRE to finance the opening, framing the restaurant as a community-facing spot where locals could gather for authentic Italian at accessible prices.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen is built around stone-oven pizza and handmade pasta — both wheat-intensive — making flour airborne cross-contamination a near-certainty. No source mentions any GF protocol.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No mention of soy sauce use in any reviewed source, but standard Italian kitchen soy-sauce risk is low; primary wheat risk is from pizza dough and pasta throughout the kitchen.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Cheese and meat are core to almost every menu item; shared prep surfaces and ovens make vegan cross-contact likely.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy, allergen menu, or accommodation language was found on any platform reviewed. Diners with serious allergies should contact the restaurant directly at 052-325-3699 before visiting.

positives.text: Cards, e-money, and QR payments accepted — no cash-only barrier for international visitors.
Online reservations available and same-day bookings accepted until 21:00, reducing uncertainty for group visits.

caveats: Opened October 2025 so review volume is limited. No English-language visitor reports found. GF and vegan suitability cannot be confirmed — the kitchen is wheat-centric by design. Chef name not publicly available in any reviewed source. Hours from Hotpepper/Gnavi (17:00–24:00 close) differ slightly from Tabelog L.O. listings; the 24:00 close with 23:30 L.O. for the wine-bar phase appears to be correct as of latest sources.

name_ja: ネパールステーション
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-14-15 河合ビル2F
geocode_note: GSI geocoder returned coordinates for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内二丁目１４番１５号. Distance from Nagoya Castle: 1,225 m (within 1.61 km radius).
neighborhood: 丸の内・久屋大通
seats_text: 60席（最大80名まで貸切対応）
cultural_comfort_note: All staff are Nepalese; the manager speaks fluent Japanese and can handle dietary questions, but front-of-house communication is mostly basic Japanese.
vegan_detail: Tabelog lists a vegetarian menu (ベジタリアンメニューあり) and health/wellness menu. Nepalese cuisine naturally includes many lentil- and vegetable-based dishes (dal, vegetable curry, chatamari with vegetable topping, pani puri). However, no confirmed fully vegan menu exists and some dishes include egg, dairy (raita), or dried fish in side pickles. Ask staff which items are fully plant-based before ordering.
gf_detail: Nepalese cuisine is fundamentally rice-based (dal bhat, basmati rice, momo wrappers are wheat), which is more GF-friendly than Indian restaurants that rely on naan. However, naan and wheat-containing dishes are on the menu. No dedicated GF menu or labelling was found in any source. Momo (dumplings) use wheat-flour wrappers. Standard soy sauce (wheat-containing) is not a typical Nepalese condiment, but cross-contamination risk is unconfirmed. Celiac diners should call ahead and ask specifically which dishes are wheat-free.

bio.background: Nepal STATION opened in 2017 as what is described as Nagoya's first authentic Nepalese cuisine specialist restaurant. The concept grew from the reality that many so-called Indian curry restaurants in Japan are actually Nepalese-run — this shop drops the Indian-restaurant disguise entirely and serves the real Nepali table. It has been selected twice for Tabelog's Asia-Ethnic EAST 100 Best Restaurants (2022 and 2024).
bio.specialty: Daal Phat Set (two curries with rice, free refills of rice and lentil curry) and Samay Baji (a traditional Newari ethnic platter)
bio.philosophy: Present Nepali food as it actually exists in Nepal — regional, rice-centred, and spice-forward — rather than the adapted Indian-restaurant version familiar to Japanese diners.

anecdotes.text: The blog 名古屋単身ライフ describes Nepal STATION as Nagoya's first genuine Nepalese specialist, noting that most 'Indian' restaurants in Japan are staffed and owned by Nepalese people, making this restaurant an honest expression of a cuisine that had been hiding in plain sight.
The restaurant was selected for Tabelog's Asia-Ethnic EAST 100 Best twice — 2022 and 2024 — a rare recognition for a restaurant in a non-major cuisine category in Nagoya.

A blog review on シャルの甘美なる日々 describes the dining room as 'surprisingly spacious and elegantly refurbished with Western design blended with Nepalese touches,' creating an upscale ambience despite the narrow staircase entry — and notes Samay Baji, a Newari dish traditionally served at festivals with up to 108 accompanying dishes.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on dedicated fryers or separate prep areas for wheat-free dishes was found in any reviewed source. Cross-contamination risk is unknown and must be confirmed directly with staff.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce is not a standard ingredient in Nepalese cooking; the cuisine relies on mustard oil, ghee, and spice blends rather than soy-based condiments. However, the restaurant also serves izakaya-style dishes which may use Japanese condiments containing wheat.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen handles meat, egg, and dairy alongside vegetable dishes. No separation protocol has been publicly documented. Ask staff whether vegetable curries are cooked in shared pans.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The manager speaks fluent Japanese and can discuss menu contents. Staff are Nepalese nationals; complex allergy queries in English or detailed Japanese may require advance notice by phone (052-202-8802).

positives.text: Nepalese cuisine is inherently rice-centred (dal bhat, basmati rice) with many naturally vegetable- and legume-based dishes, offering more GF-friendly options than Indian-restaurant menus that rely on naan.
Tabelog lists a vegetarian menu (ベジタリアンメニューあり) and a health/wellness menu, indicating the kitchen is accustomed to accommodating plant-based diners.

caveats: Hours (11:00-00:00 daily, no regular closed days) confirmed across Tabelog, Retty, and search aggregators but not verified against an official website — call ahead to confirm current hours. The vegetarian menu listing on Tabelog does not distinguish vegan from vegetarian; egg and dairy appear in multiple dishes. No gluten-free certification or labelling was found; momo wrappers and naan contain wheat. Safety confidence is low due to absence of any published allergy-handling policy.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町2-17 シャトー西の丸 2F
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町2-17 returned [136.895645, 35.183727]. 423m from Nagoya Castle target center; within target box and 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町・名古屋城西側（西区樋の口町）
seats_text: 8席。完全個室的な空間で1日最大2組まで。
cultural_comfort_note: 完全紹介制・会員制で、既存客からの紹介なしでは予約不可。日本語対応が前提の非常に敷居の高い完全オマカーセ店。
gf_detail: Yakiniku is inherently meat-forward, and some cuts may be served with tare (dipping sauce) that typically contains wheat-based soy sauce. The omakase course includes dishes such as chateaubriand wrapped in seaweed with miso, which carries gluten risk. No gluten-free menu or dedicated GF options are documented. The restaurant states that allergies should be communicated at reservation time and accommodations will be made "as much as possible," but celiac-safe preparation cannot be confirmed. Cross-contamination risk from shared grill surfaces is likely. Inquiry at reservation is essential.
vegan_detail: This is a premium beef-focused omakase restaurant with no plant-based course or vegan options. The entire concept centers on Chita beef and select meat cuts; no vegan accommodations are documented or implied.

bio.background: オーナーシェフの東氏は30代前半。大学では地理学を専攻したが、渡米中に食文化に目覚め、料理の道へ転身。知多牛の生産農家と直接取引し、牛の繁殖から管理にまで精通した選肉眼を持つ。
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区。シャトー西の丸ビル2階に構える完全予約・紹介制の隠れ家的焼肉店。
bio.specialty: 知多牛のシャトーブリアンを中心とした完全オマカーセコース。シャトーブリアンの海苔巻き（万能ねぎ・味噌添え）が看板料理。1コース約500gの肉を使用し、ポワント（フィレの希少部位）やテートなど希少カットも登場。
bio.philosophy: 肉の旬を見極め、最高の状態で提供すること。シェフ自らが炭火で焼き、産地・生産者・部位の解説を交えながらもてなす。「訪れた人だけが知る謎の料理」というコンセプトで、1日最大2組限定の濃密な体験を提供する。

anecdotes.text: 渡米中に食文化に触れ、地理学専攻から料理の世界へ転身。知多牛の農家と直接パートナーシップを結び、生産現場から肉質を知り尽くした選肉眼でコースを構成している。
シャトーブリアンを海苔で巻き、万能ねぎと味噌を添えた看板料理は、店を訪れた常連だけが知る「秘密の一皿」として語り継がれている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 共用の炭火グリルを使用するオマカーセ店であり、グルテン含有食材（醤油ベースのたれ、味噌など）との接触リスクが排除できない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 焼肉のたれや味噌など、小麦を含む調味料が使用されるオマカーセコースであり、グルテンフリー醤油の使用は確認されていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 完全な肉料理専門店のため、植物性食材のみのコースは提供されておらず、ヴィーガン対応は不可能。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギーや苦手な食材は予約時に伝えれば「できる限り対応する」とされているが、セリアック病レベルの完全除去保証は確認できない。予約時の事前相談が必須。

positives.text: 完全紹介制・完全オマカーセのため、予約時に食の制約を細かく伝える機会があり、シェフが個別対応を検討できる環境にある。

caveats: 紹介制・会員制のため一般予約は不可。初回来店には既存客からの紹介が必須。予算はひとり6万〜10万円超。グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応の公式情報なし。アレルギーは予約時要申告。

cuisine: うどん・きしめん・そば（名古屋めし）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区新道1丁目13-16
geocode_note: GSI geocode of 愛知県名古屋市西区新道一丁目１３番１６号 returned [136.88739, 35.181561]. Approximately 1.21km from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) — within 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町駅周辺（名古屋城から約1.2km）
seats_text: 39席（カウンター8席、テーブル12席、座敷19席）
cultural_comfort_note: Traditional neighborhood noodle shop with tatami seating and a Japanese-language-only menu; little to no English signage or staff English ability should be expected.
vegan_detail: All noodle broths are made with muro-bushi (dried horse mackerel) dashi — a fish-based stock — and most dishes feature meat, egg, or seafood. There are no identifiable vegan dishes on the menu. Chilled tofu and edamame appear as side options but share a kitchen with extensive animal products and the broth base is fish.
gf_detail: Every noodle on the menu — kishimen, udon, and soba — contains wheat. Breaded and fried items (tonkatsu, karaage, tempura, fry sets) add further wheat exposure. The kitchen uses tamari soy sauce for seasoning, which is traditionally lower in wheat than standard shoyu but is not certified gluten-free here. No dedicated gluten-free options are offered and cross-contamination with wheat throughout the kitchen is essentially certain. Not appropriate for celiac diners.

bio.background: Founded in Showa 1 (1926) in Nishi Ward, Nagoya, めん処 一八 has been serving kishimen and Nagoya comfort food from the same address for roughly a century. The restaurant is now run by the third generation of the founding family, who have maintained the original recipes and ingredients with minimal change.
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区新道（昭和元年・1926年創業）
bio.specialty: 牛すじ月見きしめん and miso nikomi udon — flat Nagoya-style kishimen noodles in a muroshi-dashi broth, finished with premium tamari soy sauce; the silky broth recipe is the house's defining inheritance from its founders.
bio.philosophy: Earnest and honest preservation of predecessor recipes: the dashi is still drawn from muro-bushi (dried striped jack), a house tradition passed down through generations, and seasoning relies on top-grade tamari soy sauce for clean umami with gentle sweetness.

anecdotes.text: The beef-tendon moon-viewing kishimen — braised beef tendon served over flat noodles with a soft egg — is the dish most often cited by food bloggers as the reason to make a special trip to this century-old shop.

The restaurant states its broth is drawn from muro-bushi (muroshi, striped jack), a tradition inherited from the previous generation and described as giving the soup a deep richness and sweetness well-suited to kishimen and miso nikomi udon.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All noodles (kishimen, udon, soba) are wheat-based and cooked in the same kitchen. Tempura, tonkatsu, karaage, and other breaded items are also on the menu, making wheat cross-contamination near-certain throughout the kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The kitchen uses tamari soy sauce (たまり醤油) as its primary seasoning, which is traditionally wheat-reduced compared to standard shoyu, but no gluten-free certification is claimed and standard soy sauce use cannot be ruled out for all dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The dashi base is muro-bushi (fish-derived), used throughout the noodle broths. No vegan broth alternative is offered. Shared cooking equipment with meat, seafood, and egg dishes throughout.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling information or allergy menu is mentioned on the official website or Tabelog listing. The restaurant does not appear to cater to dietary restrictions; diners with serious allergies should call ahead (052-571-0485).

positives.text: Use of tamari soy sauce (たまり醤油) rather than standard wheat-heavy shoyu is a notable ingredient choice, though it does not make the restaurant gluten-free.

caveats: Hours confirmed via Tabelog and official site as of research date; official site notes Wednesday closures should be verified via Instagram (@kisimen18) for any seasonal changes. Cash-only confirmed on Tabelog; Rakuten Pay QR may also be accepted per listing detail. No English-language allergy communication confirmed — phone contact in Japanese recommended for any dietary queries.

neighborhood: 中区丸の内 / 市役所駅
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-5-1
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内三丁目５番１号. Approximately 1031m from Nagoya Castle reference point (35.1856, 136.8997) — within the 1.61km target radius.
seats_text: 36 seats total; counter seating plus tables; second floor also available per post-relocation reviews.
cultural_comfort_note: A long-established local eel shop with Japanese-only menus and signage; staff are welcoming but English communication should not be expected.
vegan_detail: Entirely an eel (unagi) specialist. There are no plant-based dishes. The menu centers on hitsumabushi and unagi-don, with dashi-based soups. Nothing here suits a vegan diner.
gf_detail: Traditional unagi tare (kabayaki sauce) is made with wheat-containing soy sauce — standard for all eel preparations in Japan. No gluten-free or allergen-adapted options are listed anywhere. Cross-contamination risk from shared cooking surfaces and shared tare pot. No dedicated fryer info available. Celiacs should treat this as high-risk and contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

bio.background: Unabun began as a long-established charcoal-grilled eel shop in the basement restaurant arcade of the Chunichi Building (中日ビル) in Sakae, where it operated for over 40 years. When the Chunichi Building was slated for demolition in early 2019, the shop relocated rather than close, reopening in April 2019 at its current address in Marunouchi — a short walk north up Otsu-dori from its former home.
bio.specialty: Hitsumabushi — charcoal-grilled eel served in a wooden ohitsu vessel, eaten in three ways: plain, with condiments (wasabi, negi, nori), and as ochazuke with dashi poured over. A standard lunch unagi-don at around ¥1,050 makes this one of the more affordable eel options in the city.
bio.philosophy: Accessible, no-frills eel cooking grounded in the adage 串打ち3年、裂き8年、焼きは一生 ('three years to skewer, eight years to fillet, a lifetime to grill') — the shop keeps prices down while maintaining the charcoal grill tradition.

anecdotes.text: After more than four decades in the Chunichi Building basement, the shop survived that landmark's 2019 demolition by securing a brand-new stand-alone space in nearby Marunouchi, winning praise from regulars who had feared it would disappear.

A Tabelog reviewer visiting in September 2025 (their third time) noted the lunch eel bowl at ¥1,050, confirming the restaurant remains active and prices have held steady through small incremental increases.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No allergen information is published. Traditional kabayaki tare contains wheat-based soy sauce applied to all eel dishes; no wheat-free alternative is mentioned anywhere.
Shared cooking surfaces (charcoal grill) and communal tare pot are standard at this style of eel restaurant, creating cross-contamination risk.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Kabayaki sauce (tare) is the defining flavour element of all unagi dishes here; standard Japanese tare is made with wheat-containing soy sauce and mirin. No wheat-free soy sauce option is listed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is eel-based. There are no plant-based dishes, so vegan cross-contact is a moot point — the restaurant is simply not suitable for vegans.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy or dedicated procedure is listed on any platform. Contact the restaurant directly (052-971-3148) well in advance if you have a wheat allergy.

positives.text: Completely non-smoking interior; small, consistent kitchen focused on a single ingredient, which limits the variety of allergen sources present.

caveats: Hours data is from HotPepper and Tabelog listings (last confirmed update not dated on those pages); the September 2025 Tabelog review confirms the restaurant was still operating. No official website found — HotPepper is the best source for reservation booking. Cash only: no cards, no e-money, no QR payments accepted. Dinner service on Saturday is NOT available (lunch only on Saturdays).

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-22-9 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 名古屋市西区那古野1-22-9 returned [136.891541, 35.17543]. Distance to Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) ≈ 1,350 m, within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 西区那古野（四間道）
seats_text: 17席（カウンター席のみ）
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-only omakase with the chef cooking directly in front of guests — interaction is warm but menus and signage are in Japanese; English assistance is not advertised.
vegan_detail: The menu is built around premium Japanese wagyu (Ozaki beef from Miyazaki) and seasonal animal proteins; vegetables are plentiful and a deliberate counterpoint, but no plant-only course is listed. Dashi type and hidden animal fats are unknown. Advance inquiry recommended.
gf_detail: Teppanyaki French cuisine relies on French sauces and condiments; soy sauce use in marinades or sauces is unconfirmed. No gluten-free menu, dedicated fryer, or allergen information is advertised. Cross-contamination protocols are unknown. Guests with celiac disease should call ahead.

bio.background: Chef Satoh spent more than two decades mastering French cuisine, training at French restaurants in Gifu, then at establishments in Kobe, Tokyo, and Nagoya before opening Pivote in August 2014.
bio.specialty: Omakase teppan courses centred on Ozaki beef from Miyazaki Prefecture — carefully selected sirloin and tender red-meat cuts cooked tableside on an iron griddle
bio.philosophy: To dissolve the heavy image of traditional teppanyaki by pairing premium grilled proteins with abundant farm-sourced seasonal vegetables, while cooking face-to-face with guests at the counter

anecdotes.text: Chef Satoh chose the quiet, warehouse-lined streets of Shikemichi for his restaurant specifically because of their nostalgic atmosphere — close to Nagoya Station yet feeling removed from the city, which he felt matched the calm, intimate dining experience he wanted to create.

The counter ceiling was finished with a cat's-eye treatment to add warmth against the coldness of stainless steel — a deliberate design gesture to make guests comfortable while watching live teppan cooking.

caveats: No official website exists. Hours sourced from Tabelog (last confirmed via multiple listing aggregators). Lunch reservation must be made at least 2 days in advance. No allergen or dietary accommodation information is publicly available — all GF/vegan queries require direct contact (052-533-6868). Chef name romanization varies slightly across sources (Takeshi vs. Tsuyoshi); Japanese kanji is the reliable form.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町2-17 シャトー西の丸 1F
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned coordinates for 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町２番１７号. Point is approximately 0.5 km from the Nagoya Castle target center (35.1856, 136.8997), well within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 西区樋の口町（浅間町駅徒歩約8分）
seats_text: 18席（カウンター席・4〜6名用個室あり）
cultural_comfort_note: A quiet neighborhood izakaya with no English menu or English-speaking staff noted; ordering confidence in Japanese is strongly recommended.
vegan_detail: This is a seafood-forward izakaya with a dashi-based kitchen — hidden fish stock (鰹だし・昆布だし) is standard across virtually every dish. No plant-based or vegan options are indicated anywhere on the menu or in reviews. Vegans should contact the restaurant in advance, but meaningful accommodation is unlikely.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese izakaya kitchen: soy sauce (wheat-containing), mirin, and tempura batter are ubiquitous. Mixed-fry (フライ) dishes were noted in reviews, indicating shared frying oil is very likely. No gluten-free menu, dedicated fryer, or allergy communication system has been mentioned in any source. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

bio.background: 福ろう opened on June 14, 2026 in Nishi Ward's Hinokuchi-cho, near Nagoya Castle, as the relocated successor to 梟 (Fukurou) — a long-running izakaya that operated for roughly 20 years in the Shinsakae/Higashi Ward area. The original 梟 was awarded a Michelin Plate in the Michelin Guide Aichi/Gifu/Mie 2019 special edition, recognising its quality among Nagoya's izakaya scene.
bio.specialty: Fresh seasonal seafood — sashimi platters featuring whatever the market offers that day (flounder, mackerel, octopus, sea urchin), alongside carefully selected Japanese sake and shochu.
bio.philosophy: A small, intimate counter-focused izakaya that prizes fresh fish, well-chosen drinks, and a relaxed neighbourhood atmosphere over scale or fanfare.

anecdotes.text: The original Shinsakae location earned a Michelin Plate in the 2019 Michelin Guide Aichi/Gifu/Mie special edition — one of only four izakayas in the prefecture to appear in that edition.

A Tabelog reviewer who visited the new Nishi Ward location shortly after its June 2026 opening described it as 'a new izakaya near Nagoya Castle that relocated from a 20-year Sakae location,' noting dishes like sashimi platter, cheese tempura, and sesame tofu.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Mixed-fry (フライ) dishes appear on the menu, strongly suggesting shared frying oil. No dedicated fryer is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard izakaya kitchen; wheat-containing soy sauce is used throughout Japanese pub cooking and no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is noted anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-forward kitchen; dashi from katsuobushi (bonito) or niboshi is standard across hot dishes, soups, and simmered items. No plant-based alternatives noted.

positives.text: Non-smoking throughout the entire venue — a comfort for sensitive diners.

caveats: Restaurant opened June 14, 2026 — very recently relocated, so only one Tabelog review exists for the new location. Menu details are preliminary. Hours and other logistics confirmed from the new Tabelog listing. Michelin Plate and 20-year history refer to the predecessor 梟 at the Shinsakae location.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区押切1-3-1 HP城西ビル 1F 1A
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI address search for 愛知県名古屋市西区押切一丁目3番1号. Coordinates place the restaurant approximately 1.34 km west of Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), within the 1.61 km radius requirement.
neighborhood: 西区押切（浅間町駅徒歩約8分）
seats_text: 12席（カウンター8席、テーブル4席）
cultural_comfort_note: Tabelog lists English menu and English-speaking staff available; the owner, who is from Myanmar, runs a welcoming solo-diner-friendly counter.
vegan_detail: Standard izakaya menu with no declared vegan options. Many dishes involve animal products (pork, chicken karaage, shrimp mayo, sashimi). Some nominally plant-based items (fried tofu, rolled egg, grilled rice balls) likely use dashi stock with bonito or are cooked in shared oil. No vegan labeling or accommodation mentioned on Tabelog or the menu page.
gf_detail: Standard izakaya fare heavily reliant on wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu) in marinades, yakitori tare, and cooking sauces. Fried items (karaage, shrimp mayo, hand wings) use wheat-flour batter. No gluten-free labeling, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodation mentioned. Cross-contamination risk is high in a 12-seat counter kitchen. Celiacs should call ahead and ask in detail before visiting.

bio.background: A small, 12-seat izakaya opened in May 2026 in the Oshikiri neighborhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward, run by an owner originally from Myanmar. Despite the international background, the current menu is entirely standard Japanese pub fare — the owner has noted 'ミャンマー感ゼロ' (zero Myanmar character) for now, with plans to introduce Burmese dishes in the future.
bio.specialty: Hand-braised slow-roasted pork (煮豚) served with affordable drink-and-dish set combinations
bio.philosophy: A relaxed neighborhood watering hole designed for post-work drop-ins and solo diners, with warm wooden décor and a welcoming counter atmosphere. Affordable drink sets and an approachable menu are the core proposition.

anecdotes.text: The owner candidly describes the current menu as 'an ordinarily delicious izakaya with zero Myanmar character,' while hinting that Burmese dishes will be added as the shop matures.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No allergen or cross-contamination information is published. The small open kitchen (12 seats) shared across fried, grilled, and sauced dishes poses a meaningful cross-contamination risk for gluten-sensitive diners.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat-based soy sauce is used throughout the menu in yakitori tare, marinades, and cooked preparations. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fried items share oil with meat and seafood. Bonito-based dashi is standard in Japanese izakaya stocks and not flagged as absent here.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy is described on Tabelog. Phone contact (052-990-0476) is the recommended route for dietary inquiries before visiting.

positives.text: English menu and English-capable staff listed on Tabelog, lowering the language barrier for communicating dietary needs.

caveats: Very new restaurant (opened May 2026); limited third-party reviews available. Hours listed as daily 15:00-00:00 with irregular closures — confirm before visiting. No official website found. GF and vegan suitability is low; this is a standard izakaya with no dietary accommodation infrastructure.

cuisine: 日本酒バー・日本料理
neighborhood: 那古野・四間道（名古屋市西区）
address_ja: 〒451-0042 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1丁目37−17
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野一丁目37番17号 returned [136.892914, 35.175095]. Distance to target 35.1856/136.8997 is approx 1.32 km, within the 1.61 km radius.
gf_detail: A standard Japanese izakaya kitchen — soy sauce (wheat-containing) appears throughout grilled dishes, simmered dishes, dressings, and course menus. Tempura is on the menu. No gluten-free labeling exists anywhere on the site. Wheat-based dashi and soy is endemic. Celiac diners must contact the restaurant directly before visiting; cross-contamination risk in a shared kitchen is high.
vegan_detail: Tabelog listing notes a vegan course is available but requires reservation at least 3 days in advance. The standard menu is heavily meat- and seafood-focused (duck, chicken, beef, cod, eel, squid, shrimp). Hidden dashi (fish stock) is likely in many dishes not labeled vegan. The vegan course must be requested explicitly at time of booking; do not assume any a la carte items are vegan without asking.
seats_text: 32 seats in the restaurant area, 16 seats at the bar counter. Tabelog lists up to 72 total including terrace seating.
hours_raw: Sun–Thu: 17:00–23:00 (food LO 22:00, drinks LO 22:30); Fri–Sat and day before holidays: 17:00–00:00 (food LO 23:00, drinks LO 23:30)
cultural_comfort_note: Staff speak limited English and the menu is Japanese-only; some staff have made efforts to help non-Japanese speakers, though experiences vary — a Japanese-speaking companion or translation app is strongly recommended.
caveats: Vegan course availability sourced from Tabelog listing only — confirm directly with the restaurant when booking. Hours sourced from both the official site and Tabelog; slight discrepancy in last-order times noted. Seat count of 48 is from the official site; Tabelog shows 72 including terrace.

bio.background: SAKE BAR 圓谷 (Marutani) occupies a rice storehouse estimated to be over 150 years old, set in the historic Shikemichi district on the west bank of the Horikawa River — a neighborhood that dates to Nagoya's castle-town era. The bar is directly operated by Sekiya Brewery (関谷酒造), a long-established sake producer from Aichi's Mikawa region known for the Horaisen (鳳来泉) and Kū (空) labels. The shop name 圓谷 (Marutani) is the brewery's former trade name, predating the current corporate identity of Sekiya Shuzō.
bio.specialty: Brewery-direct Horaisen sake paired with seasonal Mikawa-sourced Japanese cuisine, served in seasonal course format or à la carte
bio.philosophy: The guiding principle is 和醸良酒 (wa-jo ryo-shu) — harmony among people creates fine sake, and fine sake creates harmony among people. The establishment aims to be a place where quality sake shared between companions sparks genuine conversation and connection.

anecdotes.text: The building's earthen walls and exposed wooden beams have been preserved intact, giving diners the unusual experience of eating inside a working piece of Nagoya's Edo-period merchant history.
Sake poured here comes direct from the Sekiya Brewery warehouse, with staff offering food pairing guidance and sake-vessel recommendations based on the character of each bottle.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about cross-contamination controls is published. Tempura is offered on the à la carte menu, indicating wheat flour is actively used in the kitchen.
