soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat-containing soy sauce appears throughout the menu: in simmered dishes (鶏肉の醤油煮), dressings (醤油麹ドレッシング), grilled items, and course meals. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives are mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen prepares meat and seafood across all courses. The vegan course is a separate reservation-only offering, but no information about dedicated preparation areas or cross-contact prevention is provided.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Reservations are phone-only; staff can be informed of dietary needs at time of booking. The course menu notes that nut allergies (walnuts) should be disclosed in advance, suggesting some allergy awareness, but no formal allergy protocol is described.

positives.text: A dedicated vegan course is available on request with 3 days advance notice, indicating the kitchen has the capability to prepare plant-based meals when given adequate preparation time.

cuisine: Teishoku (set meals), Omurice, Tonkatsu, Japanese-Western hybrid diner
neighborhood: 西区児玉 / 浄心 (Joshin)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区児玉1-8-12
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch API for 愛知県名古屋市西区児玉1-8-12. Coordinates fall within target box and 1,412m from Nagoya Castle (within 1.61km radius).
seats_text: 44 seats across table seating near the entrance and tatami raised seating in the back room
cultural_comfort_note: A neighborhood shokudo with no English menu or signage; ordering requires basic Japanese or pointing at the wall-mounted menu cards, but the family atmosphere is welcoming and unhurried.
vegan_detail: The menu is almost entirely meat-centric (tonkatsu, kara-age, hamburg, pork loin, omurice with chicken rice). No dedicated vegetarian or vegan dishes are listed. Standard Japanese dashi (likely bonito-based) almost certainly underpins sauces and soups. No vegan accommodation has been reported by any reviewer; a Japanese-speaker should inquire before visiting.
gf_detail: This is a tonkatsu and set-meal diner: breaded cutlets (wheat panko), wheat-based sauces, and standard soy sauce (contains wheat) are core to the menu. No gluten-free options have been noted anywhere. Cross-contamination from frying is highly likely given shared fryer use for tonkatsu and kara-age. Not suitable for celiac diners; those with mild gluten sensitivity should discuss options in Japanese with the kitchen before ordering.

bio.background: A long-running family-operated shokudo tucked into a residential block a few minutes' walk from Joshin Station in Nagoya's Nishi Ward. Three generations work the restaurant together: the husband and wife team the kitchen, their daughter manages the dining room, and the grandmother lends a hand — a setup that has made it a genuine neighbourhood institution.
bio.specialty: Omurice — an oversized rugby-ball-shaped omelette rice, dressed with a Worcestershire-style sauce instead of ketchup, that has become the dish regulars travel across the city to order. The large version is memorably enormous.
bio.philosophy: Generous portions, honest prices, and the kind of cooking that keeps a neighbourhood fed decade after decade. The menu runs from teishoku through tonkatsu, hamburger steak, kara-age, curry and noodles — a deliberate breadth so that every member of a family or work group finds something.

anecdotes.text: Multiple food bloggers have described the omurice large as 'rugby-ball-shaped,' an unusually striking presentation for a humble neighbourhood diner.
One blogger noted that sharing two or three dishes between multiple diners is the practical approach, as every dish arrives at a scale that 'approaches large-eating territory.'
The warm orange-lit dining room with wall-mounted yellow menu cards and a tatami back room gives it the feel of a place unchanged for decades — a quality that regulars cite as part of its appeal.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tonkatsu (breaded pork cutlet) and kara-age (fried chicken) are core menu items; both are wheat-breaded or wheat-battered, and no dedicated fryer for gluten-free items has been reported. Shared-fryer cross-contamination is the expected default.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is the assumed condiment throughout the menu; no tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative has been mentioned by any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All set meals include meat or seafood as the protein; kitchen surfaces and cookware are shared. No vegan-only preparation zone or protocol has been mentioned anywhere.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling protocol has been documented by any reviewer or the restaurant itself. This is a small family-run kitchen; allergen queries would need to be made in Japanese directly with the family.

positives.text: Family-run kitchen with direct communication possible; a Japanese-speaking diner can speak face-to-face with the people actually cooking the food, which is preferable to large chain restaurants where kitchen staff may be untrained on allergy protocols.

caveats: No official website found. Hours confirmed via Tabelog but should be verified before visiting as small family restaurants sometimes adjust seasonally without notice. The founding date is not confirmed by any source; the restaurant is described as long-established but no specific year was documented. Dinner hours on the Tabelog page show 17:30-21:00 (LO 20:30); the nagoya-meshi.com source listed 17:30-22:00 — the Tabelog figure is treated as more current and authoritative.

cuisine: もつ料理・もつすき / Motsu (offal) cuisine, motsu-suki
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野2-20-22 円頓寺横丁 2F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 名古屋市西区那古野二丁目２０番２２号 returned [136.889343, 35.176453]. Approximately 1.32km from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) — within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 国際センター / 円頓寺横丁 (Entdon-ji Yokocho), Nishi Ward
seats_text: 16 seats total: counter 4, tables for 6, 4, and 2. No private rooms. All non-smoking.
cultural_comfort_note: Small 16-seat counter-and-table izakaya in a yokocho alley; staff are likely Japanese-only speakers, and the menu and ordering are conducted in Japanese with no noted English support.
vegan_detail: This is a dedicated offal (motsu) specialty restaurant. Every course dish — from raw heart and tripe sashimi to the signature motsu-suki and motsu nabe — is meat-based. There are no plant-based mains or noted vegan substitutions.
gf_detail: Motsu-suki uses sukiyaki-style sauce containing wheat-based soy sauce. The signature champon noodle finish contains wheat. Motsu nabe broth typically also relies on soy sauce. No gluten-free options or allergen accommodations are mentioned on any source. Celiacs should not eat here.

bio.background: Opened in May 2023 in the narrow alley of Entdon-ji Yokocho (円頓寺横丁) in Nishi Ward, motsu・sen was produced in partnership with a well-known hormone yakiniku restaurant from Shibuya, Tokyo. The concept brought a refined, date-night sensibility to offal cuisine — a stone-and-wood interior with art by contemporary artist Miura Koga lines the walls.
bio.specialty: もつすき (motsu-suki) — a sukiyaki-style preparation of fresh offal described as Nagoya's only version of the dish. Five varieties of fresh motsu are used; the broth is ignited tableside, then transforms into a full motsu nabe. The meal finishes with fresh champon noodles dusted with Cambodian pepper sourced personally by the owner.
bio.philosophy: Fresh offal enjoyed daily — the owner emphasises that offal must be savoured at peak freshness, and personally oversees sourcing and seasoning details including the selection of Cambodian pepper used in finishing dishes.

anecdotes.text: The owner personally developed the use of Cambodian pepper as the finishing spice for champon noodles, adding it as a signature flavour detail that sets the dish apart.
A limited Ladies' Course (Nov 2023–Mar 2024) at ¥3,630 was introduced to attract female diners, including a sesame panna cotta dessert — unusual for an offal restaurant.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Champon noodles (wheat) are the standard course finale; the kitchen handles wheat throughout service.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Motsu-suki uses sukiyaki-style sauce, which traditionally and in this context is wheat-based soy sauce. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Every dish in every course is meat or offal-based; there is no plant-only preparation zone or vegan option.

caveats: Hours on Friday–Saturday extend to midnight (24:00); the second Sunday of each month is also a closing day in addition to Mondays. The address references 円頓寺横丁 (Entdon-ji Yokocho); the target brief listed 遠藤寺横丁 which is a different romanisation/transcription of the same alley. No English menu or English-speaking staff noted. Dietary safety information comes from menu analysis only — no direct allergy policy statements from the restaurant were found.

name_ja: ルーキーズこだわり生パスタ。時々ふんわりパンケーキ。
name_en: Rookies Kodawari Nama Pasta. Tokidoki Funwari Pancake.
cuisine: パスタ（生パスタ）、パンケーキ、カフェ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区二の丸1-1 愛知県体育館 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode on 愛知県名古屋市中区二の丸１番; coordinates fall ~274m east of Nagoya Castle keep, well within 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 中区二の丸（愛知県体育館 1F）/ 名古屋城駅 徒歩5分
seats_text: 55席。キッズスペースあり。カウンター席なし、個室なし。
hours_raw: 土・日 10:30〜16:00。月〜金定休。イベント時により不定休。2月・3月の営業日はカレンダー等で要確認。
closed_days: 月・火・水・木・金定休（イベント開催時は臨時休業あり）
cultural_comfort_note: 愛知県体育館内のカジュアルなカフェで、スタッフとのやり取りは日本語が基本。英語対応は確認されていない。
vegan_detail: メニューの中心は生パスタとパンケーキ。乳製品・卵を使用した料理が主体で、ヴィーガン対応メニューの記載は公式・外部サイトいずれにも確認されていない。明太子クリームパスタ・ナポリタン・アボカドチーズトーストなどが主力メニュー。
gf_detail: 店名に「生パスタ」を掲げる通り、小麦粉を主原料とする生麺が主力商品。グルテンフリー対応メニュー・小麦不使用オプションの記載はどのソースにも存在しない。パスタ・パンケーキともに小麦を多量使用する調理環境であり、セリアック病・小麦アレルギーのある方には非対応と判断。

bio.background: 2022年9月28日、愛知県体育館1階にオープンした週末専営のカジュアルカフェ。名古屋城エリアの公共施設内という立地を活かし、観光客や地元ファミリー層を主要客層としている。
bio.specialty: こだわりの生パスタ（明太子クリーム紫蘇パスタ、昔ながらの鉄板ナポリタンなど）とふんわりスフレパンケーキ。
bio.philosophy: 手打ちの生麺にこだわったパスタを、体育館という日常的な空間で気軽に楽しんでもらうことを志向している。

anecdotes.text: 愛知県体育館の1Fという珍しいロケーションで、キッズスペースを完備しファミリー層に人気。名古屋城を眺めながら食事できる環境が特徴とされる。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 生パスタが主力商品であり、厨房内に小麦粉が常時大量に存在する環境。グルテンフリー対応に関する情報はどのソースにも記載がなく、交差汚染リスクは高いと推定される。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 乳製品・卵を使った料理が主力で、ヴィーガン向けメニューの設定はなく、動物性食材との接触を回避する体制の記載は確認されていない。

caveats: 営業は土・日のみ（10:30〜16:00）。イベント開催時は臨時休業あり。2月・3月など月によって営業日が変動するため、訪問前にホットペッパーまたは食べログで最新カレンダーを確認することを強く推奨。グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応は一切確認されていない。

name_ja: 名古屋名物 みそかつ 矢場とん 名古屋城 金シャチ横丁店
name_en: Nagoya Meibutsu Misokatsu Yabaton Nagoya Castle Kinshachi Yokocho
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-2-5 金シャチ横丁 義直ゾーン
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸一丁目２番 returned [136.89917, 35.182224]. Approximately 378m from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) — well within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区三の丸・金シャチ横丁（義直ゾーン）
seats_text: 72席（うち個室8席）、全席禁煙
closed_days: 名古屋城の定休日に準ずる（通常は無休、名古屋城の臨時休城日に準じて休業）
cultural_comfort_note: Located in the tourist-facing Kinshachi Yokocho food district adjacent to Nagoya Castle; staff are accustomed to foreign visitors and the menu is visually accessible with photos.
vegan_detail: The entire menu centres on deep-fried pork cutlets (tonkatsu) coated in panko and served with Yabaton's signature red-miso pork-based sauce. No vegetarian or vegan dishes are offered. Side items include shredded cabbage and miso soup, but the kitchen handles pork throughout.
gf_detail: Miso katsu is fundamentally incompatible with a gluten-free diet: every cutlet is dredged in wheat flour and then coated in panko (wheat-based breadcrumbs) before frying. The restaurant operates a single shared fryer environment with no documented gluten-free protocol. The retail miso sauce product lists soy, pork, and rice as allergens (wheat not separately declared), but the in-restaurant coating is the primary and unavoidable wheat source. No GF options exist on the menu, and no allergen-accommodation procedures are published. Celiac diners cannot eat safely here.
caveats: Hours follow Nagoya Castle's schedule — on days the castle is closed (occasional maintenance closures), Yabaton also closes; confirm via the castle's official site before visiting. The retail miso sauce product's allergen declaration omits wheat, but in-restaurant preparation involves both wheat flour and panko coating, making the restaurant itself unsafe for wheat-allergic guests.

bio.background: Yabaton was founded in 1947 by Suzuki Yoshio as a small stall called 「矢場のとんかつ」 (Yaba no Tonkatsu) near the Minami-Ohtsu-dori 4-chome streetcar stop in Nagoya. The name derives from the Yabachō neighbourhood — the area now known as Ōsu — where the original stall stood. By 1953 the business was incorporated, and the flagship Yabachō restaurant was established in 1970 under the full name 名古屋名物みそかつ矢場とん. The chain has since grown to around 30 locations across Japan and briefly internationally (Thailand, 2014), while maintaining a family-run ethos.
bio.origin: 名古屋市（矢場町）、1947年創業
bio.specialty: みそかつ — deep-fried pork cutlet (loin or fillet) coated in Yabaton's proprietary panko blend and topped with a rich, naturally aged Nagoya red-miso (豆味噌) sauce simmered with pork for hours. The signature item is the わらじとんかつ (waraji tonkatsu), an oversized loin cutlet shaped like a traditional straw sandal.
bio.philosophy: Yabaton positions itself as 「一流の大衆食堂」 — a first-class people's diner. The kitchen ages its red-miso in casks weighted with stacked stones across multiple seasons, producing a sauce that is richer and more aromatic than commercially brewed miso. The original spirit of the dish — an accidental, joyful discovery — informs the restaurant's unpretentious, generous style.

anecdotes.text: Miso katsu was born by accident: Suzuki Yoshio, watching a customer at his stall dip skewered pork cutlets into a pot of dote-nabe (Nagoya-style miso offal broth), tasted the combination and, according to Yabaton's own account, exclaimed it was delicious — then spent years perfecting a standalone miso sauce to recreate that moment.
The restaurant kept a 27-year continuous presence at Nagoya Stadium from 1972 to 1996, making it a fixture of match-day culture for an entire generation of Nagoyans.

The Kinshachi Yokocho branch sits in the Yoshinao Zone — the traditionalist half of the food street flanking Nagoya Castle's main gate — chosen deliberately to reflect the castle's historical character rather than the livelier Muneharu Zone on the east side.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Every item on the menu is deep-fried in a shared fryer environment. There is no documentation of a dedicated gluten-free fryer, a gluten-free menu section, or any separation protocol for wheat-sensitive guests.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The miso sauce is the centrepiece of the dish. The retail version of the sauce lists soy (大豆), pork, and rice as declared allergens without separately listing wheat; however, Nagoya red-miso (豆味噌/八丁味噌) is traditionally made from soybeans only and does not inherently contain wheat — but the restaurant's full preparation environment cannot be verified as wheat-free.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen is entirely pork-focused; pork fat, pork-based miso sauce, and deep-frying in shared oil used for pork cutlets make any vegan cross-contact unavoidable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen-accommodation procedures, allergy menus, or staff allergy-handling protocols are published on the Tabelog listing, official website, or Kinshachi Yokocho shop page. A Yahoo Chiebukuro report notes that a customer with a dairy allergy was told by Yabaton that their fried items contain milk components, suggesting the kitchen does respond to direct inquiries but has no documented systematic allergy protocol.

positives.text: Cards and electronic payments accepted (VISA, MC, JCB, AMEX, Diners, Suica, iD, QUICPay, etc.) — easy for foreign visitors without cash.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野2-20-22 円頓寺横丁 1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI address search on 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野二丁目２０番２２号; coordinates 136.889343,35.176453 are ~1,386 m from the Nagoya Castle reference point, within the 1.61 km limit.
neighborhood: 国際センター駅 / 円頓寺横丁 (Nishi-ku, Nakano)
seats_text: 10 seats total — 6-seat counter plus 2 small tables (2-top each)
closed_days: 月曜定休、第2・第4日曜休
cultural_comfort_note: Intimate 10-seat counter with no English signage noted; the omakase-style format and no English menu make prior Japanese communication or a Japanese-speaking companion advisable.
vegan_detail: Edomae nigiri is the entire menu concept — fish, seafood, tamagoyaki, and eel are the core items. No plant-based alternatives are listed anywhere. Hidden animal products (dashi, bonito, fish roe) are present throughout. Not a viable option for vegans.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese soy sauce (contains wheat) is used for brushing nigiri, which is the norm for Edomae sushi. Shari (sushi rice) is seasoned with red vinegar — rice and vinegar are naturally gluten-free, but no tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative is advertised. Tempura does not appear on the menu. No dedicated GF policy or allergy menu is mentioned on any platform. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant in advance to discuss whether tamari can be substituted; cross-contamination from shared prep surfaces cannot be ruled out without direct confirmation.

bio.background: Sushi Kiwami Hanare opened on 15 June 2023 as the seated spin-off of 立ち食い寿司 極 (Tachigui Sushi Kiwami), a popular standing sushi bar near Nagoya Station. The parent shop earned a following for making skilled Edomae technique accessible and affordable in a standing format; this 'hanare' (annex) branch moves into a quiet alley of the Entoji Yokocho shopping district in Nishi-ku, giving the same artisans a 10-seat counter where they can work at a more deliberate pace.
bio.origin: Entoji Yokocho, Nishi-ku, Nagoya (円頓寺横丁)
bio.specialty: Edomae nigiri using seasonal fish — particularly fatty nodoguro (blackthroat seaperch), kinmedai (alfonsino), and saba oshi-zushi — over red-vinegar shari
bio.philosophy: Ten seats so every guest receives the chef's full attention; seasonal fish selected daily; the red-vinegar shari is a signature across the Kiwami family and considered the foundation of the flavour profile.

anecdotes.text: A reviewer who visited in July 2023 described it as 'the seated Kiwami — a must for sake lovers,' noting the counter setting transforms the standing-bar spirit of the original into an intimate, adult dining experience.

An August 2024 visitor highlighted the nodoguro as 'incredibly fatty — it just melts,' and praised the kinmedai equally, with the red-vinegar shari called out as the defining characteristic of the Kiwami style.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination controls is published. Shared prep surfaces and utensils in a small sushi counter are a realistic concern for celiac guests.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Edomae nigiri is traditionally brushed or served with standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned on any platform.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Seafood, fish, tamagoyaki, and eel are the entirety of the menu; no plant-based items or vegan accommodations exist.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, gluten-free policy, or allergen labelling is documented on Tabelog, Gnavi, HotPepper, or the parent brand website. Guests with dietary restrictions should call ahead.

positives.text: The shari (sushi rice) is made with red vinegar and plain rice — both inherently gluten-free ingredients — so the rice base itself poses no gluten risk absent cross-contamination.

caveats: Hours are irregular: the 2nd and 4th Sundays of each month are additional closing days beyond the regular Monday closure. Sunday hours shown in hours_week apply only on the 1st, 3rd, and 5th Sundays. Cancellation policy is strict: same-day cancellations incur a ¥7,700 per-person charge. Reservation strongly recommended given the 10-seat limit.

name_ja: 蕎麦いまゐ サカエチカ店
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区栄3-4-6 サカエチカ内
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned 35.16861, 136.910278 for 中区栄3. Refined estimate 35.1688, 136.9069 from multiple sources. Distance from target centroid (Nagoya Castle 35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1.98 km — outside the 1.61 km radius. However, the target area is explicitly designated as 栄・サカエチカ（栄駅すぐ）, which is the confirmed location of this restaurant. Sakae district lies ~2 km south of Nagoya Castle; the radius check does not align with the stated area label.
neighborhood: 栄・サカエチカ（栄駅すぐ）
seats_text: 27席（カウンター・テーブル席あり）
hours_raw: 11:00〜21:00（ラストオーダー 20:45）、施設休館日に準じる
closed_days: 施設休館日に準じる（サカエチカのスケジュールによる）
cultural_comfort_note: Self-service券売機（meal-ticket vending machine）format common in Japanese fast-casual soba shops; minimal staff interaction required, but all signage and menus are in Japanese only.
vegan_detail: All broths are made with katsuobushi (bonito) dashi; no vegan broth alternative is offered. The menu includes duck (鴨南蛮), pork cutlet (とんかつ), and chicken (oyakodon) dishes. No plant-based option is listed.
gf_detail: Standard soba noodles contain wheat flour as a binding agent (juwari/100% buckwheat is not indicated). The tsuyu broth uses wheat-based soy sauce. Tempura items are battered with wheat flour. Cross-contamination risk is high in a self-service soba kitchen. No allergen information is published by the restaurant or operator.

bio.background: 蕎麦いまゐ is a soba chain brand operated by Nippon Restaurant System Co., Ltd. (日本レストランシステム株式会社), a subsidiary of Doutor-Nires Holdings — the same group behind Hoshino Coffee (星乃珈琲店) and Yomennya Goeemon (洋麺屋五右衛門). The brand launched its first shop in 2017 near Gaienmae Station in Tokyo and expanded steadily through the Kanto region before making its first foray into Nagoya in February 2024 with this Sakaechika location.
bio.origin: Tokyo (Gaienmae, 2017); first Nagoya location opened February 22, 2024
bio.specialty: そば三昧 — a signature bowl combining sea-lettuce tempura, raw egg, fried tofu, and wakame in a lightly sweet kombu-and-bonito broth (¥650)
bio.philosophy: Affordable, refined soba at under ¥1,000 per meal; the brand emphasises a 'fragrant, light-bodied' bonito-forward broth and efficient self-service dining accessible to solo diners and families alike.
roles: chain brand (Nippon Restaurant System / Doutor group)

anecdotes.text: The Sakaechika location is the brand's first shop outside the Kanto region, and it offers two Nagoya exclusives: kishimen (flat local noodles) as a noodle option, and a mini miso katsu donburi set — both unavailable at Tokyo branches.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No allergen or cross-contamination information is published by the restaurant or its operator (Nippon Restaurant System). The kitchen handles wheat noodles, wheat-battered tempura, and wheat-based soy sauce tsuyu simultaneously, making celiac-safe dining highly unlikely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The soba tsuyu broth is made with standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito (katsuobushi) dashi is used as the base for all broths; animal-based ingredients (duck, pork, chicken, egg) are handled throughout the kitchen. No vegan-safe preparation pathway exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, staff allergy-handling protocol, or GF/dietary accommodation policy has been identified through any public source. For questions, contact the restaurant at 052-211-7370.

caveats: Distance from the Nagoya Castle centroid is ~1.98 km, exceeding the 1.61 km radius, but the restaurant is confirmed at the explicitly designated target area (栄・サカエチカ). Hours follow Sakaechika facility schedule and may vary on facility closure days — verify directly for holiday periods. No published allergen information exists; all dietary assessments are inferred from menu and operator data.

cuisine: Specialty coffee, morning service toast, Nagoya kissaten
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-30-16
geocode_note: GSI geocode of 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-30-16 returned [136.891983, 35.174507]. Distance to Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1,419 m — within the 1.61 km radius. Coordinates fall inside the target lat/lng box.
neighborhood: Nishi-ku, Nagono/Shikemichi historic merchant district
vegan_detail: A traditional Nagoya kissaten focused on charcoal-roasted coffee and toast morning sets. The menu is built around wheat-based toast (black sesame, brown sugar, egg toast) and Western-style sweets such as chiffon cake, cheesecake, crème brûlée, and chocolate gateau. No vegan labeling or plant-based orientation found. Dairy and eggs appear throughout the menu. Dashi and other hidden animal products in sweets cannot be ruled out. Vegan diners should ask before ordering.
gf_detail: Toast is the signature offering — wheat bread is central to the menu (black sesame toast, brown sugar toast, egg toast). No gluten-free menu, labeling, or awareness noted across multiple review sources. The kitchen is not celiac-safe. Customers with celiac disease or serious wheat allergy should not eat here.
hours_raw: Mon & Thu 8:45–13:00 (may close at 12:00 when items sell out); Tue/Wed/Fri/Sat 8:45–16:00; Sun & holidays closed; irregular additional closures announced via Instagram
closed_days: Sunday, public holidays; irregular closures posted on Instagram (@cafe.de.sara16)
seats_text: 16 seats across roughly 4–5 tables, including counter seating. Solo diners are welcome. The space is intimate and fills quickly during morning service — waits of 30 minutes or more are common on weekends.
cultural_comfort_note: A solo-operated Japanese kissaten with no English signage or menu noted; basic pointing or simple Japanese phrases will be needed, though the quiet, unhurried atmosphere is welcoming to visitors.

bio.background: Cafe de SaRa occupies a renovated townhouse estimated at 120 years old or more in Shikemichi (四間道), Nagoya's best-preserved Edo-period merchant street in Nishi-ku. The building's exposed beams and alley setting earned recognition as part of the city's landscape heritage. The owner runs the cafe single-handedly.
bio.specialty: Charcoal-roasted coffee brewed one cup at a time by paper drip, paired with morning toast sets — the half-and-half black sesame and brown sugar toast is the signature item. Coffee is served in rotating high-end dishware including Hermès and Herend cups. Iced coffee arrives with a companion vessel of coffee-ice cubes to prevent dilution.
bio.philosophy: Slow, attentive coffee service in a space that values quiet and unhurried time — the kissaten tradition at its most considered.

anecdotes.text: The building has stood for over 120 years and the exposed timber interior earned a Nagoya city landscape commendation. The owner works alone, so patience is part of the experience.
Each coffee order on the regular menu comes with three types of complimentary mini-sweets — a hallmark of Nagoya's distinctive kissaten culture where morning and sweets are given, not charged extra.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Toast — wheat bread — is the core product of this cafe. Flour is present throughout the kitchen. No allergen controls or gluten-free procedures have been noted anywhere in available sources. This venue is not appropriate for anyone with celiac disease.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy and eggs appear throughout the menu (butter on toast, egg toast, cream-based sweets). No vegan accommodation has been documented.

positives.text: Entirely non-smoking interior.

caveats: Hours vary by day of week and the owner sometimes closes early when items sell out (especially Mon/Thu). Irregular additional closures are announced only on Instagram. Cash only — no cards or electronic payment. No reservations; arrive early to avoid waits. No gluten-free or vegan accommodation confirmed.

name_ja: レイヤーズ 丸の内 本店
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-8-26
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned 136.903915, 35.176258 for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内三丁目８番２６号. Approximately 1.1 km from Nagoya Castle center point (35.1856, 136.8997), within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 丸の内 / 久屋大通駅から徒歩約5〜6分
seats_text: 52席（1階22席、2階30席）。テラス席あり（ペット可）。
cultural_comfort_note: Walk-in casual burger shop with no English menu; staff are accustomed to local clientele, but pointing at a menu or using a translation app will get you served without difficulty.
vegan_detail: All burgers are built around 100% beef patties (Australian and Hida beef blend) with dairy cheese as standard; no plant-based patty or dairy-free option has been documented on any source reviewed. Buns are house-made and contain wheat and likely dairy/egg. Effectively no vegan path.
gf_detail: Standard wheat-flour burger buns are the core of every item on the menu and there is no gluten-free bun alternative documented anywhere. Fries and other sides are prepared in a shared kitchen environment; no dedicated fryer information is available. Soy sauce use in sauces cannot be ruled out. Not safe for celiac diners.

bio.background: LAYER'S opened in Nagoya's Marunouchi district around 2006 and has grown into one of the city's most celebrated gourmet burger destinations, earning a place on Tabelog's Hamburger Top 100 list repeatedly, including for 2026. The founder trained at the respected Tokyo burger shop BROTHERZ before establishing this location.
bio.specialty: The signature LAYER'S Burger — a towering stack built on house-made, lightly sweetened buns crafted fresh daily, filled with a 100% beef patty blended from Australian and Hida wagyu, plus lettuce, onion, cheese, bacon, pineapple, and egg, finished with a choice of BBQ, teriyaki, red hot chili, or sweet chili sauce.
bio.philosophy: Emphasis on materials and handcraft: every bun is made one by one in-house each day, and the beef blend is chosen for both flavour and quality. The kitchen has also produced a number of independent burger chefs who have gone on to open their own shops.

anecdotes.text: The owner trained at BROTHERZ, a respected Tokyo burger establishment, before bringing the concept to Nagoya — a pedigree that underpins the shop's reputation for technical craft.
LAYER'S has been selected to Tabelog's Hamburger Top 100 multiple times and has been operating for approximately 20 years, spawning several independent alumni chefs.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free fryer or preparation area is documented. Wheat buns are central to every menu item and the kitchen is a standard burger operation.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sauce ingredients (BBQ, teriyaki, chili variants) are not publicly disclosed; teriyaki sauce in Japanese kitchens typically contains wheat-based soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All patties are beef; cheese is standard on most burgers. No plant-based items exist to assess cross-contact.

caveats: Hours from Tabelog (11:00-22:00 daily, LO 21:00); one secondary source (ekiten) reported shorter Sunday hours (to 17:00) — confirm on the day if visiting Sunday. Cash only confirmed by Tabelog; one user review platform mentioned PayPay, which may reflect an outdated or incorrect entry. Official website (layers7.com) was unreachable at time of research.

name_ja: 美味しいごはん じらふ
neighborhood: 丸の内 / 久屋大通
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-15-23
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch API for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-15-23. Coordinates fall within the target lat/lng box. Distance from Nagoya Castle (~1.27 km) is within the 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 42 seats total: 18 table seats, a private annex (hanare) with 14 seats, and a private room with 10 seats. Full venue buyout available for groups of 8 or more.
cultural_comfort_note: A small neighborhood izakaya catering to Marunouchi office workers; menus and signage are in Japanese only, and while the owner is accommodating, communicating dietary restrictions will require Japanese or translation assistance.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu or labeling exists. Signature dishes center on Aichi Mikawa pork, local fried chicken, and kushiage (battered, deep-fried skewers). The special yakibuta sauce is soy-based and has been maintained since opening — likely contains wheat. Dashi type is unconfirmed but izakaya-style cooking typically uses katsuobushi-based dashi. Off-menu requests are accepted depending on available ingredients, so a diner willing to communicate needs in Japanese may find limited options, but nothing can be guaranteed without direct inquiry.
gf_detail: No gluten-free awareness or labeling found anywhere on the official site, Tabelog, or review sources. Core menu items are high-gluten: kushiage uses four types of breadcrumbs (wheat-based), karaage is battered, and the house yakibuta sauce is a soy-based tare (standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat). No dedicated fryer information available; the same fryer almost certainly handles breadcrumbed kushiage. Celiac diners should not eat here without detailed staff consultation in Japanese — and even then, cross-contamination risk from frying is very high.

bio.background: The owner-chef has over 20 years of culinary experience and runs this intimate izakaya in Nagoya's Marunouchi business district, conveniently positioned between Hisaya-odori and Marunouchi subway stations.
bio.specialty: 愛知みかわ豚の特製焼豚 — Aichi Mikawa pork that is steamed, pressure-cooked, then grilled with a house soy-tare sauce maintained continuously since the restaurant opened. Also known for local Oku-Mikawa chicken karaage and a rotating kushiage platter using seasonal ingredients.
bio.philosophy: Handmade creative cuisine that pairs well with alcohol; the chef will prepare off-menu dishes when ingredients allow, making each visit feel personal.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's operating motto, noted on the official site, is that service continues 'as long as the chef's stamina permits' — a candid admission of the one-person-driven nature of the kitchen.
The special yakibuta tare (sauce) has been kept alive and continuously added to since the restaurant first opened, a technique reminiscent of long-running ramen broths.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Kushiage (deep-fried skewers) is a core menu category using four types of breadcrumbs, almost certainly wheat-based. The fryer is very likely shared with other fried items including karaage. No dedicated fryer information found.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature yakibuta dish uses a soy-based tare that has been maintained since opening. Standard Japanese soy sauce (koikuchi shoyu) contains wheat. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pork, chicken, and egg dishes dominate the menu. The frying oil is shared with animal-based kushiage. No vegan-safe cooking zones or oils confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The owner-chef accepts off-menu requests when ingredients are available, suggesting some flexibility, but no allergy protocol or dietary accommodation system is described on any public source. Communication in Japanese is essential.

positives.text: Owner-chef with 20+ years of experience runs a small, personal kitchen — direct communication about dietary needs is more feasible than at a chain restaurant.

caveats: Hours and cash-only policy confirmed across multiple sources but should be verified before visiting as small independent restaurants change without notice. No gluten-free or vegan accommodation information exists in any public source — all dietary safety notes are inferred from menu content and izakaya norms, not confirmed staff statements.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区名西2-14-4
geocode_note: GSI address-search returned [136.882065, 35.189175]; Yahoo Maps confirms 35.18926, 136.88208. Straight-line distance from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1,651 m — marginally outside the 1,610 m target radius by ~40 m, but the restaurant sits squarely within the Tabelog-designated 浄心/浅間町 sub-area.
neighborhood: 浄心 / 名西 (Nishi Ward)
vegan_detail: No vegan options identified. The menu centers on soba and udon served with standard fish-based dashi (katsuobushi/niboshi), plus ramen and donburi. No plant-based broth alternatives are mentioned on any source.
gf_detail: Soba is listed alongside udon and ramen, so shared cooking water and wheat cross-contamination are near-certain. The restaurant's tsuyu is conventional and wheat-containing soy sauce is standard practice here. No allergen menu, no GF labeling, no staff allergy protocols are mentioned on any source. Not safe for celiac diners.
closed_days: 水曜日・木曜日 (Wednesday and Thursday)
seats_text: 20 seats, table seating only
cultural_comfort_note: A quiet, old-fashioned neighborhood soba shop with a local clientele and no English signage or menu; basic Japanese helpful for ordering.

bio.specialty: Ebi-katsu don (shrimp-cutlet rice bowl) and chilled tanuki soba are the dishes most praised in visitor posts; the lunch sets are noted for generous portions at modest prices.

anecdotes.text: A visitor described a chilled tanuki soba set (¥1,200) featuring springy, chilled buckwheat noodles with crisp tenkasu, a rich tsuyu, and a side potato croquette — calling it reliable neighborhood value.
The weekday mini-curry-don lunch set (¥1,000) drew praise for its stable, well-seasoned Japanese-style curry broth.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Soba is cooked alongside udon and ramen in a small kitchen; shared boiling water and utensils make wheat cross-contamination very likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or GF soy sauce is mentioned; standard wheat-containing soy sauce is used in the tsuyu and dashi, as is universal practice at this type of conventional soba-ya.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Broth is fish-based (katsuo/niboshi dashi standard for this style); no plant-based broth alternative is offered.

caveats: Distance from Nagoya Castle is ~1,651 m, marginally outside the 1,610 m radius; included because the restaurant sits in the designated 浄心/浅間町 area. Smoking is permitted throughout the restaurant (全面喫煙可). Hours confirmed across multiple sources but may change seasonally — call ahead. No official website exists.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間1-3-11
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned coordinates for 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間一丁目３番１１号; 789m from Nagoya Castle target, well within 1.61km radius and within target lat/lng box.
neighborhood: 西区 浅間町、浅間町駅徒歩2分
seats_text: 16 seats total — 4 counter seats, 12 table seats in a compact Showa-era dining room
cultural_comfort_note: A tiny Showa-era counter-and-table izakaya run by an elderly couple; no English menu or signage, weekday-only hours with dinner by reservation months in advance — comfortable for Japanese-speaking diners or confident regulars.
vegan_detail: This is a tempura-focused izakaya. Tempura batter is wheat-based and the frying oil is shared across shrimp, fish, and vegetable items. Miso soup accompanies most sets and likely contains katsuobushi (bonito) dashi. No vegan menu exists; vegetable-only ordering may be possible at dinner with advance notice but cannot be confirmed. Ask directly when reserving.
gf_detail: Tempura is the core offering — batter is wheat flour. Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is almost certainly used in sauces and marinades. A shared fryer handles shrimp, fish, and vegetables together, so cross-contamination is inherent to the operation. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodation has been reported anywhere. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: A long-established neighborhood izakaya in Nagoya's Nishi Ward, a short walk from Senamamachi Station. The restaurant is run by an elderly couple and occupies a modest, traditional building with a distinctly Showa-era atmosphere.
bio.specialty: Tempura rice bowls — especially the oversized kakiage-don (mixed vegetable fritter bowl) whose fritter famously extends well beyond the rim of the bowl — and the upper-grade ebi tendon with plump shrimp.
bio.philosophy: Simple, honest tempura crafted to order by a skilled master; lunch is limited to four items and runs only about 75 minutes, with dinner seats booked out roughly three months in advance.

anecdotes.text: The kakiage fritter is described by multiple visitors as comically large — it domes high over the bowl — yet the oil feels light and the batter stays crispy throughout the meal.
Dinner reservations reportedly require a wait of around three months, a remarkable waitlist for a 16-seat neighborhood izakaya.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is the restaurant's core offering; shrimp, fish, and vegetables are fried together in shared oil. Cross-contamination with shellfish and wheat is inherent and unavoidable.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (which contains wheat) is the expected base for tempura dipping sauce (tentsuyu) and any seasoning used at this izakaya. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives have been mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Shrimp and fish are fried in the same oil as vegetable tempura; miso soup served with lunch sets likely uses katsuobushi (bonito) dashi. No vegan-separated preparation has been reported.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information about allergy handling, allergen menus, or staff training for dietary restrictions was found across any source reviewed.

positives.text: Dinner is reservation-only and the small team takes each order seriously; advance reservation provides an opportunity to communicate dietary needs directly to the cook before arrival.

caveats: Dinner hours and reservation lead time (approximately 3 months) confirmed across multiple sources; lunch closes early (around noon) and lines form before 11:30 opening. No official website found. Cash only confirmed via Tabelog. GF/vegan suitability ratings are based on the nature of tempura cuisine — no restaurant-specific allergy disclosure was found; celiac diners should avoid.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内1-10-11 シーアイマンション丸の内1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内一丁目１０番１１号; approximately 1.29 km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（丸の内駅 徒歩1分）
seats_text: 本店70席（カウンター、掘りごたつ個室2〜20名）＋別棟「はなれ」完全予約制の個室（2〜20名）
cultural_comfort_note: 週末は定休で平日ビジネス客が中心。英語メニュー・英語対応の記載なし。基本的に日本語でのコミュニケーションが必要。
vegan_detail: 典型的な蕎麦居酒屋で、出汁はかつお・昆布ベースが標準的。天ぷら・煮物・酒肴など動物性食材を多用するメニュー構成。ヴィーガン対応の明示なし。北海道産蕎麦粉の手打ち蕎麦自体は植物性だが、つゆ（かえし＋鰹出汁）が動物性。季節野菜料理はあるが、隠れ動物性食材の回避は要問い合わせ。
gf_detail: 北海道産蕎麦粉を毎朝手打ちしているが、そばつゆ（醤油）・天ぷら衣（小麦）・各種料理に小麦が使用される。純粋な蕎麦のみであれば蕎麦粉100%の可能性があるが、十割そばか否か、つゆ回避の可否、小麦粉使用調理器具との交差汚染リスクなど、セリアック対応の明示は一切なし。グルテン過敏の方は来店前に必ず確認を。
caveats: 営業時間は複数ソースで23:00閉店・LO22:00と22:00閉店・LO21:30の表記揺れあり。来店前に公式サイトまたは電話（15〜17時は繋がりにくい）で要確認。「はなれ」ページ（/info-hanare.html）は404で現在取得不可。

bio.background: 名古屋・丸の内の地下鉄出口から徒歩1分、ビジネス街の一角に構える手打ち蕎麦と日本料理の店。本店はシーアイマンション丸の内1Fに70席を擁し、さらに本店近くに築160年の歴史的建造物を転用した完全予約制の別棟「はなれ」を持つ。
bio.specialty: 北海道産蕎麦粉を毎朝製粉・手打ちした蕎麦。コシと香りが特徴で、薬味には信州産「親田辛味大根」を使用。
bio.philosophy: 毎日柳橋市場で仕入れる旬の鮮魚・野菜と手打ち蕎麦を軸に、日本酒・焼酎とともに楽しむ「蕎麦居酒屋」スタイルを追求。

anecdotes.text: 別棟「はなれ」は築160年の情緒ある建物を活かした完全予約制の個室空間。ビジネス接待から少人数の会食まで対応し、都心の喧騒を忘れる静謐な雰囲気を提供する。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 天ぷらなど小麦粉を使用するメニューを提供しており、揚げ油・調理器具の共用による交差汚染リスクについての情報は一切公開されていない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: そばつゆに使用される醤油は通常小麦を含む。グルテンフリー醤油（丸大豆醤油等）使用の記載なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: かつおや昆布を使った出汁が料理全般に使われている蕎麦居酒屋。ヴィーガン向けの出汁分離・調理分離についての情報なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー・食事制限への対応方針について、公式サイト・各グルメサイトのいずれにも具体的な記載なし。来店前に直接電話での確認を推奨（052-201-1559、電話受付は15〜17時を避けること）。

positives.text: 毎朝手打ちの蕎麦は北海道産蕎麦粉100%使用の可能性があり、純粋な蕎麦のみ注文・つゆ別添えで対応できるかは要確認。

name_ja: メゼス サントリーニ
neighborhood: 浅間町駅周辺（名古屋市西区城西）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区城西1-12-14
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区城西一丁目１２番１４号 returned [136.893158, 35.18441]. Distance to Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 608m — well within the 1.61km radius.
seats_text: テーブル席30席（貸切最大34名着席・50名立食）
cultural_comfort_note: Staff includes both a Greek owner and a Japanese spouse; some English is available, but the menu and setting are primarily Japanese-language.
vegan_detail: Greek cuisine here centers on charcoal-grilled meats, lamb, seafood, and dairy-rich preparations like moussaka (béchamel) and yogurt sauces. Olive oil, fresh vegetables, and marinated seafood feature prominently, and some cold mezze plates may be naturally plant-based, but no menu is explicitly labeled vegan. Dairy (feta, yogurt, béchamel) appears throughout. Best to call ahead and ask.
gf_detail: Greek cuisine avoids soy sauce and dashi, which removes two common Japanese gluten vectors. However, moussaka uses a wheat-flour béchamel, Greek pies (spanakopita-style) use phyllo, and no gluten-free menu or dedicated fryer is mentioned anywhere. The kitchen is small and likely shared. No source confirms GF awareness or accommodation. Diners with celiac should call in advance to ask which dishes are flour-free.

bio.background: 名古屋唯一のギリシャ料理店として2016年10月にオープン。ギリシャ人のオーナーシェフと日本人の奥様によって営まれる小さな一軒家レストラン。浅間町駅から徒歩数分の古い長屋建築を使い、ミドルイースタン音楽が流れる落ち着いた空間を作り出している。
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区城西、浅間町駅周辺
bio.specialty: 炭火焼ミックスグリル（仔羊・ソーセージなど6種）およびムサカ（ナス・ポテト・ミートソース・ベシャメルソースの重ね焼き）。ギリシャ産エクストラバージンオリーブオイルとシーソルトを使用。
bio.philosophy: 地中海食の原則に基づき、オリーブオイル・新鮮な野菜・魚介類・肉類をバランスよく使う「メゼス（小皿料理）」のスタイルでギリシャ本国の味を再現することを目指している。
roles: オーナーシェフ（ギリシャ人男性）

anecdotes.text: ギリシャ人オーナーはギリシャ本国の料理法をそのまま持ち込み、「日本にいながら本物のギリシャ料理を」というコンセプトで開業。アテネ出身のレビュアーが「本国と同じ味」と評した。
ムサカはキッシュのように切り分けて提供され、ヨーグルトソースとの組み合わせで「コクが増す」と評判。肉料理は炭火焼きにこだわる。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on kitchen cross-contamination practices was found in any source. The kitchen appears small (30-seat restaurant in a converted townhouse), raising the likelihood of shared surfaces.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Greek cuisine does not use soy sauce or dashi as base ingredients, which eliminates the most common Japanese gluten/hidden-fish vectors. However, wheat flour is used in béchamel (moussaka) and likely in Greek pies.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Charcoal grill is used for both meat and seafood; no separation from plant-based items confirmed. Dairy (feta, yogurt, béchamel) is central to the menu.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No source mentions any allergy-handling policy, allergen menu, or staff training for dietary restrictions. English communication is noted as possible with the Greek owner.

positives.text: Greek cuisine base avoids soy sauce, mirin, dashi, and other umami seasonings standard in Japanese kitchens, making it structurally safer from several common hidden-gluten and hidden-fish sources.
Olive-oil-based cooking and fresh vegetable/seafood focus means some dishes (marinated octopus, grilled fish, salads) may be naturally GF — but this must be verified per dish.

caveats: Hours conflict between sources: Tabelog (primary) shows Mon-Sat lunch+dinner, Sun closed; Ekiten shows Mon/Tue/Wed/Sun dinner-only, Thu-Sat closed. The Tabelog data is used here as it is the most authoritative and recent. Confirm by phone before visiting. The restaurant operates on an irregular basis with course-only reservations required at times — call at least 3 days ahead for courses.

cuisine: 蕎麦・丼もの（福井郷土料理）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-19-19 沖田ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI address-search on 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-19-19; first feature coordinates [136.900284, 35.173466]. Approximately 1.35km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（丸の内駅 徒歩1〜4分）
seats_text: カウンター7席、テーブル8席、計15席
hours_raw: 月〜金 11:30〜14:00（LO 13:45）、土曜は不定休（Facebookで要確認）、日曜・祝日定休
closed_days: 日曜・祝日定休、土曜は不定休
cultural_comfort_note: 小さなランチ専門店でメニューは日本語のみ、英語対応の記録なし。
vegan_detail: 越前おろしそばのトッピングにかつお節を使用。ソースカツ丼は豚カツ（ラード・豚肉）主体。出汁や醤油の詳細は不明だが動物性食材が全面的に使われており、ヴィーガン対応メニューは存在しない。
gf_detail: ソースカツ丼のカツは小麦パン粉を使用。越前おろしそばは純粋に蕎麦粉だけである可能性があるが（一部口コミに「十割」の言及あり）、出汁・醤油への小麦混入や厨房でのクロスコンタミネーションリスクについて公式情報が一切ない。グルテンフリー対応を明示しておらず、セリアックには不適。

bio.background: 2015年4月13日開業。オーナーは福井県出身で、福井の郷土料理を名古屋のビジネス街に届けるべくこの店を開いた。地下鉄丸の内駅すぐそばのビル1階に構える小さなランチ専門店。
bio.specialty: 福井ソースカツ丼と越前おろしそば。薄く大きなロース肉にきめ細かいパン粉をまとわせ、濃厚なウスターソースにくぐらせたカツを丼飯の上に載せた一品と、鰹節・青ねぎ・大根おろしをたっぷり乗せた清涼感のある越前おろしそばをセットで味わうのが定番。
bio.philosophy: 地元福井の味を正直に再現する姿勢。ランチのみの営業で、食材・仕込みの質を保つ。

anecdotes.text: 福井出身者が試食して「本物の味」と太鼓判を押したと地元ブロガーが報告している。

gf_cross_contamination.text: ソースカツ丼の揚げ物と蕎麦を同一厨房で調理しており、グルテン交差汚染のリスクが高い。専用フライヤーの有無は未確認。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 出汁・つゆへの醤油使用が想定されるが、小麦含有の有無について公式情報なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: かつお節を越前おろしそばのトッピングに使用しており、動物性食材を全メニューで使用。ヴィーガン対応不可。

caveats: 土曜日は不定休のためFacebook等で要事前確認。現金のみ。ランチのみの営業（14時閉店）。グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応は一切なし。

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル4階
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 西区樋の口町3-19 returned [136.896072, 35.185513]; ~0.32 km from Nagoya Castle centroid, well within 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 西区樋の口町（浅間町駅徒歩約7分）
seats_text: 6-seat hinoki counter only
cultural_comfort_note: All materials and reservations are in Japanese; staff may have limited English, and the formal omakase setting assumes familiarity with traditional tempura counter etiquette.
gf_detail: Standard tempura batter is wheat flour — this is not a GF-friendly format by default. Tentsuyu dipping broth contains wheat-based soy sauce. The restaurant requests allergy information be communicated at the time of reservation as same-day changes are difficult; whether wheat-free alternatives can be arranged is unknown and must be confirmed directly before booking.
vegan_detail: Tempura is traditionally fried in sesame/vegetable oil (potentially vegan-compatible frying medium), but tentsuyu dipping sauce is dashi-based (likely bonito/katsuobushi). No plant-based or vegan course is advertised. Allergy and dietary restrictions must be communicated at reservation time; viability for strict vegans is unconfirmed.

bio.background: Chef Masanobu Inaba was born in Tokyo in 1967 and entered professional Japanese cuisine at age 18 after his family's sushi restaurant faced financial difficulties. He is largely self-taught, rising without the conventional pathway through prestigious establishments. He served as vice head chef at Grand Hyatt Tokyo's Roppongi sushi counter, then as executive chef at Conrad Tokyo's Kazahana Japanese restaurant, and later led the kitchen at Aman Nemu luxury resort in Ise-Shima.
bio.origin: Tokyo (Ginza flagship); Nagoya Owari location opened October 1, 2025 within the newly built Espacio Nagoya Castle luxury hotel, the first expansion of the Ginza Inaba group outside Tokyo.
bio.specialty: Seasonal omakase tempura: each piece fried individually with technique matched to the ingredient's characteristics, served at a 6-seat hinoki counter where guests watch the chef work. This is Inaba's first dedicated tempura counter.
bio.philosophy: 温故知新 — honoring tradition while refusing to be bound by convention. Inaba has stated he values 'rejecting outdated assumptions while treasuring heritage.' He pays close attention to seasonal ingredients, appropriate dishware, timing, temperature, and each guest's comfort to create 'a special experience of blissful moments.'

anecdotes.text: The breakfast Inaba developed at Aman Nemu in Ise-Shima was recognized by US magazine Brides as one of 'The Best Hotel Breakfasts in the world' — an unusual international accolade for a Japanese inn-style morning meal.

Inaba established Ginza Inaba in 2021 in partnership with MUGEN's Naohiro Uchiyama during the COVID-19 pandemic, a deliberate rejection of caution that reflects his 'reject conventional wisdom' philosophy.

The Nagoya venue, 尾張 粋 稲葉, marks the group's first regional expansion and Inaba's first tempura-dedicated counter, housed inside Espacio Nagoya Castle, a luxury hotel that opened October 1, 2025 on the historic Nagoya Castle Hotel site.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura batter is made from wheat flour by definition; a dedicated GF fryer or wheat-free batter is not mentioned anywhere on the restaurant's site or in any review source found.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tentsuyu dipping sauce is standard dashi + mirin + soy sauce (wheat-containing); no tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative is indicated.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dipping broth (tentsuyu) is almost certainly bonito-dashi based, the standard for tempura restaurants. No plant-based dashi alternative is mentioned.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The official site states: 'Allergies and other dietary restrictions should be communicated in advance, as same-day substitutions are difficult to accommodate.' This implies some flexibility if flagged at booking, but no specifics are given.

positives.text: Completely reservation-only format with advance allergy disclosure requirement means the kitchen has time to prepare; a 6-seat counter gives chefs full control over each guest's experience and direct knowledge of individual needs.

caveats: Hours listed as irregular because Tabelog and official site show slightly different lunch/dinner end times; the two-seating dinner system (17:30 / 20:30) means effective last entry varies. No regular closed day is declared (不定休) — confirm before visiting. GF and vegan viability is speculative; tempura batter is wheat flour and no alternative has been documented. Under30 is no based on dinner pricing ¥30,000–39,999.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区新道1-23-31
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch; coordinates fall ~1.27km from target center (Nagoya Castle), within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 西区新道 (浅間町駅徒歩7分)
seats_text: Counter seats and tatami with sunken kotatsu; small interior described as compact.
cultural_comfort_note: A small, smoky neighbourhood spot with a decades-long local following; no English signage or menu, phone reservations in Japanese only.
vegan_detail: This is a meat-only yakiniku and horumon specialist. Every dish centres on beef offal, pork, or other animal proteins. There are no plant-based mains, and the kitchen has no documented vegan provisions.
gf_detail: No gluten-free claims anywhere. Standard Japanese soy sauce (contains wheat) is almost certainly used in the tare and marinades. The stamina teppan uses hacho miso, which may also contain wheat. No allergen menu, no dedicated fryer information, and no staff allergy-handling protocol documented. Celiac diners should call ahead and approach with caution.
caveats: Seat count not publicly disclosed on any source reviewed. No official website found. Hours include a floating closure (3rd Wednesday) so confirm before visiting. All dietary safety ratings are inferred from cuisine type — no allergy menu or staff protocol was documented.

bio.background: Barikiya has been serving the working-class Shindo neighbourhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward since around 1970, making it one of the older surviving horumon-yaki joints in the area. The shop's blackened exterior, red lanterns, and perpetual haze of charcoal smoke mark it as a relic of Showa-era downtown Nagoya.
bio.specialty: Ninniku Sagari — thick-cut beef hanging tender blanketed in raw grated garlic, grilled table-side until the garlic mellows and caramelises on the hot grate.
bio.philosophy: Honest, unglamorous yakiniku at neighbourhood prices: fresh offal sourced daily, served in a no-frills room where the meat does the talking.

anecdotes.text: Regulars describe the interior fans running at full blast to cope with the smoke from flaring horumon — small intestine famously ignites on the griddle, filling the compact room with a satisfying char.

The finishing move is the garlic fried rice, built on the same punchy garlic base as the sagari and described by one blogger as wholly different from any Chinese-style fried rice — a distinctly Barikiya creation.
Selected for Tabelog's Yakiniku EAST Top 100 (百名店) in 2025 — a rare distinction for a cash-only, no-card, no-frills neighbourhood shop.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No fryer information is documented. The kitchen handles marinated meats and offal; cross-contamination risk from shared equipment is unknown.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No allergen menu exists. Standard Japanese soy sauce containing wheat is the expected baseline for yakiniku tare and marinades at this type of establishment — no wheat-free soy sauce or tamari is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: This is an all-meat establishment; every surface and grill is in contact with animal products. No plant-based preparation area exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling protocol has been documented for this restaurant. Phone reservations are accepted and calling ahead (052-551-0598) in Japanese is the only known channel for allergy enquiries.

cuisine: マクロビオティックカフェ / Macrobiotic Organic Cafe
vegan_detail: The daily lunch set and curry lunch are fully plant-based (no meat, fish, eggs, or dairy). The desserts — including a soy-milk soft-serve parfait — are also egg-, dairy-, and refined-sugar-free. Standard soy sauce (containing wheat) is likely used in cooking; no tamari-specific claim confirmed. Dishes use macrobiotically sourced organic vegetables, whole grains (enzyme brown rice), and plant proteins such as takakibi (sorghum) and soy-based substitutes. Hidden animal products such as bonito dashi are not part of the macrobiotic philosophy, but explicit confirmation that all broth is vegan has not been found.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu or certification. The menu is rice-based (enzyme brown rice) and naturally avoids many wheat sources, but standard soy sauce containing wheat is likely used in seasoning. No dedicated gluten-free fryer or kitchen separation confirmed. Celiac diners should ask specifically about soy sauce and any wheat-containing condiments before ordering. The macrobiotic framework does not inherently exclude wheat.
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区城西2丁目15番地13号
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI address search. Approximately 0.58km west of Nagoya Castle — well within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 城西 / Josai, Nishi Ward
seats_text: 15 seats; compact counter and table seating inside the Healing Ai natural foods store
closed_days: 日曜・月曜定休（一部日曜はワンデイシェフイベントで臨時営業あり）
cultural_comfort_note: An English-language menu is available, but the setting is a small local macrobiotic cafe embedded in a Japanese natural foods store — some familiarity with Japanese dining customs and basic phrases will make the visit smoother.

bio.background: Healing Ai was founded in 1991 by health consultant Katoh Hiroko, who turned to macrobiotics and natural therapy after recovering from her own illness caused by nutritional excess. She passed the business to current owner Itoh Kazushige, who has continued her mission for over two decades — writing a monthly newsletter for customers across 500 manuscript pages, later compiled into a published book titled '20 Years Later, Me.' The café won the 3rd Japan Vegetarian Award (Corporate Category) in 2018 for its contribution to plant-based lifestyle promotion.
bio.specialty: 毎日替わりプープーランチ (daily macrobiotic lunch set) — a single-plate meal featuring enzyme brown rice, miso soup, and rotating sides such as takakibi hamburger (made from sorghum, not meat), tofu terrine, hijiki marinade, and root vegetable potage
bio.philosophy: Three macrobiotic principles guide every dish: 身土不二 (body and soil as one — eat local and seasonal), 一物全体 (use the whole ingredient), and 陰陽調和 (yin-yang balance). The café aims to 'correct imbalanced nutrition and refresh daily fatigue' through Japanese traditional food, and stocks only products the owners personally believe in.

anecdotes.text: Founder Katoh Hiroko developed her health philosophy after personal illness revealed the dangers of nutritional excess; her recovery through macrobiotics became the founding story of Healing Ai in 1991.
The establishment won the 3rd Japan Vegetarian Award in the Corporate Category in 2018, recognizing its long-running commitment to plant-based lifestyle content.
Owner Itoh has written a monthly 'Healing Ai Letter' for over 20 years — a total of 500 manuscript pages — which was compiled and published as a book.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free kitchen or fryer confirmed. The menu is naturally low in wheat due to its macrobiotic, rice-based focus, but cross-contamination from shared surfaces or utensils cannot be ruled out.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard soy sauce (which contains wheat) is likely used in seasoning, as no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere in available sources. Celiac diners must ask specifically about soy sauce used in cooking.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The café philosophy is fully plant-based — no meat, fish, eggs, or dairy are used in the main kitchen — so vegan cross-contact is very unlikely in normal operation.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Staff are trained macrobiotic practitioners knowledgeable about ingredients sourced from the adjacent natural foods store; however, no formal allergy protocol or allergen-menu documentation has been confirmed.

positives.text: All standard desserts are made without eggs, dairy, or refined sugar; soy-milk soft-serve is used throughout the sweets menu.
The daily lunch uses organic, pesticide-free vegetables and whole grains (enzyme brown rice); no animal products appear in the main menu.

caveats: Hours marked irregular because occasional Sunday openings occur for one-day chef events — check the official site or Instagram (@healai1991) before visiting on a Sunday. Cash-only with PayPay QR code accepted; no credit cards. Lunch service ends at 14:00; arriving after that means sweets and drinks only.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋4-10-11
neighborhood: 浄心（西区）
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch API; coordinates fall within target box and approximately 1.2km from Nagoya Castle centroid, within the 1.61km radius.
seats_text: 10-seat counter only
hours_raw: 月火水金土 11:30-13:30、木・日曜定休
cultural_comfort_note: A compact 10-seat counter shop with no English menu or signage; ordering is straightforward but best managed by pointing at the menu or using a translation app.
vegan_detail: The signature shoyu ramen is built on an animal-based broth (chicken and/or pork bone stock), and the soy sauce tare almost certainly contains wheat. No plant-based or vegan options are listed or described in any source. Vegans have no workable choice here.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese shoyu (soy sauce) is brewed with wheat and is the defining flavour of this bowl. No gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere. The kitchen is a tiny counter operation with no dedicated allergen protocols on record. This shop is not safe for anyone with celiac disease or serious wheat allergy.

bio.specialty: Shoyu ramen — a light, clear, old-school soy-sauce broth bowl priced around ¥700, prepared without chemical seasoning (無化調). Takana (pickled mustard greens) variations and a ramen-plus-fried-rice lunch set are also available.
bio.philosophy: Reviewers describe the broth as 'gentle and additive-free' — a quietly principled approach to keeping the soup honest in an era of flavour boosters.

anecdotes.text: One Tabelog reviewer wrote: 'A high-level shoyu ramen made without chemical seasoning' — high praise for a sub-¥1,000 neighbourhood bowl.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on cross-contamination controls. The kitchen is a small counter operation and no allergen protocols are documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-brewed shoyu is the defining ingredient of the ramen. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth is animal-based (chicken and/or pork bone); no plant-only preparation exists at this shop.

caveats: Hours from Tabelog show lunch-only service (11:30-13:30, Mon-Wed/Fri-Sat). Navitime lists different hours (11:00-14:00 / 17:00-22:00) which may be outdated or erroneous — Tabelog's data, corroborated by multiple sources, is treated as current. No official website exists to cross-check. The shop has only 12 Tabelog reviews, limiting data depth. Owner/chef identity is not documented in any accessible source.

name_ja: 矢場とん 名古屋城金シャチ横丁店
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-2-5 金シャチ横丁義直ゾーン
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-2-5. Approximately 0.38 km from Nagoya Castle center — well within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 中区三の丸（金シャチ横丁・義直ゾーン）
seats_text: 68 seats; counter and table seating for 2–8 guests; no private rooms or tatami areas; fully non-smoking
hours_raw: 10:30〜17:30（L.O. 17:00）、名古屋城の定休日に準ずる
closed_days: Follows Nagoya Castle closure schedule (typically no regular weekly closing day, but closed on Nagoya Castle's designated off-days — check nagoyajo.nagoya.jp for current schedule)
cultural_comfort_note: English menus available; located inside a tourist-facing food complex at Nagoya Castle, so staff are accustomed to international visitors.
vegan_detail: Yabaton specializes entirely in breaded pork cutlet (tonkatsu). The core menu is pork-centric with no documented vegan alternatives. Side vegetables and cabbage are present but cooked in the same environment as meat and served alongside animal products. The signature miso sauce is simmered with pork. No vegan menu or plant-based options have been identified at this or any Yabaton location.
gf_detail: Miso katsu is inherently wheat-heavy: the pork cutlet is dredged in wheat flour and coated with panko breadcrumbs before deep-frying. The proprietary miso sauce (aged 18 months, simmered with pork) is soybean-based, but the breading alone makes the core product unsafe for anyone avoiding gluten. No gluten-free preparation, dedicated fryer, or wheat-free menu option has been identified at Yabaton. Celiac diners should not eat here.

bio.background: Yabaton was founded in 1947 by Shigeo Suzuki as 'Yaba no Tonkatsu,' a small shop in front of Minami-Otsu-dori tram stop in postwar Nagoya. Suzuki reportedly discovered the miso katsu concept when he dipped a skewered cutlet into a dote-nabe (miso-braised offal) pot at a street stall and was struck by how good it tasted — he then developed his own proprietary miso sauce and built it into a full restaurant. The company became a limited company in 1953, expanded to a flagship Yabacho store in 1970, and passed to second-generation leader Takayuki Suzuki in 1980. Today the chain operates 28 domestic locations and has international outposts including Bangkok. This branch opened inside the Kinshachi Yokocho Yoshinao Zone food district, a curated dining complex built adjacent to Nagoya Castle's main gate.
bio.specialty: Miso katsu — breaded pork cutlet (loin or fillet) dipped in or plated with a deeply savory red soybean miso sauce aged 18 months in cask under stone weights. The signature 'waraji' (sandal-sized) cutlet is the crowd-draw.
bio.philosophy: Yabaton insists on dipping the cutlet into miso sauce rather than simply pouring it on top — maintaining the founder's original technique against the modern trend of sauce-on-top service.

anecdotes.text: The miso katsu concept was born when founder Shigeo Suzuki dipped a kushikatsu (skewered cutlet) into a dote-nabe miso pot at a postwar street stall and realized the combination was extraordinary — leading him to develop his now-famous proprietary sauce.

A longtime customer recalled: 'During postwar recovery, miso katsu was truly extravagant. We worked hard and enjoyed it only once every few months as our sole pleasure.' It was considered a luxury item in the 1940s and 1950s.
Since 2008, Yabaton has built five elementary schools in Cambodia through donations, and in 2017 established the 'Yabaton Children's Fund' — unusual community philanthropy for a tonkatsu chain.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All katsu items are deep-fried in shared fryers — seafood (scallop, shrimp, oyster) and vegetable kushikatsu are fried alongside pork cutlets. No dedicated fryer for any item has been documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature dish is coated in wheat flour and panko breadcrumbs before frying — this is the core preparation and cannot be omitted. The miso sauce itself is soybean-based, but cross-contact with wheat throughout the kitchen is guaranteed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All cooking occurs in a pork-focused kitchen. The miso sauce is simmered with pork. No plant-only preparation zone or vegan menu items have been identified.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No specific allergen handling protocols or allergy communication procedures have been documented for this location. The HotPepper listing notes English menus are available but does not mention allergen cards or staff training.

positives.text: Credit cards and multiple e-money formats accepted; English menus available; located in a well-lit tourist-facing complex with clear signage — easier navigation for foreign visitors than a typical street restaurant.

caveats: Closure days follow Nagoya Castle's schedule rather than a fixed weekly pattern — verify current calendar at nagoyajo.nagoya.jp before visiting. Hours (10:30–17:30) are significantly shorter than a typical dinner restaurant; this is a lunch/daytime venue only. Seat count varies between 68 (official Kinshachi site) and 72 (HotPepper); 68 used as primary figure. This is a large commercial chain, not a small independent.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野2-20-22 円頓寺横丁 2F
geocode_note: GSI geocode of 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野二丁目20番22号 returned [136.889343, 35.176453]. Distance to Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1.38km — within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 那古野・円頓寺横丁 (Nakono / Endoji Yokocho)
cuisine: もつ鍋・もつすき (Offal hot pot / motsu sukiyaki)
vegan_detail: Entirely meat/offal-focused. Every dish — sashimi of heart and tripe, grilled hormonyaki, the signature motsusuki, champon finish — is built around beef offal. No plant-based or vegan options exist on the menu.
gf_detail: The signature motsusuki is a sukiyaki-style broth almost certainly based on soy sauce (which contains wheat) and mirin. No gluten-free claims or accommodations are mentioned anywhere. Champon noodles (〆) contain wheat. This venue is not safe for celiac diners.
seats_text: 16席（カウンター4席、テーブル6・4・2名用）
cultural_comfort_note: No English menu or signage mentioned; reservations and communication are in Japanese, though the intimate counter format and staff attentiveness noted in reviews suggest a welcoming environment for those who can gesture or use translation apps.

bio.background: Opened in May 2023 on the second floor of Endoji Yokocho (円頓寺横丁), a refurbished alley of small restaurants in Nagoya's Nishi Ward. The concept was produced by a well-regarded hormonyaki specialist from Shibuya, Tokyo, with the aim of bringing motsu sukiyaki — a style nearly absent from Nagoya — to the city in a stylish, accessible setting.
bio.specialty: もつすき (motsusuki): beef offal cooked sukiyaki-style in a soy-based sauce, evolving through three stages from seared offal to full hotpot, finishing with champon noodles dusted with Cambodian pepper sourced directly by the owner.
bio.philosophy: The owner/producer insists on daily-fresh offal delivery, personally crafts the original sauces, and sources Cambodian pepper directly — a level of ingredient control unusual for a 16-seat neighbourhood spot. Staff cook tableside to maintain the correct heat and technique throughout the course.

anecdotes.text: The motsu sashimi — sliced raw heart and tripe served as course openers — was cited by multiple Retty reviewers as unexpectedly refined, changing their assumptions about offal cuisine.

A ladies-only abbreviated course (6 dishes, ¥3,630) was introduced in early 2024, reflecting the owner's intent to make the venue approachable for diners who might be intimidated by offal-focused omakase.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on cross-contamination controls. The kitchen handles wheat-containing soy sauce, champon noodles, and likely other wheat-based ingredients throughout service.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Motsusuki is a sukiyaki-style dish whose sauce is almost certainly soy-sauce-based (containing wheat). No gluten-free soy sauce (tamari) alternative is mentioned anywhere in listings or reviews.
The finish course is champon noodles, which contain wheat.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Entire menu is built around beef offal — no plant-based dishes exist; vegan cross-contact is moot as there is nothing vegan to order.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy or allergen menu is referenced in any source. Diners with dietary restrictions should call 052-571-2919 before visiting.

caveats: Second Sunday closure is irregular — confirm before visiting. Hours and closed days sourced from HotPepper and Tabelog as of mid-2026; call ahead or check the reservation system to verify current schedule. No official standalone website; Instagram @motsu.sen may carry the most current info.

name_ja: 長命うどん 弁天通店
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区城西5-19-2
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch; coordinates 35.1919, 136.8967 are within the target box and approximately 0.75 km from Nagoya Castle, well inside the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 浄心（西区）
seats_text: 25 seats across counter, table, and raised tatami-style seating.
hours_raw: 月〜金 11:00〜16:00頃（売り切れ次第終了）、土・日・祝 11:00〜14:00頃（売り切れ次第終了）、無休
cultural_comfort_note: A no-frills cash-only counter shop with no English menu or signage; basic Japanese is helpful for ordering, though the short menu and pointing at photos or the menu board is manageable.
gf_detail: Udon is the core product — wheat noodles are unavoidable. Dashi is almost certainly fish-based (bonito/katsuo). Soy sauce in the broth contains wheat. No GF options exist and cross-contamination from wheat is inherent to every dish. Celiac diners cannot eat here safely.
vegan_detail: Broth is fish-based dashi (bonito); tempura toppings include seafood. No plant-based or vegan options are offered or noted anywhere in available sources.

bio.background: 長命うどん (Chomei Udon) traces its roots to 1913 (Taisho 2), when a Nagoya noodle-stall family was chosen to make the ceremonial first crossing of the newly built Naya Bridge. The city mayor marked the occasion by bestowing the name 'Chomei' (longevity) on the shop. The Bentendori branch is one of several noren-wake (apprentice-spun-off) locations; only trainees who complete an apprenticeship at the main Nakamura-ward shop and are recognised as masters earn the right to open an affiliated branch.
bio.origin: Nagoya (Nakamura Ward main shop, est. 1913; Bentendori branch in Nishi Ward)
bio.specialty: Classic Nagoya market-style udon with interchangeable noodle types (udon, soba, kishimen, chuka soba) and mix-and-match combinations — house-made noodles, light savory broth, kakiage tempura; the branch is known for not serving kishimen.
bio.philosophy: Deliver honest, affordable noodles hand-crafted with the same sincerity as the original 1913 shop; slight branch-to-branch variation is accepted as proof of handmade craft.

anecdotes.text: The name 'Chomei' (long life) was personally granted by the Nagoya city mayor at the Naya Bridge opening ceremony in the Taisho era — an origin story the chain still highlights as its founding legend.

The Bentendori location is housed in the ground floor of an apartment building on the north side of Nagoya Castle's perimeter, with a small rear car park — an incongruous setting that adds to its local-secret feel.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat is the primary ingredient (udon noodles); cross-contamination is inherent and unavoidable throughout the kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Broth is seasoned with standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat; no wheat-free soy sauce alternatives are mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi is used in the broth; tempura includes seafood toppings. No plant-based broth option is available.

caveats: Hours end when sold out — closing time varies daily and can be earlier than posted. No allergy menu exists; this is a wheat-centric shop with no accommodations for GF or vegan diners. The official chain website covers the main shop network; the Bentendori branch has no separate official web presence.

cuisine: もつ鍋・福岡風（博多水炊き風）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸ノ内3-5-40 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内三丁目５番４０号 returned [136.906113, 35.177818]. Distance to Nagoya Castle reference point (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1043m, within the 1.61km radius. Coordinates fall within target lat/lng box.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内 / 市役所・久屋大通駅圏
seats_text: 43席。カウンター6席、掘りごたつ4席、座敷（最大14名）を含む。
closed_days: 火曜日定休（6〜7月は不定休あり）
cultural_comfort_note: 地元客中心の小さな居酒屋で、英語メニューや英語対応スタッフの記載はなく、日本語でのやり取りが基本となる。
vegan_detail: もつ鍋（内臓肉）が看板メニューの肉料理専門店。植物性のみの選択肢はなく、ビーガン対応は不可。
gf_detail: もつ鍋のスープには自家製醤油ベースの酢だれが使用されており、醤油には通常小麦が含まれる。〆の麺（ちゃんぽん・ごまラーメン）にも小麦が含まれる。グルテンフリー対応の明示はなく、食物アレルギーへの対応については事前に電話で確認が必要。専用フライヤーの有無も不明。

bio.background: 愛知県一宮市出身。会社員を辞めて調理師専門学校へ進み、中華料理店での修業を経て大阪の餃子専門店でも腕を磨いた。その後、福岡の名門もつ鍋店が大阪に出した支店で約1年半の修業を積み、2007年に暖簾分けの形で名古屋・丸の内に「もつ藤」を開いた。
bio.origin: 福岡・博多の名門もつ鍋から暖簾分け（2007年開業）
bio.specialty: 博多水炊き風もつ鍋。丁寧に下茹で・脂抜きした国産モツを、自家製醤油酢だれとゆずこしょうで食べるあっさりとした一鍋。
bio.philosophy: 国産の新鮮な食材と九州の本場調味料にこだわり、くどさのないすっきりとした味わいを追求。カウンターでは焼き物を目の前で仕上げるライブ感も楽しめる。

anecdotes.text: もつ鍋の上に餃子の皮を蓋のようにのせ、そのまま鍋に溶かし込むのが看板の食べ方。〆はちゃんぽんかごまラーメンで、テーブルごしに大量のごまをかけるパフォーマンスも名物のひとつ。
伊藤シェフは「餃子を作るとき、普通の水の代わりに熱湯を使うと生地の温度が保たれてパリッと仕上がる」と語る。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテンフリー対応や交差汚染への取り組みについて公式・口コミ上での言及はなく、詳細は不明。事前の電話確認が必須。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: もつ鍋の自家製たれに醤油ベースの酢だれを使用。一般的な醤油には小麦が含まれるため、セリアック病や小麦アレルギーの方は要注意。グルテンフリー醤油使用の記載なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: もつ鍋専門店であり、全メニューが動物性食材を使用。ビーガン・ベジタリアン向けの選択肢および交差汚染への配慮についての情報はない。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応や食事制限への配慮に関する記載はウェブ上に見当たらない。来店前に電話（052-961-9525）で確認することを強く推奨。

caveats: もつ鍋専門の肉料理店であり、グルテンフリー・ビーガン対応の明示はない。醤油ベースのたれや麺類〆に小麦が含まれる可能性が高く、セリアック病の方には推奨できない。6〜7月は不定休あり（7月22日水曜も臨時休業の記録あり）。公式ウェブサイトは確認できず。

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-4-31 愛知県水産会館B1
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-4-31 returned [136.904938, 35.177357]; approx 1.03 km from Nagoya Castle target point, within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 丸の内・名古屋城駅徒歩5分
seats_text: 66席（カウンター7、テーブル28、座敷31）。掘りごたつの個室あり。
cultural_comfort_note: Established izakaya catering to local Japanese clientele; no English-language menu or signage found — Japanese fluency or a translation app recommended.
vegan_detail: A seafood-specialist izakaya built around dashi-based broths (the signature mozuku tako shabu uses seafood stock), fish sashimi, simmered fish, and seasonal fugu courses. No plant-based or vegan options are indicated anywhere on the menu or website. Hidden dashi (bonito/seafood) is present in virtually every dish.
gf_detail: No gluten-free claims or allergen information published. Wheat risk is high on multiple fronts: soy sauce (tamari not mentioned) is standard in Japanese izakaya cooking and almost certainly used in simmered fish and dipping sauces; the lunch menu explicitly features フライ定食 (fried dishes), meaning wheat-battered items are fried in the kitchen; dashi-based broths may contain wheat-containing seasonings. No dedicated fryer. The restaurant has not addressed cross-contamination. Celiac diners should avoid.

bio.background: 晴快荘は愛知県日間賀島で旅館「ホテル晴快荘」を営む一家が名古屋に開いた海鮮料理店。「島の幸を気軽に楽しんでほしい」という思いのもと、日間賀島産のとらふぐやタコ、季節の魚介を産地直送で提供する。本店は愛知県水産会館の地下1階という漁業ゆかりの場所に構え、1970年代から地元ビジネスパーソンや食通に愛され続けている。
bio.specialty: もずくたこしゃぶ（日間賀島産タコを香り高い出汁ともずく・ワカメと合わせる名物しゃぶしゃぶ）、季節のとらふぐコース（10月〜3月）
bio.philosophy: 日間賀島から直送した旬の食材を「お値打ちな価格でくつろいだ雰囲気で」提供することをモットーとする。

anecdotes.text: レビュアーは女将を「気さくなおばちゃん」と評し、カジュアルで温かみのある接客が常連客を引きつけていると記している。
ランチは週替わりの魚定食が人気で、開店前から行列ができることもあるほど地元の支持を集めている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: ランチメニューにフライ定食（揚げ物）が含まれており、同一キッチンで小麦を使用した揚げ物が調理されている。専用フライヤーの有無は不明だが、小麦との接触汚染リスクは高い。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油ベースの味付けが標準的な居酒屋・海鮮料理店であり、煮魚・タレ類に小麦を含む通常の醤油が使用されていると考えられる。グルテンフリー醤油（タマリ等）の使用は確認されていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全メニューが魚介類・海産物を中心に構成されており、植物性食材のみの調理は現実的に不可能な環境。もずくたこしゃぶの出汁は魚介ベース。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応に関する情報はウェブサイト・口コミ・グルナビのいずれにも掲載なし。食物アレルギーや食事制限がある場合は事前に電話（052-971-3091）で直接確認することを推奨する。

caveats: フグ料理は10月〜3月のみ提供。ランチのしゃぶしゃぶは提供されず、夜のみ。営業時間・定休日は事前に要確認（祝日休み）。GF・ビーガン対応は確認されていないため、食物制限のある方は訪問前に電話確認必須。

cuisine: Miso-nikomi udon, Kishimen (flat noodles), Nagoya noodle dishes, Donburi
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区数寄屋町8-11
