geocode_note: GSI address search returned exact match for 数寄屋町８番１１号; coordinates are within 550m of Nagoya Castle, well inside the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 西区数寄屋町 (Joshin / Nishi Ward)
seats_text: 42 seats across counter, table, and raised tatami (zashiki) sections; parking for 6 cars on-site.
cultural_comfort_note: A traditional neighborhood noodle shop with no English menu or signage; the atmosphere is warm and the proprietor is described as welcoming, but Japanese is the working language.
vegan_detail: The menu is built around miso-based noodle broths, pork cutlet (miso katsu), chicken dishes, tempura, and fried items. No plant-based or dashi-free options are documented anywhere. Traditional katsuobushi-based or pork-based dashi is standard in this style of cooking; assume nothing is vegan.
gf_detail: The core product is wheat noodles (udon and kishimen) and the restaurant has not documented any gluten-free alternatives. Soy sauce, miso, and wheat noodles are fundamental to every dish on the menu. No dedicated fryer or allergen accommodation is mentioned on any source. This is not a safe choice for anyone with celiac disease or wheat allergy.

bio.specialty: Miso-nikomi kishimen — flat Nagoya-style wheat noodles simmered in a rich, concentrated miso broth served piping hot in an earthenware pot

anecdotes.text: Multiple food writers describe the proprietor as a warm, motherly figure whose welcoming manner is as much a draw as the food itself.

A local food blog singles out the restaurant's proximity to Nagoya Castle and calls the miso-nikomi kishimen an authentic example of the city's flat-noodle tradition — a rarer find than miso-nikomi udon.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: All noodle dishes use wheat-based udon or kishimen noodles; the miso broth almost certainly contains wheat-based miso and standard soy sauce. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Broth bases are traditional Japanese style; katsuobushi (bonito) or pork-derived dashi is standard in miso-nikomi preparations. No plant-based broth option is documented.

caveats: Hours are irregular due to the second-Saturday closure in addition to the standard Friday closure — verify before visiting. Cash-only (PayPay QR accepted). No English menu; no documented accommodation for wheat allergy or vegan diets. The miso-nikomi kishimen is the headline dish but is entirely off-limits for celiac or wheat-intolerant diners.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区新道1-21-17 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode matched 愛知県名古屋市西区新道一丁目２１番１７号 exactly. Distance from Nagoya Castle ~1.23km, within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町駅周辺（名古屋市西区新道）
seats_text: 16 seats — 8 counter, 8 table
cultural_comfort_note: A small Showa-era retro shop where ordering is done in Japanese with no English menu observed; staff are noted as warm and welcoming to solo diners.
gf_detail: Soba is buckwheat-based but standard Japanese soba shops almost universally use wheat-containing soy sauce in their tsuyu dashi broth, and tempura on the menu means wheat flour is present in the kitchen with high cross-contamination risk. No dedicated gluten-free preparation or labeling has been noted in any source. Celiac diners should ask before ordering; safe GF eating here is unlikely without direct confirmation from the kitchen.
vegan_detail: The menu centres on domburi bowls (oyakodon, butadon, tendon) and soba served in fish-based broth. Katsuobushi (bonito) dashi is strongly implied by the classic tsuyu style described by reviewers. No plant-based or vegan options were identified.

bio.specialty: 梅おろしそば (plum daikon soba) and a legendary-value service set — onigiri plus dashimaki tamago for around ¥200 — that regulars order on every visit

anecdotes.text: One reviewer visited 10 times between June 2021 and July 2024, working through the whole menu from tanuki soba to tsukimi soba, calling the broth rustic, slightly sweet, and faintly acidic in the best old-school way.
The Showa-era retro interior and famously energetic, warm hospitality make it a reliable lunch stop for locals near Asamacho Station.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is on the menu, meaning wheat flour is actively used in the kitchen. No source mentions a dedicated fryer or separate prep area for allergen-sensitive orders.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No source confirms wheat-free soy sauce. Standard Japanese tsuyu (the dipping/broth base for soba) contains wheat-based soy sauce; this shop has given no indication it deviates from that norm.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi is implied by the classic broth flavor described by reviewers; pork and chicken bowls are also served, making a fully plant-based meal extremely unlikely.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling information appears in any reviewed source. With no English menu and a small cash-only operation, allergy communication will require Japanese or a translation aid.

positives.text: Soba noodles are buckwheat-based, which is inherently gluten-free before any broth or coating is applied — a useful starting point for a conversation with staff.

caveats: No official website found. Phone number not publicly listed on Tabelog. Hours confirmed from Tabelog (last editor update April 2026) but not verified against an official source — confirm before visiting. All dietary safety assessments are inferred from menu content and standard Japanese soba-shop practices; no allergy menu or staff statement was found.

name_ja: ザ・カワブン・ナゴヤ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2丁目12-30
geocode_note: GSI address-search returned coordinates for 丸の内二丁目; restaurant is 1,018m from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 丸の内・市役所
seats_text: 80席（バンケット・個室含む）。個室は4名～12名対応、平日のみ利用可。
cultural_comfort_note: Operated by Plan Do See, a major hospitality group; staff experienced with international guests and formal dining etiquette; online reservations available in English via TableCheck.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Courses are Italian-style multi-course using animal proteins (meat, seafood, dairy, eggs) as central ingredients. The Kenzo Estate wine partner page notes staff can accommodate guests with food restrictions, but no documented plant-based courses exist. Ask well in advance when booking.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or dedicated protocols confirmed. Italian courses include pasta, bread, and wheat-based sauces. Kitchen is not reported as celiac-aware. Wheat-containing soy sauce unlikely (Italian cuisine), but cross-contamination from pasta cooking is a real risk. Must inquire directly at reservation — no reliable basis for celiac diners to assume safety.

bio.background: THE KAWABUN NAGOYA opened in 2008 on the site of Ryotei Kawabun, documented as Nagoya's oldest traditional restaurant, which served the Owari Tokugawa clan as a purveyor during the Edo period. The four-storey building incorporates Taisho-era (1912–1926) architectural elements — carved wooden transoms, tiled roofs, and stone water basins — alongside a 400-year-old shiitake tree planted when Nagoya Castle was constructed. The venue is operated by Plan Do See Co., Ltd., a Tokyo-based hospitality group.
bio.specialty: Seasonal Italian course dining (tasting courses) with ingredients personally sourced by the chef from producers across Japan, served against a backdrop of traditional Japanese garden views and open kitchen theatre.
bio.philosophy: Honor 400 years of Kawabun's hospitality legacy while pursuing modern Italian cuisine through simple cooking methods that let premium seasonal ingredients speak for themselves.
bio.origin: 名古屋市中区丸の内 — built on the grounds of Ryotei Kawabun, Nagoya's oldest ryotei

anecdotes.text: The original Ryotei Kawabun hosted luminaries including Ito Hirobumi, and a 400-year-old shiitake tree on the grounds is said to have been planted as a landmark during the construction of Nagoya Castle.
The venue was selected for Tabelog Italian EAST 'Tabelog 100' in 2025, recognising it among the top 100 Italian restaurants in eastern Japan.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Italian courses feature fresh pasta and wheat-based preparations; no separate gluten-free cooking line or dedicated equipment has been documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Italian kitchen is unlikely to rely on wheat-containing soy sauce, but dressings, sauces, and desserts may contain wheat; no ingredient list is publicly available.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Courses are built around meat, seafood, and dairy; shared kitchen surfaces and cookware make vegan cross-contact likely without prior arrangement.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The Kenzo Estate partner listing notes the restaurant can accommodate guests with food restrictions and international guests, but no formal allergy protocol is documented publicly.

positives.text: Course-based dining with advance reservations creates an opportunity to communicate dietary needs directly with the kitchen before arrival.

caveats: Two Tabelog listings share this name and address: 23045289 (target; listed as その他/Other, primarily used for wedding receptions and special-event dining, with 80 seats) and 23007484 (the main Italian restaurant listing, 70 seats, Tabelog 100 ranked). Both represent the same physical venue operated by Plan Do See. Hours sourced from HotPepper and official site as of mid-2026; irregular closure days — always call or check online before visiting. Dinner start time moved to 17:00 from July 2026 per official site.

neighborhood: Nishi-ku, Joshin
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区浄心1-4-6
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI address search; coordinates 35.1936, 136.8901 are within the target box and approximately 1,244m from Nagoya Castle — well within the 1.61km radius.
gf_detail: Tokai region's first dedicated gluten-free bento shop. Wheat, barley, rye, and oats are excluded from all ingredients including seasonings and condiments. Gluten-free soy sauce and miso are sourced specifically. Contamination prevention is structural: no grain products are permitted on premises, all kitchen equipment is newly purchased (not leased), and staff uniforms are washed exclusively on-site. The founder (who has a wheat allergy) designed protocols to address even trace-protein reactions.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Standard bento boxes use fish, eggs, and/or meat (grilled mackerel, chicken karaage, chicken nanban, chikuwa). Some vegetable-based sides (kinpira gobo, potato salad, leafy greens) may be plant-based but shared kitchen preparation with animal products. Tabelog notes "vegetarian menu available" — inquire directly for current vegan-safe options.
seats_text: Primarily a takeout counter. Tabelog notes a relaxing space (くつろげる空間あり) but no private rooms; seating count not published. No on-site parking; coin parking nearby.
closed_days: Irregular (不定休) — check Instagram @gf_michinori before visiting
cultural_comfort_note: Listed on Tabelog's English site, accepts major international credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, JCB, AMEX), and offers delivery via Uber Eats and Wolt — accessible for non-Japanese speakers, though phone and in-person reservations are in Japanese.

bio.background: Naco developed a wheat allergy as an adult and experienced first-hand the isolation of being unable to share meals with friends and family. Determined that no one should have to give up the pleasure of eating together, she trained as a gluten-free advisor and founded 株式会社テマトジカン (Tematojikan Co., Ltd.). みちのり弁当 opened January 27, 2023, in a lovingly repurposed 60-year-old wooden building in the Joshin neighborhood — chosen to reduce demolition waste, with reclaimed wood and a transparent glass front that lets passersby see into the kitchen.
bio.specialty: Rice-flour karaage bento — crispy chicken fried in rice flour batter, entirely wheat-free from coating to seasoning
bio.philosophy: 「小麦アレルギーの人も、そうでない人も、一緒に食卓を囲み同じ食事で『おいしいね』を分かち合う」— People with and without wheat allergies can sit at the same table and share the joy of 'this is delicious.'

anecdotes.text: Rather than lease kitchen equipment that might have contacted gluten, Naco purchased every piece of equipment new — a significant upfront cost that she absorbed to guarantee contamination-free preparation from day one.

The shop building itself is a sustainability statement: a 60-year-old wooden structure was renovated instead of demolished, with reclaimed wood used for interior fittings and glass walls installed so the kitchen is fully visible to customers.

みちのり弁当 was the first dedicated gluten-free bento shop in the entire Tokai region when it opened in January 2023, filling a gap Naco had experienced personally as a wheat-allergy sufferer living in Nagoya.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No grain products (wheat, barley, rye, oats) are permitted to enter the premises at all, eliminating the primary cross-contamination vector.
All kitchen equipment was purchased new rather than leased, ensuring no residual gluten from prior use.
Staff uniforms are washed exclusively on-site to prevent outside gluten introduction.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Gluten-free soy sauce and miso are specifically sourced; standard wheat-containing soy sauce and miso are not used in any preparation.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen is not vegan-dedicated. Bento boxes regularly contain fish, chicken, and eggs; plant-based sides are prepared in the same kitchen environment.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The shop advises contacting them directly for allergen accommodation requests beyond gluten: 「食物アレルギー対応につきましてはお問い合わせください」(Please inquire about food allergy accommodations).
Protocols account for even trace-protein quantities sufficient to trigger allergic reactions, reflecting the founder's personal experience with wheat allergy.

positives.text: Tokai region's first dedicated gluten-free bento shop — the entire menu is safe for wheat allergy; no need to ask or modify orders.
The founder is herself a wheat-allergy sufferer and certified gluten-free advisor, bringing lived-experience credibility to every safety decision.

caveats: Primarily a takeout bento counter, not a sit-down restaurant — plan to take food away. Closed days are irregular; check Instagram (@gf_michinori) before visiting. Vegan status requires direct inquiry as most standard bentos contain fish or meat. Delivery available via Uber Eats, Demae-can, and Wolt.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区城北町3丁目122-1 ハートイン城北町1C
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区城北町三丁目122番地; 1,591m from Nagoya Castle target center — within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 西区城北町（庄内通駅徒歩約11分）
seats_text: 座席数は公表情報なし。ハートイン城北町の1階テナント（小規模スペース）。
cultural_comfort_note: スリランカ人オーナーの個人経営店で英語対応の可能性あり。日本語が基本だが、身振りや指差しでの注文も通じると思われる。
vegan_detail: メニューにベジタブルカレーが存在し、スリランカ料理はもともと豆・野菜ベースのカレーが豊富。ただしヴィーガン対応を明示した情報はなく、魚・鶏・豚カレーも提供。来店前に個別確認が必要。
gf_detail: スリランカ料理はライスベースが基本でグルテンを含む食材が少ない傾向にあるが、醤油・小麦含有調味料の使用有無は不明。グルテンフリー対応の明示はなく、パパダム（豆粉製）は通常GF対応だが揚げ油の共用リスクは確認不可。セリアック等の方は必ず事前に電話（070-3810-4231）で確認を。

bio.background: 2026年6月21日オープン。スリランカ出身のオーナーが名古屋市西区城北町の目立たない路地裏（ハートイン城北町1C）で営む一軒。相場の半値ほどという圧倒的なコストパフォーマンスと、本場の味がそのまま皿に上る「ガチなスリランカ料理」として注目を集め始めている。
bio.specialty: フィッシュカレー・チキンカレー・ベジタブルカレーなど本格スリランカカレー、ファルーダ（デザート）
bio.philosophy: 本場の味をそのままに、手頃な価格で提供することをコンセプトとしている模様。

anecdotes.text: 「通りから奥に入ったかなり目立たないところ」にあるにもかかわらず、食べログレビュアーが「相場の半値でガチなスリランカ料理」と評し、高い価格評価（CP4.0）を付けた。

positives.text: スリランカ料理はライス（米）を主食とし、豆・野菜・スパイスが主体のカレーが多いため、グルテンフリーに親和性がある料理文化といえる。

caveats: 2026年6月21日オープンの非常に新しい店舗で、食べログレビューは1件のみ。公式サイト（goope.jp/wok）は同名の東京・武蔵村山の中華料理店と混同の恐れがあり、当店固有の公式Webページは未確認。ヴィーガン・グルテンフリー対応の詳細は要電話確認。

name_ja: 炭焼 うな富士 白壁別邸
neighborhood: 東区芳野（白壁・尼ケ坂エリア）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区芳野1-10-21
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned lng 136.921387, lat 35.184898 for 東区芳野1-10-21. Distance from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 1.98 km — outside the 1.61 km target radius. The restaurant is a confirmed operating business but sits east of the zone boundary.
vegan_detail: Eel specialty restaurant. All signature dishes are built around unagi (eel). Tare (sauce) uses soy sauce; clam soup and rice preparations contain dashi. No plant-based or vegan menu options identified. Not suitable for vegans.
gf_detail: Eel tare (glaze) is made with soy sauce, which in Japan almost certainly contains wheat. Dashi (broth used in clam soup and potentially rice preparation) may contain additional allergen risk. No gluten-free menu, dedicated fryer information, or allergy protocol published on the official site or Tabelog. Celiac diners should call ahead (052-325-4788) and treat this as high-risk — wheat exposure through tare is near-certain.
seats_text: 94席。個室あり（3名以上）。掘りごたつ・座敷・テーブル席。
cultural_comfort_note: Traditional upscale Japanese eel restaurant in a registered historic building; English menus are not confirmed and staff are likely Japanese-speaking only — basic Japanese or translation app recommended.

bio.background: 炭焼 うな富士は1995年、名古屋生まれの創業者が養殖飼料の研究開発職を辞し、50歳で独自のタレを引っ提げて昭和区山王通に開いたうなぎ専門店。開業からわずか25年でミシュランガイド掲載・食べログ百名店入りを果たした。白壁別邸は2021年7月24日に東区芳野にオープンした別邸で、昭和27年築・名古屋市登録地域建造物資産に指定された木造和風住宅を改装した空間に店を構える。増改築を経た庭と錦鯉の泳ぐ中庭が特徴的な、旅館のような佇まい。
bio.origin: 名古屋市昭和区（本店）→ 東区芳野（白壁別邸、2021年開業）
bio.specialty: ひつまぶし・うな丼。希少な「青うなぎ」（通常より30%以上大きい）を1000度超の炭火で焼き上げ、外はカリッと中はふわっとろの食感を生み出す。創業者開発の自家製タレが味の核。
bio.philosophy: 徹底したこだわりの素材と職人技で、日常を特別な一瞬に変える。創業者の豪快な人柄と鰻への熱い思いを別邸にも継承。

anecdotes.text: 創業者は養殖飼料の研究開発に携わるなかで独自のタレを開発し、50歳で脱サラしてうなぎ専門店を開業。異色のキャリアから生まれた一軒が、25年でミシュラン掲載店になった。
白壁別邸の建物は昭和27年築の木造和風住宅で、名古屋市の「登録地域建造物資産」（第85号）に認定された歴史的建造物。錦鯉の泳ぐ中庭と座敷が、旅館さながらの空間を演出する。
「うなぎの枕®」は水産庁長官賞を受賞した自社開発ブランド商品で、不定期販売のたびに注目を集める。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No cross-contamination protocols or dedicated preparation areas are disclosed. The kitchen handles eel glazed with wheat-containing soy sauce tare throughout service.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Eel tare (glaze) is made with soy sauce. Standard Japanese soy sauce (醤油) contains wheat. All grilled eel dishes are coated in this tare, making wheat exposure near-certain for celiac diners.
Dashi-based broth is used in the clam soup (しじみ汁) served alongside hitsumabushi sets. Dashi ingredients and soy sauce content not disclosed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All dishes are eel-based; no plant-based options exist. Animal-derived dashi and eel fat present throughout the kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy inquiry form, or staff allergy-handling protocol is published on the official website or Tabelog page. Guests with serious allergies should call 052-325-4788 before visiting.

positives.text: Upscale restaurant where advance reservations are the norm — calling ahead to discuss dietary needs (アレルギー対応の確認) is standard practice and likely to be accommodated with notice.

caveats: OUTSIDE TARGET RADIUS: The restaurant geocodes to lat 35.184898, lng 136.921387 (東区芳野1-10-21), approximately 1.98 km from Nagoya Castle — outside the 1.61 km constraint. The Tabelog area tag says A230104 (白壁 subarea), which is east of the Castle zone. The listing is real and operating but does not qualify geographically for the target area. — Hours listed are the standard pattern; irregular closures (不定休) mean you should check before visiting. — Seat count of 94 is from Tabelog; some sources cite 98. — No English menu confirmed; Japanese ability or translation app recommended.

name_ja: ヴェジタブルカフェ&シーフードバー サイエン
cuisine: オーガニック野菜・シーフード・牡蠣・ダイニングバー
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区二の丸1-2 金シャチ横丁 宗春ゾーン
geocode_note: GSI geocoder returned coordinates for 名古屋市中区二の丸1番地 (Ninomaru 1-chome). Approximately 0.45 km from Nagoya Castle center — well within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 市役所駅・中区二の丸（名古屋城東側金シャチ横丁宗春ゾーン）
vegan_detail: The menu is vegetable-forward at lunch with NYC-style chopped salads (romaine, red cabbage, spinach with dressing choices including soy-based and sesame-ginger), but the restaurant's core identity is an omnivore dining bar. Evening focus is on raw oysters, wagyu beef, and seafood. No dishes are labeled vegan. Soy-based dressings are on offer alongside other options. Hidden animal-based ingredients (bonito dashi, oyster sauce, etc.) in cooked items cannot be ruled out — ask staff before ordering.
gf_detail: No gluten-free labeling or certification found on any platform. Menu includes soy-based dressings (standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat), burgers with buns, grain rice bowls, fried items (soft-shell crab burger, fried shrimp), and dessert shortcakes — all high-risk for gluten. Shared kitchen environment with fried items means cross-contamination risk. No dedicated fryer information available. Those with celiac disease or serious gluten sensitivity should inquire carefully with staff before ordering; safe options are not guaranteed.
seats_text: 50席（テーブル席・ソファ席・カウンター席・テラス席）
cultural_comfort_note: Located inside the tourist-oriented Kinshachi Yokocho complex at Nagoya Castle — an English-friendly environment designed to welcome international visitors, with multilingual signage common in the area.

bio.background: Saien opened on March 29, 2018, as part of the Soharu Zone of Kinshachi Yokocho, a food-and-culture complex built along the eastern edge of Nagoya Castle. The Soharu Zone was conceived to showcase ambitious, creative restaurateurs bringing fresh ideas to Nagoya, and Saien was one of its founding tenants.
bio.specialty: Soft-shell crab burger — a whole soft-shell crab tucked between buns — is the venue's most talked-about item and a recurring centerpiece of visitor photos and reviews.
bio.philosophy: The restaurant's founding concept centered on bringing New York-style salad culture to Nagoya: self-cultivated organic vegetables paired with daily market-sourced seafood, aiming for cuisine described as 'beautiful, delicious, and body-friendly.' Over time the concept evolved into a broader dining bar with a strong oyster and wagyu focus in the evenings while maintaining the fresh-vegetable-and-seafood identity at lunch.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's signature 'five-minute shelf-life' seasonal shortcake — a dessert intentionally marketed as perishable to signal ultra-fresh cream — became a social media talking point among Kinshachi Yokocho visitors.
The soft-shell crab burger, priced around ¥980, features a whole crab and drew consistent praise in visitor blogs as an unusual and photogenic item not found at typical Nagoya spots.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Fried items (soft-shell crab burger, fried shrimp, miso cutlet) are on the menu and likely share kitchen fryers with other dishes. No dedicated GF fryer information is publicly available.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy-based dressings are offered on salads, and soy sauce is a standard ingredient in Japanese cuisine — standard Japanese soy sauce (醤油) contains wheat. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives are mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen prepares raw oysters, wagyu beef, seafood, and fried items alongside vegetable dishes. No separation between vegan and omnivore prep is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling protocol is mentioned on any official or third-party platform. Phone reservations are accepted at 052-212-8553, which would be the best avenue to inquire about specific dietary needs before visiting.

positives.text: The restaurant's concept emphasizes self-cultivated organic vegetables and daily fresh market seafood, suggesting ingredient quality awareness even if formal allergy protocols are not advertised.

caveats: Hours conflict across sources: Tabelog shows Mon-Fri 17:00-24:00 / Sat-Sun 15:00-24:00 (dinner-only), while HotPepper and kinshachi-yokocho.com show 10:30-22:30 (all-day). The all-day hours appear more current given the lunch-cafe positioning and tourist complex context. Confirm before visiting. The restaurant appears to have evolved from "vegetable cafe" to a broader "oyster and meat" izakaya concept over time — the vegetable-forward branding remains but the evening menu is now meat and seafood heavy. The official site (nagoyajo-saien.com) returned a TLS certificate error and could not be fetched.

cuisine: Kissaten (Japanese coffee shop); Nagoya comfort food; griddle dishes
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1丁目36-52
neighborhood: 那古野・四間道 (Nagono / Shikemichi historic district), Nishi-ku — ~5-min walk from Marunouchi Station (Exit 8)
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch to lat 35.175735, lng 136.892517. Approximately 1,275m from Nagoya Castle reference point (35.1856, 136.8997) — within the 1.61km radius. Address ward is Nishi-ku (西区), which abuts the Chuo-ku/Marunouchi area across the Horikawa river; the Shikemichi district is the neighborhood linking these zones.
seats_text: 34 seats across two floors: counter and sofa seating on the ground floor, loft tables with red sofas on the second floor.
hours_raw: 月～水・日 8:00～18:00、金・土 8:00～22:00、木曜定休（第2水曜も休みの場合あり）
closed_days: Thursdays; second Wednesday of each month (confirm in advance)
cultural_comfort_note: A beloved local kissaten in a historic district — menus are in Japanese with no English translation; staff may have limited English, but the ordering process is simple and the atmosphere is warm and welcoming.
vegan_detail: The menu is a classic Nagoya kissaten lineup: toast with red bean paste and butter, teppan spaghetti (Napolitan), curry rice, and morning sets with egg and salad. No vegan-designated dishes exist. Hidden animal products are the norm here — butter in toast, egg in morning sets, likely milk in coffee drinks. Coffee drinks (black) would be the only reliably vegan option. Ask (in Japanese) if a specific item can be modified.
gf_detail: The entire menu is built around wheat: thick-cut toast, spaghetti, and bread rolls are the core offerings. No gluten-free alternatives exist. Soy sauce likely appears in the Napolitan and other savory dishes. No dedicated fryer information available, but there is no indication of fryer use. This venue is not appropriate for celiac diners.

bio.background: New Poppy is the second-generation revival of 喫茶ポピー, the coffee house Masashi Bito's parents opened two years before his birth and ran in the Meieki district from 1977 until December 2010, when building deterioration forced its closure after 34 years. Masashi worked alongside his parents during the shop's final three years, catching the coffee bug. He went on to establish BEANS BITOU, a specialty roasting and wholesale company, and ran 喫茶神戸館 in Fushimi before opening 喫茶ニューポピー in January 2019 in the historic Shikemichi preservation district of Nagono — a neighborhood with deep personal resonance: his great-grandfather once lived near Gojo Bridge there, and his father is buried in the area. His mother Takako (尾藤孝子) works alongside him, making this a literal mother-and-son continuation of a family story.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi — historic Shikemichi (四間道) district, Nishi-ku
bio.specialty: Teppan Ogura Toast (鉄板小倉トースト) — thick-cut bread delivered on a sizzling iron plate, spread with black sesame paste and sweet red bean, crowned with melting vanilla ice cream. House-roasted Poppy Blend and New Poppy Blend coffees are also a draw.
bio.philosophy: Masashi chose the word 'New' deliberately: to inherit tradition while creating something genuinely new. He wanted a space where people could sit without urgency — his study of kissaten across Japan convinced him that a shop's history is its most irreplaceable asset. He offers 'Story Coffee' (物語珈琲), custom wedding-favor blends built from couples' personal narratives.
roles: Owner / roaster — 尾藤雅士 (Masashi Bito)
Floor staff — 尾藤孝子 (Takako Bito), Masashi's mother

anecdotes.text: Masashi's great-grandfather once lived near Gojo Bridge in Nagono, and his father is buried in the same neighborhood — connections he describes as feeling 'invited' to bring the family's kissaten back to this exact block of the city.

The original 喫茶ポピー opened in 1977, two years before Masashi was born; he grew up in and around the shop, then watched his mother run it alone after his father's early death before taking over himself at age 28.

New Poppy's Masu Presso collaboration pairs direct-fire-roasted coffee with 枡 (traditional wooden measuring boxes) from Ohashi Ryoki, a century-old Ogaki craftsman — described by the shop as 'the world's first marriage' of the two.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat is the central ingredient across the entire menu — thick-cut toast, spaghetti, and bread rolls are the core offerings. No source mentions gluten-free soy sauce or wheat-free alternatives.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Morning set includes boiled egg and toast with butter; coffee drinks are served with milk by default. No plant-based milk alternative is mentioned in any source.

caveats: Address is Nishi-ku (西区那古野), not Chuo-ku — the Shikemichi district straddles the ward boundary near Marunouchi Station, which is why it is commonly described as a Marunouchi-area spot. Hours sources conflict slightly (some list 2nd Wednesday as a closure, some do not); confirm before visiting. Cash and PayPay only — no credit cards. No reservations accepted; weekend waits can exceed 2 hours.

cuisine: Ramen (shoyu / shio; pork-bone and seafood broth)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区新道2-1-18
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市西区新道2-1-18 returned [136.888672, 35.179993]; approximately 1.16 km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町（西区）
seats_text: Counter seating only, 14 seats
closed_days: Sundays; third Monday of each month
cultural_comfort_note: A tiny 14-seat counter shop with no English signage or staff language noted; ordering is straightforward (point at menu items), but expect a fully Japanese-language environment.
vegan_detail: Both ramen varieties use a pork-bone and seafood-blended broth; the signature chashu pork is the headline topping. No plant-based broth option exists. Vegan diners have nothing suitable here.
gf_detail: Standard ramen shop: the shoyu (soy sauce) broth contains wheat via conventional soy sauce. Noodles are wheat-based. No tamari or GF alternatives noted anywhere. No allergen menu, no staff allergy-handling protocol found. This place is not safe for celiac or serious wheat-sensitive diners.

bio.background: Opened on January 8, 2018 in a quiet residential side street a short walk from Asama-cho Station. The space is styled like a cozy hideout — warm wooden counter, intimate atmosphere — and has built a loyal neighbourhood following since opening.
bio.specialty: Low-temperature slow-cooked chashu pork, praised across reviews as 'melt-in-your-mouth.' Offered in both shoyu (soy sauce) and shio (salt) broths built on a pork-bone and seafood base.
bio.philosophy: A stripped-back, solo-diner-friendly spot that keeps the menu tight and the quality high.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers consistently single out the chashu as the star of the bowl, with one describing it as 'exquisite, melt-in-your-mouth' — the result of precise low-temperature cooking.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Standard ramen kitchen with no GF protocols noted; wheat noodles cooked in shared water, wheat-based soy sauce used in broth.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Shoyu (soy sauce) variety uses conventional soy sauce which contains wheat. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative mentioned on any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Entire menu is built around pork-bone and seafood broth; no plant-based option exists, so cross-contact is total and unavoidable.

caveats: No official website found. Hours and closed days sourced from Tabelog; verify directly before visiting as small independent shops sometimes change hours without notice. PayPay QR accepted but no credit cards — bring cash. Seat count of 14 from Ramen Walker listing.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区上竪杉町25
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned coordinates for 愛知県名古屋市東区上竪杉町２５番地; 1.207km from Nagoya Castle, within 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 東区上竪杉町（白壁地区）
seats_text: 30席。2階に個室あり（要予約）。
cultural_comfort_note: 高級古民家イタリアン。日本語主体の環境だが、コース料理の流れは国際的で、英語対応の可否は不明。予約時に英語でのコミュニケーションは確認が必要。
vegan_detail: 完全なヴィーガンメニューは設定されていない。コースは魚介・肉類が主体で、バター・チーズ・生クリームを多用するイタリア料理。契約農家の無農薬野菜を豊富に使用しており、予約時にリクエストすれば対応を検討してもらえる可能性はあるが（「苦手な食材があれば申し付けるよう」との案内あり）、ヴィーガン対応を明示的に保証する情報はない。
gf_detail: グルテンフリー対応の明示はなし。コースにはパスタ（小麦粉）が含まれており、タリアテッレ等が定番メニューに登場する。醤油使用の記載はないがイタリアンベースなので醤油・味噌は少ないと思われるが確認が必要。パン・パスタを除いた対応については予約時に直接相談することを強く推奨。セリアック病の方は専用厨房・グルテンフリー保証がないため注意が必要。

bio.background: 明治時代（約130〜140年前）に建てられた古民家を改装した隠れ家イタリアン。名古屋・東区の白壁地区に位置し、アンティークな調度品と年代物の建築が醸し出す「時間が止まったような」空間が特徴。オーナーが北イタリアのレストランで歴史的建築とモダンな料理が融合するスタイルに触れ、名古屋でも同様のコンセプトを実現した。
bio.origin: 名古屋市東区上竪杉町（白壁地区）
bio.specialty: 旬の食材を活かしたイタリアンコース料理。ジビエ（若鳩・山鴫・鶉・キジ・鴨）、三河一色産のハモ、五島列島のアワビ、国産黒毛和牛、日本のタイガープロウンなど、日本の旬食材をイタリアン技法で仕上げる。
bio.philosophy: 「サービス、料理、空間のすべてに幸せを感じてもらいたい」（支配人・尾関慎二氏）。日本ならではの旬の素材に徹底的にこだわり、契約農家から届く無農薬野菜、厳選された国産食材を四季に合わせて使用。イタリアワインはソムリエが選んだ60種以上を用意。

anecdotes.text: 建物は明治時代築約130〜140年の古民家を改装。ワインセラーも改装した内装に組み込まれており、「時間が止まったような」雰囲気と評されている。
二階には1日1組限定で利用できる個室があり、プロポーズや記念日のシーンに利用されることで知られる。
支配人の尾関慎二氏は「お客様が何を好み、何が苦手かを事前に把握し、おいしく食べていただけるように工夫します」と語っており、コース内容の柔軟な対応を示唆している。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテンフリー専用の調理環境・フライヤーについての情報はなし。コースにパスタ（タリアテッレ等）が含まれるため厨房での小麦粉の使用は常態的と考えられる。セリアック病レベルの対応確認は予約時に直接必要。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: メニューに醤油の記載なし。イタリアン主体の調理のため醤油・味噌の使用は限定的と推測されるが、和食材（ハモ、和牛、ウニ等）使用時の調味料については確認が必要。

vegan_cross_contact.text: バター・チーズ・生クリームが標準的に使用されるイタリアンコース構成。動物性食材の排除については予約時のリクエストが必要。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 予約時にアレルギーや苦手な食材をコメント欄で伝えるよう案内されており、スタッフは対応を検討してくれる姿勢がある（「おいしく食べていただけるように工夫します」）。ただし書面によるアレルゲン対応保証の情報はない。

positives.text: 契約農家からの無農薬野菜を多用しており、野菜の質と鮮度へのこだわりは高い。コース内に豊富な野菜料理が含まれる。

caveats: Chef name differs slightly between sources: the official site menu lists "Menu Chef Okada" (岡田シェフ) while the about page mentions "Hayashi Satoshi (林 哲)" — these may be different staff roles or a change over time; treat chef name as uncertain. Building age is cited as 100, 120, 130, and 140 years old across different sources; "approximately 130 years old Meiji-era" is the most commonly cited figure. The otona-no-nagoya.com article mentions a French-trained head chef specializing in game (gibier), which aligns with the game-heavy menu, but no name is confirmed. GF and vegan accommodations have no explicit policy — all dietary assessment is based on menu composition and a general statement about accommodating guest preferences.

name_ja: 鳥開総本家 金シャチ横丁店
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-2-5 金シャチ横丁 義直ゾーン
geocode_note: GSI geocoded to 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸一丁目２番; ~378m south-southwest of Nagoya Castle, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区・名古屋城 (Nakaku, Nagoya Castle area — Kinshachi Yokocho, Gishoku Zone)
cuisine: 鶏料理・親子丼・手羽先 (Chicken dishes — Nagoya Cochin oyakodon, tebasaki fried wings)
vegan_detail: Entirely a chicken-specialist restaurant. Every dish centres on Nagoya Cochin or domestic chicken. The dashi base is made from Kyushu bonito flakes (katsuo), and the signature oyakodon uses chicken and egg. No plant-based or vegan options are offered or documented anywhere on the official site or third-party sources.
gf_detail: The restaurant uses standard wheat-containing soy sauce in both the tebasaki wing sauce and the oyakodon tare. The tebasaki is deep-fried (battered/coated) — wheat is present in the breading. The oyakodon dashi contains bonito; the soy-based sauce is not tamari. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen-substitution program are documented anywhere. Celiac-unsafe; avoid.
seats_text: 28 seats: 4 counter seats and 24 table seats across a compact dining room inside Kinshachi Yokocho's Gishoku (Yoshinao) Zone.
hours_raw: 10:30–17:30 (last order 17:00) daily; closed on days Nagoya Castle is closed (irregular)
closed_days: Irregular — follows Nagoya Castle's operating calendar
cultural_comfort_note: Located inside a purpose-built tourist precinct at Nagoya Castle, this shop is well-accustomed to international visitors; signage and menus are available in multiple languages, and staff at Kinshachi Yokocho venues regularly handle non-Japanese guests.
caveats: Hours are daytime only (closes 17:30) and follow Nagoya Castle's irregular closure calendar — check before visiting. Seat count varies slightly across sources (24–28); 28 used from Tabelog. The chain operates 16+ locations across Japan plus international franchises — this is a well-branded chain, not a mom-and-pop spot.

bio.background: Nishimura Kazunori grew up in the Ise-Shima fishing community with a fisherman father. Despite those coastal roots, it was chicken — specifically the joy of biting into wings at childhood Christmas dinners — that became his culinary north star. In 2000 he opened a small 10-tsubo izakaya called Torikai at Nagoya Station's west exit. The turning point came at the 2005 Aichi Expo, where he ran a booth selling both branded Nagoya Cochin and unbranded chicken side by side: despite a price difference of nearly 10x, diners overwhelmingly chose the Nagoya Cochin. That moment convinced him that quality provenance could command loyalty. In 2007 he incorporated as Progre Corp. and rebranded the operation as Torikai Sohonke, pivoting the menu to centre on oyakodon and tebasaki made exclusively with Nagoya Cochin. The Kinshachi Yokocho branch, opened inside the newly developed tourism precinct at Nagoya Castle's main gate, carries that flagship identity into one of the city's most historically resonant settings.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi — founded at Nagoya Station West Exit 2000; Kinshachi Yokocho branch opened in the Gishoku (Yoshinao) Zone of the castle precinct.
bio.specialty: 特撰名古屋コーチン親子丼 (premium Nagoya Cochin oyakodon) and 名古屋コーチン手羽先唐揚げ (Nagoya Cochin tebasaki fried wings)
bio.philosophy: 鳥開総本家's guiding principle is: 'a chicken specialist shop that has consistently prioritized authenticity since its founding' (創業当時から本物にこだわり続けた鶏専門店). This means sourcing only Nagoya Cochin raised for 120+ days — more than double the broiler standard — to achieve the breed's characteristic firm texture and depth of flavour. The wing sauce is a proprietary tare replenished gradually in its original pot, so that each new batch absorbs the complexity of every batch before it.

anecdotes.text: At the 2005 Aichi World Expo, Nishimura sold Nagoya Cochin and unbranded chicken from the same booth. The Nagoya Cochin — priced nearly 10 times higher — outsold the generic product decisively. The experience convinced him to rebuild the brand entirely around premium provenance.

Nishimura traces the oyakodon's slightly sweet, egg-rich flavour profile to a childhood memory: eating sukiyaki leftovers the morning after, stirring the sweet broth into rice and raw egg. That combination became the emotional blueprint for the restaurant's signature dish.

The tebasaki wing sauce is kept in a traditional tare pot that is never fully emptied — new sauce is added to the residue of previous batches, building cumulative depth. The restaurant has won the Japan Karaage Association Grand Prix (tebasaki division) three consecutive years at the highest gold level, and nine gold awards at the National Donburi Grand Prix (oyakodon division).

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tebasaki (fried chicken wings) are coated and deep-fried. No dedicated fryer for any allergen-free preparation is documented. Cross-contamination with wheat from frying is a realistic risk.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The cooking philosophy page confirms the use of 'Central Japan soy sauce marinade' (中日本しょうゆダレ) in the karaage and wing preparations. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat; no tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere on the official site or third-party sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built on chicken and chicken-derived ingredients (egg, bonito dashi). There is no plant-based preparation at this restaurant; vegan cross-contact is unavoidable throughout.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen declaration, staff allergy protocol, or dietary accommodation program is documented on the official site, Tabelog listing, Kinshachi Yokocho operator page, or any third-party source reviewed. Diners with serious allergies should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

name_ja: 山本屋総本家 金シャチ横丁店
cuisine: 味噌煮込みうどん・きしめん
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-2-4 金シャチ横丁 義直ゾーン
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 中区三の丸一丁目２番returned [136.89917, 35.182224]; within 0.4 km of Nagoya Castle target point.
neighborhood: 中区三の丸、名古屋城駅直近（金シャチ横丁義直ゾーン）
closed_days: 火曜日（祝日の場合は翌日）
seats_text: 40席（テーブル20席、掘りごたつ16席、カウンター4席）
cultural_comfort_note: Tourist-area location adjacent to Nagoya Castle with international visitors in mind, but menus and staff interaction are primarily in Japanese; some English menus available given the Kinshachi Yokocho tourist setting.
vegan_detail: All udon and kishimen dishes are simmered in a broth made from katsuobushi (bonito), mackerel, sardine, kombu, and shiitake. The miso base blends Kakukyu Hacho miso with white miso and uses soy sauce; no plant-based broth option exists. Side dishes include beef tendon, oden with fish products, and chicken. There is no vegan pathway on this menu.
gf_detail: Noodles are made from 100% domestic wheat flour — wheat is the central ingredient in every dish. The dashi and miso tare both contain soy sauce (wheat). Buckwheat flour is used as dusting powder on the noodles, adding a soba cross-contact risk on top of direct gluten. No gluten-free noodle substitution is offered. This restaurant is not suitable for anyone avoiding gluten.

bio.background: 山本屋総本家は大正14年（1925年）に創業し、2025年に創業100周年を迎えた名古屋を代表する味噌煮込みうどんの老舗。現在は町田福之氏が代表を務め、両親から受け継いだ暖簾を守る。
bio.specialty: 味噌煮込みうどん：愛知県岡崎産カクキューの八丁味噌と白味噌をブレンドしたコクのある味噌だしに、国産小麦100%の生麺を土鍋のままグツグツと煮込む。蓋を取り皿として使う独自の食べ方も創業当時から変わらない。
bio.philosophy: 「毎年、小麦の品質も味噌の品質も変わる。それでも変わらぬ味を守るため、毎年ブレンドや配合を調整し続けている」（町田福之代表）。創業時の味と食べ方をそのまま守り続けることを最優先としている。
roles: 老舗チェーン（名古屋市内5店舗）
名古屋めし観光スポット代表格
金シャチ横丁の主要テナント

anecdotes.text: 麺には「コシ・太さ・鮮度・打ち粉・素材」の五つのこだわりがあり、毎日製造した生麺のみを使用。冷蔵保存は一切行わない。
名古屋城の金シャチにちなんで考案した「金シャチ煮込うどん」（¥2,420）は、エビの天ぷらを金シャチに見立てたこの店舗限定の一品。
麺の打ち粉にそば粉を使用しており、そばアレルギーの顧客からの問い合わせを受けて成分表示を明示している。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Noodles are made entirely from wheat flour; wheat is inseparable from the dish. No gluten-free alternative exists.
Buckwheat (soba) flour is used as dusting powder on all udon noodles, creating an additional cross-contact risk for both gluten-sensitive and soba-allergic diners.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The miso tare contains soy sauce (shoyu), which contains wheat. Allergen labeling for Yamamotoya products explicitly lists wheat (小麦) as a declared allergen across noodles, miso blend, and broth components.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Broth is made from katsuobushi (bonito), mackerel, mackerel pike, and sardine. The miso blend contains bonito and mackerel. No plant-only broth option is available.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information found about staff allergy handling procedures at the restaurant. Given the allergen complexity (wheat in noodles and broth, soba dusting powder, multiple fish), diners with serious allergies should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

caveats: Hours confirmed as 10:30-17:30 from official Kinshachi Yokocho site and Nagoya City tourism page; Tabelog and Gurunavi show slightly different hours (some list until 21:00) — the official Kinshachi Yokocho listing (10:30-17:30, L.O. 17:00) is most authoritative for this specific branch. The restaurant is a chain (not independent) with 4-5 Nagoya locations. Founding year appears as both 1925 and 1926 in different sources; 1925 (Taisho 14) is used by the official brand and the centenary celebration.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区城西4-1-32
geocode_note: GSI address-search matched 愛知県名古屋市西区城西四丁目１番３２号; approx 621m from Nagoya Castle target point, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 浄心駅 徒歩8分（西区城西）
seats_text: 22席（カウンター席あり、2名個室あり）
closed_days: 水曜・木曜（第1・第3木曜）
cultural_comfort_note: Casual neighborhood Italian café in a residential area; menus and signage are in Japanese, but the approachable shokudo format means pointing at daily specials boards works fine.
vegan_detail: The menu centers on pasta sets with soup, salad, focaccia, and quiche — all components likely contain dairy or egg. No vegan-specific labeling or menu found. Diner should ask about individual dishes; some oil-based pasta may be adaptable.
gf_detail: This is a pasta-focused Italian restaurant; wheat pasta is the core product. No gluten-free pasta option or GF labeling has been identified. Cross-contamination risk is high in a pasta kitchen. Celiacs should not assume safety here and would need to call ahead (052-325-5615) to ask directly.
caveats: Closed days are irregular (all Wednesdays plus the 1st and 3rd Thursdays each month — confirm before visiting). No dedicated GF or vegan options confirmed; this is a pasta restaurant where wheat is central. Hours and menu rotate daily; check Instagram @23sourire for current specials.

bio.background: sourire食堂 opened in December 2023 at a corner spot in the residential Johsai neighborhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward, taking over a space that previously housed the izakaya Kushiyoshi. The new kitchen kept alive the pasta that regulars had loved at the former occupant, reframing it within a relaxed shokudo format.
bio.specialty: Daily-rotating pasta sets — generous portions of seasonal pasta (tomato, oil-based with ingredients like firefly squid) accompanied by salad, soup, and focaccia bread.

anecdotes.text: A diner who visited early on noted the interior was 'シャレオツやん' (quite sophisticated/chic) and rated the value as '超抜群' (outstanding), saying both pasta dishes came with abundant fresh ingredients at a very reasonable price.

The restaurant shifts character across the day — opening as a morning café from 7:30 AM, pivoting to a lunch set spot by midday, then transforming into an Italian bar-style dinner venue in the evening.

gf_cross_contamination.text: This is a pasta-specialist kitchen; wheat flour is the primary ingredient in virtually every dish. Cross-contamination risk for celiac diners is high and has not been addressed in any public source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pasta sets include soup, bread (focaccia), and quiche, all of which likely contain dairy or egg. No plant-based alternatives have been noted in any review or listing.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-16-11 パークマンション 102
geocode_note: GSI address-search geocoded to 丸の内三丁目16番11号; 1,244m from Nagoya Castle, within the 1.61km target radius; coordinates fall within target bounding box.
neighborhood: 丸の内 / 久屋大通
seats_text: 38席。座敷あり、貸切24〜36名対応。
cultural_comfort_note: A classic salaryman izakaya with a Showa-era feel — predominantly Japanese-speaking clientele, no English menus, and a lived-in neighbourhood-pub atmosphere where regulars are at home.
vegan_detail: The entire menu revolves around chicken, beef, seafood, and pork. No plant-based dishes are listed, and no vegan accommodation is mentioned on any platform. Dashi and animal stocks are standard in izakaya cooking; this place is no exception.
gf_detail: Gluten is pervasive: all lunch sets feature deep-fried items in wheat breading (karaage, tonkatsu, fry sets), conventional wheat-containing soy sauce is used throughout, and there is no dedicated fryer or GF menu. This is not a safe venue for anyone with celiac disease or serious wheat allergy.

bio.specialty: 丸鳥の丸焼き (whole roasted chicken)

anecdotes.text: Reviewers describe the interior as having a deeply nostalgic Showa-era atmosphere — the kind of izakaya that feels like it hasn't changed in decades, drawing salary workers and neighbourhood regulars who treat it as their go-to local spot.
The whole roasted chicken (丸鳥の丸焼き) at ¥1,680 is the crowd-pleaser, and draft beer at ¥390 keeps the cost-performance ratio firmly in regulars' favour.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All lunch sets are deep-fried in wheat breading in a shared kitchen; no information about fryer separation is available.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is a core ingredient across the menu — house tare, miso preparations, and grilled chicken sauces all involve conventional shoyu.

vegan_cross_contact.text: There are no plant-based dishes on the menu; animal proteins are present in every listed item.

caveats: No official website exists. Smoking policy is noted as "全席喫煙可" (all seats allow smoking) on Tabelog — one secondary source incorrectly stated non-smoking; Tabelog data is treated as authoritative. Chef/owner identity is not publicly documented on any platform. Hours and closed-days are consistent across all sources. Cash only confirmed by Tabelog.

neighborhood: 中区 丸の内 (Naka Ward, Marunouchi)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内1-11-28 田中屋ビル101
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内一丁目11番28号; coordinates [136.893539, 35.173939] fall within target box and approximately 1.41km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61km radius.
vegan_detail: Several dishes are marked with a "V" symbol and can be made vegan for an additional ¥200 (e.g., oil-fried chicken with soy meat, yuba fried rice, dandan noodles, mapo tofu, sweet and sour pork with soy meat). The restaurant also accommodates five-allium-free (五葷抜き) Buddhist dietary requests. It is not a fully vegan restaurant — omnivore dishes share the kitchen. Hidden animal products: no dashi or katsuo mentioned in vegan dishes; house-made soy sauce koji and salt koji used, brown sugar for sweetness.
gf_detail: The restaurant advertises gluten-free adaptation (グルテンフリー対応) and a TripAdvisor reviewer reported a six-course vegan gluten-free dinner. However, the signature vegan dandan noodles use egg-free domestic wheat noodles — not gluten-free. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce is specifically confirmed in any source; standard Chinese soy sauce contains wheat. No dedicated GF fryer or preparation area confirmed. Cross-contamination protocols not documented. Celiacs should communicate needs clearly in advance; ask specifically about soy sauce and noodle alternatives before ordering.
seats_text: 15 seats total: 5 counter, 10 table. Intimate space; reservations strongly recommended.
cultural_comfort_note: English menus are available with vegan options clearly marked; multiple foreign reviewers confirm the chef proactively explains each dish and staff ask about dietary restrictions at the table.

bio.background: Owner-chef Ai Hiroshi holds international certification as a medicinal cooking instructor (国際薬膳調理師) and previously served as head chef at a renowned hotel before opening this intimate 15-seat spot in Nagoya's Marunouchi district. The restaurant is a husband-and-wife operation; the wife prepares desserts including refined-sugar-free tarts and whole cakes available for special occasions.
bio.specialty: Vegan dandan noodles (ベジ担々麺) and soy-meat sweet and sour pork with black vinegar are signature dishes, alongside mapo tofu and seasonal medicinal lunch sets.
bio.philosophy: Anzu builds Chinese cuisine on a foundation of traditional Chinese medicinal dietary principles (薬膳), using no chemical seasonings or refined white sugar. Oils are rice oil and non-GMO soy oil; sweetness comes from unrefined brown sugar (粗糖). The kitchen makes its own salt koji, soy sauce koji, and hon-mirin seasonally. The chef actively visits local producers — sourcing lotus root from 田島蓮園 and strawberries from かとう農園 — and holds cooking classes five times a year aligned with the 24 solar terms. The stated goal is that every diner, regardless of dietary restriction, receives the same quality meal rather than a stripped-down substitute.

anecdotes.text: A TripAdvisor reviewer described bringing one vegan and three omnivores and receiving a six-course meal that was simultaneously vegan and gluten-free: 'We all enjoyed this wonderful vegan gluten-free deliciousness! Highly recommend giving them a try!'

A note.com natural-food reviewer described the chef as bringing a rare approach: instead of simply removing ingredients, he redesigns dishes so the vegan version stands on its own merits — including a plant-based egg made from potato and pumpkin.

roles: international medicinal cooking instructor (国際薬膳調理師)
culinary instructor at Nagoya Tsuji Culinary Professional School

positives.text: No chemical seasonings (MSG) or refined white sugar used anywhere in the kitchen.
Staff proactively ask about dietary restrictions; the chef personally brings dishes to the table and explains every ingredient, enabling real-time dialogue about allergen concerns.
English menu clearly marks vegan options, reducing communication friction for non-Japanese-speaking diners with dietary needs.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The kitchen uses homemade soy sauce koji (醤油麹) as a core condiment. No source confirms wheat-free or tamari soy sauce is used. Standard soy sauce in Chinese cooking contains wheat; celiacs must ask explicitly whether a wheat-free alternative is available for their specific dishes.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or cross-contamination protocols are documented in any available source. The kitchen prepares wheat-based noodles (the vegan dandan noodles use egg-free domestic wheat noodles) alongside adapted GF dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Not a fully vegan restaurant — meat and seafood dishes are prepared in the same kitchen. No source documents whether dedicated cookware or utensils are used for vegan preparations.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Multiple reviewers confirm staff ask about dietary preferences and allergies at the table, and the owner-chef explains each dish personally — suggesting genuine attentiveness rather than a scripted allergy protocol.

caveats: Hours from Tabelog (Tue-Sat lunch+dinner, Sun dinner only, Mon closed) — verify before visiting as small independent restaurants may adjust seasonally. Cash only confirmed. GF confidence set to "ask" because: (1) noodles contain wheat; (2) soy sauce wheat content unconfirmed; (3) no cross-contamination protocols documented. A six-course GF dinner was achieved by one visitor but advance communication is essential. The restaurant has relocated once (from 1-15-28 to 1-11-28 Marunouchi) — current address confirmed by multiple current sources.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区樋の口町3-19 エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 4F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 樋の口町3-19; coordinates [136.896072, 35.185513] place the restaurant approximately 0.03 km from Nagoya Castle — well within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 名古屋城近く・浅間町（エスパシオ ナゴヤキャッスル 4F）
seats_text: 8席のカウンターのみ
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-only omakase with direct chef interaction; the chef is known for being warm and conversational, but the experience is conducted almost entirely in Japanese with no documented English service.
vegan_detail: Pure seafood omakase — every course centres on fish, shellfish, and roe. There is no plant-based alternative and the restaurant explicitly declines reservations from guests with extensive ingredient restrictions.
gf_detail: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is used throughout the nigiri service. Dashi is made from fish bones. No gluten-free protocols or dedicated preparation areas are documented. The restaurant's own policy explicitly turns away guests with severe allergies or broad food restrictions, making it incompatible with celiac requirements.

bio.background: Chef Nishikawa spent over 20 years honing his craft in Japanese cuisine before opening this restaurant, choosing sushi specifically because the counter format lets him interact directly with each guest — a style he felt matched his personality. The restaurant originally opened in Shimizu before relocating to the Meieki area in 2018. In October 2025 it moved again to its current home on the fourth floor of the newly opened luxury hotel Espacio Nagoya Castle, just steps from the castle keep.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi (originally Shimizu; relocated to Meieki 2018; current Espacio location from October 2025)
bio.specialty: Kuruma ebi (tiger prawn) sourced from Mikawa Isshiki, each large shrimp yielding two contrasting preparations — one chilled immediately after boiling for a snappy texture, the other fully cooked to draw out natural sweetness.
bio.philosophy: "To provide enjoyable dining experiences — that is our only goal." Nishikawa sources ingredients each morning only for that day's confirmed guests, and applies what he calls a 'golden ratio' of salt and aging time tailored to each fish variety to concentrate umami.

anecdotes.text: When offered a television appearance, Nishikawa declined — explaining that he could only give passing grades to his own dishes, reflecting standards too high to broadcast.

The restaurant earned two stars in the Michelin Guide Aichi, Gifu & Mie special edition, and a Tabelog ranking inside the national top 1,000, yet the chef's stated ambition remains simply to make customers smile.
Diners at the Espacio counter look directly onto the floodlit silhouette of Nagoya Castle, making the backdrop as much a part of the experience as the food.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or protocol is documented. In a small 8-seat counter kitchen handling multiple courses simultaneously, cross-contamination from soy sauce and other wheat-containing condiments is essentially certain.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is used in nigiri preparation. No mention of tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternatives in any reviewed source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Every course involves seafood; fish-bone dashi is used for appetiser preparations. There is no plant-based track and no separation of animal products.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant's official policy is to decline reservations from guests with severe allergies or broad ingredient restrictions, citing incompatibility with the omakase format.

caveats: Omakase-only format with no substitutions; the restaurant actively screens out guests with significant dietary restrictions at the booking stage. Not suitable for GF or vegan diners. Dinner courses run until 1:00 AM; last seating is at 22:00. Price point is ¥27,500+ for dinner. 15% service charge added.

name_ja: 名古屋市市政資料館 喫茶室
cuisine: 喫茶・軽食・トースト
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区白壁1丁目3 名古屋市市政資料館2F
geocode_note: GSI geocode returned 136.910156, 35.182091 for 愛知県名古屋市東区白壁一丁目３番地. Approx 1.03 km from Nagoya Castle centroid — within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 東大手・白壁（東区）
seats_text: 26席（カウンター3席、テーブル席）、全席禁煙
hours_raw: 火〜日 9:00〜16:45（LO 16:15）、月曜定休・毎月第3木曜定休（祝日の場合は翌日または第4木曜）、12/29〜1/3休館
closed_days: 月曜（祝日の場合は翌平日）、毎月第3木曜（祝日の場合は第4木曜）、年末年始（12/29〜1/3）
cultural_comfort_note: A quiet, old-school kissaten with no English menu; staff are described as friendly, but expect Japanese-only communication.
vegan_detail: Menu is simple kissaten fare: mixed sandwiches (egg, ham, potato salad fillings), ogura toast, and coffee. No dedicated vegan options; most sandwiches contain egg and/or ham. Beverages (drip coffee, lemonade, cream soda) are likely plant-based, but fillings should be verified on arrival. No explicit vegan labeling noted on any source.
gf_detail: Signature items are toast and sandwiches made on wheat bread — gluten is central to the menu. No gluten-free alternatives are advertised. Standard kissaten soy sauce is likely wheat-based. No mention of a dedicated fryer (cafe does not serve fried food). Those with celiac disease should be aware that wheat-based bread is the main food offering and cross-contamination is not addressed anywhere in available sources.

bio.background: The kissashitsu opened in 2003 inside the Nagoya City Archives building (名古屋市市政資料館), a striking Neo-Baroque brick structure completed in 1922 as the Nagoya Court of Appeals and District Court. The building served judicial functions until 1979, when it was repurposed as a civic archives and designated an Important Cultural Property. The cafe occupies a former courtroom on the second floor, its original wooden furniture and high ceilings preserved largely intact.
bio.specialty: ミックスサンド（混合サンドイッチ）and 小倉トースト — the mixed sandwich (¥600) pairs cucumber, tomato, and potato salad, while ogura toast (¥350) nods to Nagoya's red-bean toast tradition; coffee is hand-drip nel-brewed at ¥300.
bio.philosophy: Unhurried, inexpensive refreshment in a space that doubles as living history — visitors rest among marble staircases, stained-glass windows depicting scales of justice, and the quiet hum of an archive. The cafe makes no effort to modernize; its retro character is the point.

anecdotes.text: The central staircase inside the archives features two stained-glass windows — a sun symbolizing fair judicial proceedings above, and scales of justice at the entrance. Over 1,000 couples have held wedding ceremonies on these stairs, and cosplayers regularly use the corridors as a backdrop.
NHK's 2024 drama 'Tora ni Tsubasa' used the archives building as a primary filming location, renewing public interest in the kissashitsu and its former-courtroom setting.
Reviewers note the cafe has almost no exterior signage — finding it feels like discovering a neighborhood secret tucked inside a grand civic monument.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about gluten cross-contamination precautions is available from any source. The kitchen prepares wheat-bread sandwiches and toast as its core menu items, making a gluten-safe environment unlikely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce is listed as an ingredient in menu items. The menu is limited to sandwiches, toast, and beverages, so soy sauce exposure risk is minimal, but no explicit allergen information is provided.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Sandwiches contain egg and ham. No vegan-separated prep area or dedicated utensils are mentioned. Beverages appear plant-based but are not explicitly labeled vegan.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy protocol, or staff allergy-handling practice has been documented in any available source. Phone ahead (052-953-0051) to inquire directly before visiting with a serious allergy.

positives.text: Menu is very short and simple — sandwiches, toast, and drinks — so ingredient questions are straightforward to ask. Staff are consistently described as friendly and approachable.
Entirely non-smoking environment inside a civic building.

caveats: Hours sources are slightly inconsistent (some say 16:30 close, Tabelog says 16:45 with LO 16:15). Third-Thursday closures make the weekly schedule irregular. The building also closes for the New Year period (12/29-1/3) and may close for additional maintenance periods. Confirm current hours by phone (052-953-0051) before visiting.

name_ja: キューオーエルコーヒー
cuisine: カフェ・スペシャルティコーヒー・サンドウィッチ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-5-1 マジマビル
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 丸の内3-5-1; coordinates fall within target box and approximately 1.0 km from Nagoya Castle.
neighborhood: 丸の内・市役所（中区）
seats_text: 27 seats across two floors: ground floor has counter and table seating; second floor has 4 counter seats and several table configurations with window views.
cultural_comfort_note: English menu available; staff are accustomed to solo international visitors; relaxed specialty-coffee shop atmosphere with no pressure.
vegan_detail: No declared vegan menu. Coffee drinks are plant-milk-adaptable in principle, but no confirmation available. The signature food item is a dashi-seasoned rolled egg sandwich with mayo — neither vegan nor likely GF. Pastries and baked goods contain dairy/eggs. Ask staff about milk alternatives and whether any items can be made without animal products.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or allergen information published. The kitchen produces sandwiches on standard wheat bread and baked goods. Black coffee and other straight coffee drinks are naturally GF, but shared equipment and a small kitchen mean cross-contamination risk for any food item. Dashi-maki tamago sandwich uses dashi (broth) whose wheat content is unknown. Patrons with celiac should ask staff specifically about ingredients and preparation; safe to drink black coffee but food options are risky without direct confirmation.

bio.background: Shima Yuya grew up in Nagoya dreaming of running his own kissaten. After a serious run at competitive basketball through high school, he pivoted back to that dream, enrolling in culinary school and then spending years in Italian restaurants before eight years at a respected Nagoya coffee shop in Higashi ward. He then trained at a Gifu roastery to master the craft of roasting, and at age 30+ relocated to Melbourne, Australia — the epicentre of specialty coffee culture — working as a barista for several months before returning to open Q.O.L. COFFEE in June 2017.
bio.specialty: 自家焙煎スペシャルティコーヒー（シングルオリジン・ブレンド）とだし巻き玉子サンド
bio.philosophy: The name Q.O.L. stands for Quality of Life — the belief that one well-made cup of coffee can meaningfully improve a person's day. Shima sources beans directly from farms around the world, roasts them on a GIESEN W6A machine used in international competitions, hand-picks for defects, and measures roast colour numerically to guarantee consistency. Every cup comes with a card naming the bean and its origin.
roles: オーナー・ロースター・バリスタ

anecdotes.text: Shima holds a Q-Grader licence — an internationally recognised coffee quality-grading certification held by only around 300 people in Japan — as well as Coffee Meister and Coffee Instructor credentials. He has served as a judge at Cup of Excellence international competitions and at Japan's national hand-drip championship.

On the shop's 7th anniversary in June 2024, Shima released a limited blend called '7th Anniversary Blend FURUMAI' using Colombian and Salvadoran Geisha beans, reflecting on seven years that 'passed in a flash despite various equipment troubles along the way.'
A second location, Q.O.L. COFFEE BREWERS, opened in April 2022 inside Nagoya PARCO's west building, bringing the brand to a younger, commercial-district audience.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No allergen or cross-contamination information is published. The café bakes pastries and prepares sandwiches on wheat bread in a small two-floor space; shared preparation surfaces are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature dashi-maki tamago sandwich is seasoned with dashi broth; the soy sauce or dashi type (and any wheat content) is not disclosed in any published source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All food items reviewed contain eggs, dairy, or both. No vegan-designated preparation area is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy is publicly documented. The café does offer an English menu, which suggests some capacity to communicate with non-Japanese speakers about ingredients.

positives.text: Straight specialty coffee drinks (black coffee, cold brew, flat white without cow's milk) are naturally gluten-free and vegan-adaptable, and represent the core offering of the shop.

caveats: Hours sourced from Tabelog (07:45–17:30 weekdays, 09:00–17:30 weekends, Monday closed with irregular closures). One blog source listed different hours (07:30–19:00/18:00); Tabelog is treated as more current. No allergen menu exists; all dietary ratings require direct confirmation with staff. The qolcoffee.thebase.in URL from Tabelog appears to be a Bean Shop online store rather than the main official site; qolcoffee.com is the primary domain.

cuisine: 鉄板焼き・A5黒毛和牛・活魚介
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内1-9-7 バンケービル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocoded to 丸の内一丁目; 1.23km from Nagoya Castle — within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内、地下鉄丸の内駅1分
seats_text: カウンター12席＋個室18席、計約33席（4名・6名・12名対応の個室4室あり）
closed_days: 不定休（月1〜2回程度）。詳細は要問合せ。
cultural_comfort_note: 完全予約制の高級鉄板焼き店で、英語メニューや英語対応スタッフの情報は確認できず。日本語での予約・来店が前提と思われる。
gf_detail: グルテンフリー対応の明示なし。鉄板焼きコースには醤油ベースのタレや、小麦を含む調味料が標準的に使用される。専用フライヤーはなし（鉄板調理）。コース内容はゲスト個別の予算・要望に応じて相談可能とのことで、アレルギー等は予約時に問い合わせが必須。
vegan_detail: メニューはA5和牛・活魚介を主軸とした完全なオムニコース構成。ヴィーガン・ベジタリアン対応の記載なし。オーナーが自家栽培した野菜を使用するなど素材へのこだわりはあるが、動物性食材を除いたコース提供は確認できない。予約時に個別相談の余地はある可能性がある。

bio.background: 名古屋・丸の内のビジネス街に静かに構える鉄板料理の隠れ家。薄暗い路地を抜けた先にあるカウンターと個室は、接待や記念日のための特別な空間として地元の常連に知られる。オーナーは自ら野菜を栽培し、客の顔ぶれや予算に合わせた仕入れを行うという徹底したこだわりを持つ。
bio.specialty: A5ランク黒毛和牛の鉄板焼き（サーロイン・フィレ・シャトーブリアン）、活アワビ・伊勢海老
bio.philosophy: 「最高級の食材で最上級のおもてなしを」。完全予約制を採ることで一組一組に向き合い、食材は予約ごとに個別調達する。
roles: オーナー（野菜栽培・食材選定）

anecdotes.text: 「隠れ家的」な雰囲気で、薄暗い通路を抜けたU字カウンターが特徴的。レビュアーはオーナーが食事中にスタッフへワインサービスを指導し始めたことをユニークな体験として記録している。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 鉄板焼きでは同一の調理面を複数の食材・ソースに使用するため、小麦を含む醤油ベースのソース等との接触リスクがある。グルテンへの配慮に関する記載は公式サイト・予約サイトのいずれにも見当たらない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 鉄板料理のタレや仕上げには醤油（小麦含有）が一般的に使われる。グルテンフリー醤油（丸大豆醤油等）の使用に関する明示はない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全コースが和牛・魚介を中心に構成されており、同一の鉄板面での交差接触は避けられない構造。ヴィーガン向けの分離調理に関する案内は確認できない。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 公式サイトは「ご予算・ご要望に合わせてコースをご用意」と記載しており、アレルギー等の事前申告は予約時に電話で確認することを推奨。英語対応は未確認。

positives.text: 完全予約制かつ少人数対応の個室あり。食材は予約ごとに個別調達するスタイルで、事前に詳細な要望を伝えやすい環境。

caveats: GF・ヴィーガン対応の公式情報は一切なし。醤油・出汁・バターが鉄板料理の基本要素であり、セリアック病や厳格なアレルギーには不向き。予約時に日本語で詳細を事前確認することを強く推奨。営業時間・定休日は不定休のため来店前に電話確認が必要。

neighborhood: 東区 泉 (Higashi-ku, Izumi) — nearest subway: 市役所駅 / 東大手駅
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区泉２丁目１−２７ ライオンズマンション東片端
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市東区泉二丁目１番２７号; result 35.17791, 136.914322 — 1.58 km from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), just within the 1.61 km radius. Address is listed in third-party aggregators (Tabelog area code A230109 = 市役所 area) despite the restaurant itself keeping contact details private.
seats_text: Approximately 8 seats at an L-shaped counter
hours_raw: 18:00〜22:00 (reported across multiple aggregators; exact per-day schedule and closed days not publicly confirmed)
cultural_comfort_note: Entirely in Japanese; no English spoken; access is limited to returning regulars — first-time visitors cannot make a reservation through any public channel.
vegan_detail: Yakitori course built around grilled chicken; no plant-based alternatives noted anywhere. Standard yakitori tare (basting sauce) contains mirin and soy sauce. Not a viable option for vegans.
gf_detail: Yakitori tare almost certainly contains wheat-based soy sauce (regular shoyu). No GF accommodations, dedicated fryer, or allergen information published. Course-only format leaves no room for substitutions. Not safe for celiac diners.

bio.background: Opened around early 2018 in a discreet residential building in Nagoya's Higashi-ku Izumi district. The restaurant has no signage, no public phone number, and no official website — access is through returning regulars only.
bio.specialty: Omakase yakitori course featuring premium chicken parts, showcased daily in a wooden box arrangement reminiscent of an osechi tray before grilling begins.
bio.philosophy: The owner-chef reportedly devotes virtually all waking hours to the craft, sourcing and preparing ingredients with an intensity that reviewers describe as singular even by yakitori standards.

anecdotes.text: A blogger visiting a pre-opening event in late 2017 noted that every element of the course — from appetizers through grilled skewers to palate cleansers — bore the stamp of the chef's personal obsession.

Celebrity blogger Kato Chika wrote that the chef presents the day's raw ingredients arranged in a beautiful wooden box before service, and closes each course with handmade chocolate — a detail that stuck with regulars.
One commenter noted the restaurant's owner previously discouraged even naming the place on social media, though that policy softened over time.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No cross-contamination data available. The kitchen is a tiny owner-operated counter; no allergen protocol has been documented anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Yakitori tare (the basting glaze applied during grilling) standardly contains wheat-brewed soy sauce. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative has been reported.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Entire menu is animal-based; vegan cross-contact is not a meaningful concept here as no plant-based dishes are offered.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling information published. The course-only, regulars-only format means the kitchen does not adapt to dietary restrictions for new guests.

caveats: Address (愛知県名古屋市東区泉２丁目１−２７) is drawn from third-party aggregators; the restaurant itself keeps its precise location and contact details private and accessible only to regulars. Hours (18:00-22:00) are similarly from aggregators and unconfirmed directly. Closed days are unknown. The restaurant is functionally inaccessible to first-time diners — it should not be recommended as a walk-in or general-public option in the app. GF and vegan ratings reflect the standard yakitori format; no specific allergen communication has been observed from this venue.

name_ja: 炭火やきとり ごまめ泉
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区泉1-14-3 HASEGAWAビル 3A
geocode_note: GSI address search on 愛知県名古屋市東区泉1-14-3 returned [136.908875, 35.175648]. Distance from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is 1386m — within the 1.61km requirement. Coordinates fall within target box (lat 35.171–35.200, lng 136.882–136.917).
neighborhood: 久屋大通・泉 (Hisaya-odori / Izumi, Higashi Ward)
seats_text: 32 seats: 8 counter (open kitchen view), 24 table seats
vegan_detail: Entirely meat-focused yakitori with chicken as the centerpiece (Satsuma Jidori, Tanba Akadori). Seafood items include horse sashimi. No plant-based options noted anywhere. All skewers are animal-derived; there is no known vegan-friendly menu path.
gf_detail: Standard yakitori tare (dipping sauce) almost certainly contains wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu). Salt-grilled (shio) skewers are likely wheat-free on the skewer itself, but shared grill and kitchen cross-contamination are unconfirmed. No allergen menu or gluten-free declaration found anywhere. Must ask the kitchen specifically about tare ingredients and shared preparation surfaces before ordering.
cultural_comfort_note: A stylish but neighborhood-local izakaya; menus and staff communication will be in Japanese, though the modern setting and card-payment system make it navigable with basic Japanese or a translation app.

bio.background: Opened February 21, 2025 in the Izumi 1-chome district of Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a 3-minute walk from Kuyamachi Station. The shop occupies the third floor of the Hasegawa Building, offering views over the Nagoya nightscape from its counter and table seats.
bio.specialty: Charcoal-grilled yakitori using Wakayama Kishu binchotan charcoal, which generates far-infrared heat that seals in umami. Signature skewers draw from aged thigh meat (jukusei momo) sourced from two premium breeds — Satsuma Jidori and Tanba Akadori — letting diners compare flavour profiles side by side.
bio.philosophy: Authentic binchotan technique in a refined, non-smoking space that suits both casual evenings and business hospitality.

anecdotes.text: The counter faces an open kitchen, so guests can watch the master tend skewers over glowing charcoal — a deliberate design choice that turns grilling into part of the dining experience.
Jukusei momo (aged thigh) is offered in two breed variants on the same visit, a tasting-flight approach unusual for a neighbourhood yakitori-ya.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on shared grilling surfaces or kitchen cross-contamination practices. The charcoal grill is almost certainly shared across all skewer types.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tare (yakitori dipping sauce) is standard in Japanese yakitori and typically contains wheat-based soy sauce. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative has been mentioned by any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All cooking surfaces are used exclusively for meat and seafood; there is no plant-based section and no vegan cross-contact protocol documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy declaration, or staff allergy-handling procedure has been documented by any source checked. Phone inquiry (052-212-5595) before visiting is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

positives.text: Shio (salt) grilled skewers avoid tare entirely and may be the safest ordering path for wheat-avoiders, though shared-grill cross-contamination remains unconfirmed.

caveats: Opened February 2025; still accumulating reviews (101 on Tabelog as of research date). No official website accessible. No allergen menu exists in any source — phone ahead for any dietary need. Chef/owner identity not publicly documented; bio confidence is low.

name_ja: オステリア オルカドーロ
neighborhood: 西区花の木 (浄心駅徒歩3分)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区花の木2-17-9
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区花の木二丁目１７番９号; confirmed ~931m from Nagoya Castle centroid, within 1.61km radius.
hours_raw: 火〜日 ランチ 11:30〜13:30、ディナー 17:30〜22:00。月曜定休（月曜が祝日の場合は翌火曜休）。
closed_days: 月曜日（月曜が祝日の場合は翌火曜日）
seats_text: テーブル席20席・カウンター席6席、計26席
cultural_comfort_note: Owner-operated neighbourhood osteria near Joshin Station; no English menu or signage noted, and ordering centres on a chalkboard of daily specials — a working knowledge of Japanese is helpful.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu or explicit accommodation listed. The kitchen works à la carte with daily market seafood, meat, and handmade wheat pasta. The chef is noted for adapting to guest requests, so vegetable-only plates may be possible if arranged in advance, but hidden animal-derived ingredients (fish stock, butter, bonito dashi) are standard in Italian cooking here and cannot be guaranteed absent without direct inquiry.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu, dedicated fryer, or allergen protocol is documented anywhere. Fresh handmade wheat pasta is a signature, and bread (focaccia) is served routinely. Cross-contamination from flour is a realistic concern in this kitchen. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant directly before visiting; safe options are not assured without custom arrangement.

bio.background: Born in Shizuoka Prefecture in January 1976. Yokoyama is an Italian cuisine specialist who opened Osteria Orca d'Oro in Nagoya's Nishi Ward, operating it as owner-chef.
bio.specialty: Seasonal à la carte Italian: house-made fresh pasta (tagliatelle, pappardelle, tagliolini), acqua pazza (whole fish simmered in light broth), and charcoal-grilled Mikawa beef bistecca alla fiorentina. The menu changes daily based on what the chef selects at Yanagibashi Market each morning.
bio.philosophy: Yokoyama centres the menu on ingredients rather than a fixed list: he visits Yanagibashi Market every morning to hand-pick fresh seafood and vegetables, then builds the day's dishes around what is finest. He describes 'deliciousness as the foundation' while aiming for visual beauty in every plate, and expresses that he is grateful to each guest who chooses his restaurant from among the many options in the city.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi (opened as an independent osteria in Nishi Ward)

anecdotes.text: The chef visits Yanagibashi Market every morning to personally select that day's seafood and vegetables, then composes dishes around what he finds — the chalkboard specials change daily as a result.
The restaurant offers more than 200 Italian wines, positioning itself as a wine destination as much as a food destination in a residential neighbourhood not far from Nagoya Castle.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Fresh handmade wheat pasta is a core menu item and flour is in active use throughout service; cross-contamination cannot be ruled out without direct confirmation from the kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce-based preparations are documented for this Italian kitchen, but standard wheat-containing Italian ingredients (pasta dough, bread, flour-dusted proteins) are present throughout the menu.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Butter, cream, fish stock, and cured meats are standard components of an Italian osteria kitchen; vegan cross-contact from shared surfaces and cookware is expected unless the kitchen is asked to accommodate a specific order separately.
