The venue asks that guests with dietary restrictions notify them in advance at reservation time; accommodations may not be guaranteed. Bar cocktails may include dairy or egg-based ingredients. Non-alcoholic options are available.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu. Afternoon tea explicitly includes wheat-containing scones (whole wheat and hatcho miso flavored) and Atelier Ukai pastry. No dedicated GF fryer or kitchen. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is likely used in savory preparations. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant directly before visiting; accommodations after arrival cannot be guaranteed per their own policy.
hours_raw: Daily 12:00-23:30 (L.O. 23:00). Afternoon tea: two seatings at 12:00 and 14:30, served until 17:00. Bar service: 17:00-23:30 (L.O. 23:00). After 17:00, venue is restricted to guests aged 20 and over. Reservations accepted for afternoon tea; bar time walk-in only (no reservations accepted for bar time after 19:00).
closed_days: No regular closed days listed. Hours may change; confirm with restaurant before visiting.
seats_text: 40 seats total: 6 counter seats, 14 outdoor terrace seats with castle views, and interior table seating. Minimum 2 guests required for afternoon tea.
cultural_comfort_note: Part of a luxury international hotel (Espacio Nagoya Castle, member of The Leading Hotels of the World) with English-language website, international-facing booking platforms, and staff accustomed to foreign guests.
caveats: Bar time (17:00-23:30) is walk-in only with no reservations accepted. Age restriction: 20+ after 17:00. A 10-15% service charge is added. Hours verified from Tabelog and TableCheck; always confirm with the restaurant as hours may change seasonally.

bio.background: THE BAR CASTLE occupies the 4th floor of Espacio Nagoya Castle, a luxury hotel that opened in October 2025 adjacent to Nagoya Castle. The bar concept is a collaboration between the hotel and SG Group, the bartending empire built by Shingo Gokan — a Japanese bartender who moved to New York at age 23 and spent seven years as head bartender at the legendary Angel's Share. In 2012, Gokan won the Bacardi Legacy Cocktail Competition, cementing his global reputation. He returned to Japan and opened The SG Club in Tokyo's Shibuya in 2018, which went on to rank 10th globally on The World's 50 Best Bars in 2020 and be named Best Bar in Japan. SG Group bars have appeared on both the World's and Asia's 50 Best Bars for nine consecutive years.
bio.specialty: Eight signature cocktails from The SG Club, blending Japanese bartending sensibility with international cocktail craft; afternoon tea by Shigeo Suzuki, Grand Patissier of Atelier Ukai, featuring sweets made from Tokai region seasonal ingredients
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture (opened October 2025 as part of Espacio Nagoya Castle)
bio.philosophy: Gokan's approach — described as 'no flash for the sake of flash, no ego for the sake of ego' — channels Japanese hospitality (omotenashi) into world-class mixology. The bar imagines what Japanese culture looks like when filtered through global cocktail culture, with non-alcoholic options offered alongside for sober-curious guests. The afternoon tea adds a further layer: pastry chef Shigeo Suzuki of Atelier Ukai channels the spirit of the Japanese tea ceremony into each seasonal sweet.
roles: Head bartender program: SG Group / Shingo Gokan
Pastry / afternoon tea: Shigeo Suzuki, Grand Patissier, Atelier Ukai

anecdotes.text: Shingo Gokan moved to New York at age 23 without speaking English, initially pursuing pastry before pivoting to bartending. Seven years at Angel's Share — one of NYC's most influential Japanese cocktail bars — sharpened his craft. He won the Bacardi Legacy Cocktail Competition in 2012 and has since built one of the most globally decorated bar groups in Asia.

The SG Club achieved Top 10 on The World's 50 Best Bars — an achievement described as 'almost no venue in Asia can claim' — and SG Group has placed bars on the list for nine consecutive years across Tokyo, Shanghai, and beyond.

THE BAR CASTLE features Moroccan lamp lighting, counter seating with video displays, and an outdoor terrace overlooking Nagoya Castle — bringing the SG Group's multi-concept, immersive atmosphere philosophy to Nagoya.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on dedicated GF prep areas or cross-contamination controls. The kitchen produces wheat-containing pastry (scones, Atelier Ukai sweets) in the same kitchen context. Celiac diners should not assume safety.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Savory afternoon tea items (e.g., marinated tomatoes, soup, prosciutto preparations) are likely prepared with standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. No wheat-free soy sauce (tamari) is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Afternoon tea is built around dairy-based pastries, clotted cream, and likely egg-containing sweets. No vegan-separated prep confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The venue asks guests to notify them of allergies or ingredients they cannot consume at time of reservation. They note they may be unable to accommodate requests made after arrival, and some requests may be declined prioritizing customer safety.

positives.text: As part of a luxury international hotel, staff are accustomed to dietary-restriction conversations and communicate in English. Advance notice requests are built into their booking process via TableCheck and phone reservations.
Non-alcoholic cocktail alternatives are available for all bar items, and alcohol-free accommodations for dietary or lifestyle reasons are explicitly noted.

cuisine: 日本料理・懐石（フレンチ融合）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-30-6
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野一丁目３０番６号; 1.45km from target center — within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 西区那古野（四間道）
cultural_comfort_note: 完全予約制のおまかせ懐石で、予約・来店ともに日本語対応が基本。英語メニューや英語スタッフの情報なし。
vegan_detail: おまかせコースのみで、魚介・肉類・卵・乳製品を使用したフランス懐石。ヴィーガン対応の記載なし。だしは魚介系と推定される。カスタマイズ不可のコース制のため代替対応は期待できない。
gf_detail: グルテンフリー対応の公式情報なし。メニューに麩（小麦グルテン）が含まれることが確認されており（「麩・筍・菜の花・里芋の炊き合わせ」）、フランス料理の手法を取り入れているため小麦使用の可能性が高い。醤油はほぼ確実に小麦含有の一般醤油使用と推定。おまかせコースのためアレルギー対応は要事前相談。

bio.chef_name: 鹿部戸純生（かぶとじゅんき）
bio.background: オーナーシェフは20年以上にわたりフランス料理の現場で修業。その後、名古屋の歴史地区・四間道（しけみち）の古い町並みの中に店を構え、フランス料理の技法と懐石の精神を融合させた独創的な日本料理を23年以上提供し続けている。
bio.specialty: 季節の食材を活かしたフランス懐石おまかせコース。烏賊を細麺状に仕立てた一皿やオマール海老のグラタン、鯛茶漬けなど、フランスと和食の境界を軽やかに越える料理が印象的。
bio.philosophy: 「完全予約制おまかせコースのみ」という姿勢を開業以来一貫して守り続ける。毎朝シェフ自ら市場で食材を吟味し、全国から選りすぐった旬の素材を使う。
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区那古野（四間道）。江戸・明治期の蔵と町家が連なる歴史的景観保存地区の一角に、古民家を再生した空間で営む。

anecdotes.text: 「二十年フレンチの現場で学んだオーナーシェフ」が作る「フレンチの手法を取り入れた独創的な和の世界観」と食べログユーザーが評する。
亡き父が12〜13年前に通っていた店に、子が初めて訪れて感慨にふけったというレビューが残り、長年の常連を持つ地元の名店であることが窺える。
ナプキンは四間道を通るたびに柄が変わるという細やかなホスピタリティも話題に上る。

gf_cross_contamination.text: フランス懐石のおまかせコースにつき、小麦（フランス料理の技法に由来するソースや揚げ衣）のクロスコンタミネーションリスクは排除できない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油の種類（小麦含有の有無）について公式情報なし。一般的な日本料理で使用される醤油には小麦が含まれる。

vegan_cross_contact.text: コース全体を通じて魚介・肉・卵・乳製品が使用されており、ヴィーガン向けクロスコンタクト回避は困難と推定される。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応について公式ウェブサイト・予約サイト等に明示的な記載なし。完全予約制のため、予約時に直接問い合わせることを推奨。

caveats: グルテンフリー・ヴィーガンともに公式対応情報なし。メニューに麩（小麦グルテン素材）が明記されており、セリアック・小麦アレルギーの方には非推奨。おまかせコースのみのため個別対応は限定的と思われる。予約時に必ず詳細を直接確認のこと。

name_ja: からめ亭 丸の内店
cuisine: あんかけスパゲッティ (Ankake Spaghetti)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-14-4 EXEビル丸の内ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内二丁目１４番４号 returned [136.899658, 35.175034]; approximately 1.18 km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 丸の内 / 丸の内駅徒歩3分
seats_text: 35席（カウンター・テーブル）
hours_raw: 月〜金・祝前日・祝後日 11:00〜19:00（L.O.18:30）、土・祝 11:00〜15:00（L.O.14:30）、日曜定休。※複数情報源で営業時間に差異あり（20:30閉店とする情報もあり）。来訪前に要確認。
cultural_comfort_note: A no-frills business-district lunch counter; menu is in Japanese only, staff likely speak minimal English, but ordering is straightforward — point at the menu board or say the dish name.
vegan_detail: The signature ankake sauce is simmered with beef, onions, carrots, potatoes, and tomatoes over two days. All dishes contain meat-based sauce. No plant-based or meat-free option is available; vegan diners cannot be accommodated.
gf_detail: The core dish is wheat-based spaghetti and the sauce almost certainly contains wheat via soy sauce used in seasoning. The pasta itself is standard wheat spaghetti. No gluten-free noodle alternative is offered. No allergen accommodation is mentioned anywhere. Cross-contamination risk is high in an active pasta kitchen. Not suitable for celiac or wheat-allergic diners.

bio.background: Founded in 1979 in Nagoya's Chikusa ward as ソール本山 (Sole Motoyama), the restaurant later relocated and rebranded as からめ亭. Founder Shichi Shoji — who came to cooking with no formal culinary training — developed the recipe from scratch, refining it over roughly two years before it reached its definitive form.
bio.specialty: あんかけスパゲッティ — thick-cut spaghetti stir-fried in lard, topped with a peppery brown gravy simmered for two days from beef, onion, tomato, carrot, and potato, thickened with potato starch. The dish leans heavily on black pepper for heat.
bio.philosophy: Shoji drew inspiration from Nagoya's own あんかけうどん when he coined the term 'あんかけスパゲッティ', deliberately naming a dish that already existed in the city's cafés but lacked a common identity. He has continued to develop new toppings and regional variations over four decades.

anecdotes.text: Shoji explained his naming insight in a 2021 Tokai TV feature: 'The color of Nagoya's ankake udon looked very similar to what we were serving, so I borrowed the idea and called it ankake spaghetti.' The term caught on and is now the standard name for the dish city-wide.

In 2010 からめ亭 attempted a national expansion by opening a location in Chiba Prefecture, but the dish struggled to find an audience outside Nagoya. Shoji later developed a milder 'Kansai Mild' version using dashi broth to try to bridge the flavor gap.
The restaurant describes itself as 'the namesake of ankake spaghetti' and considers itself one of the three founding pillars of the genre, alongside Yokoi and So~re.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen handles wheat pasta exclusively — this is an ankake spaghetti specialist. No gluten-free preparation zone or cross-contamination controls are documented anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Ankake sauce recipes for this style typically include soy sauce as a seasoning component; no wheat-free shoyu alternative is mentioned. Assume standard wheat-containing soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All dishes are built on a beef-simmered sauce base; no vegan-safe preparation path exists on the menu.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy or allergen menu is documented at this restaurant. Contacting the restaurant directly (052-212-3537) before visiting is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

caveats: Hours are inconsistent across sources (ranging from 19:00 to 20:30 closing on weekdays); the Tabelog data showing 19:00 closing is used as primary, but confirm by phone before visiting. The restaurant is confirmed open as of available 2025–2026 search data with no closure notices found.

cuisine: 鴨料理・懐石（河内鴨専門）
neighborhood: 西区那古野（四間道）/ 国際センター駅徒歩4分
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-30-8
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野一丁目30番8号 → [136.891876, 35.174179]. Approximately 1.43km from Nagoya Castle centroid — within the 1.61km target radius.
seats_text: 全30席、すべて完全個室（2名・4名・6名・8名・10〜16名用）
hours_raw: 月〜土 17:30〜22:30（料理LO 21:30、ドリンクLO 22:00）、日曜・祝日定休
cultural_comfort_note: 英語・中国語（簡体・繁体）・韓国語メニューあり、英語対応スタッフ常駐と明記されている。
vegan_detail: 鴨料理専門店のため、すべてのコースに鴨肉（河内鴨）が使用される。だしも鴨ベースと推定され、植物性のみの食事には対応不可。ヴィーガン向けの代替メニューの記載は一切なし。
gf_detail: グルテンフリー対応の明示なし。鴨のタタキには三重県産の醤油（小麦含有の可能性が高い通常醤油）を使用と記載あり。鍋コースの割り下・たれにも一般的な醤油が使われる可能性が高い。専用フライヤーの記載なし。小麦アレルギーの方は予約時に事前確認が必須。

bio.background: 合資会社宮治（名古屋市西区那古野1-18-10）が50年にわたって営む和食グループの一店舗。グループは『新しい和食の価値観のクリエイト』をコンセプトに、やきとり・鴨料理・四季料理など那古野エリアに5軒を展開する。鴨料理 風月は伝統的な日本家屋を改装した建物に入り、坪庭の見える全席完全個室を備える。
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区那古野（四間道エリア）
bio.specialty: 河内鴨のタタキ（近畿地方限定流通の河内鴨を炭火で焼き上げ、わさび醤油で供する看板料理）および鴨しゃぶしゃぶ（京鴨・河内鴨を食べ比べ、締めに蕎麦）
bio.philosophy: 「旨みたっぷりの赤身としつこくない脂で胃にもたれない」河内鴨を丁寧に研究・仕入れ、接待や記念日に使える完全個室で心のこもったおもてなしを届けることを掲げる。
roles: 完全個室・鴨料理専門店

anecdotes.text: 鴨料理 風月はやきとり 風月と同じ宮治グループがオープンした姉妹店で、「鴨の味」という日本語のことわざ（美食の代名詞）にちなんだ店名を持つ。
河内鴨は近畿地方のみで流通するとされる希少な品種で、脂の融点が低く体に脂が蓄積しにくいとされる不飽和脂肪酸を多く含むことを店側は強調する。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテン交差汚染についての公開情報なし。コース料理の厨房内での小麦使用状況は不明。予約時に直接確認が必要。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 鴨のタタキには「三重県産醤油」を使用と記載あり。使用する醤油が小麦を含む通常醤油である可能性が高く、グルテン過敏症・セリアック病の方には注意が必要。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全コースに鴨肉・鴨だしが使われる鴨専門店のため、動物性素材の排除は構造上不可能。ヴィーガンの交差汚染対応の記載も一切なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 英語・中国語・韓国語対応スタッフありと記載されており、アレルギーの事前相談は電話予約時（052-485-7808）に行うのが現実的。ただしアレルギー対応ポリシーの明文規定は公開されていない。

positives.text: 全席完全個室のため、他のテーブルからの食材飛散リスクは低い。個室ならではの落ち着いた環境でアレルギー情報を事前にスタッフへ伝えやすい。

caveats: 公式サイト（kamo-fugetsu.com）はTLS証明書エラーのためWebFetch不可。fugetsu-shop.jp（もう一つの公式URL）も接続拒否。情報はTabelog・ぐるなび・ホットペッパー・ヒトサラ・宮治グループサイトから取得。グルテンフリー・アレルギー対応の詳細は公開情報に存在せず、予約時の直接確認を強く推奨。

name_ja: ロカンダ オカーダ
cuisine: イタリアン・ジビエ料理（手打ちパスタ、鹿・猪などのジビエ）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間2-14-30 後藤ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI address search on 愛知県名古屋市西区浅間二丁目 returned [136.888428, 35.185028]; approximately 1.0 km from Nagoya Castle reference point, within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 浅間町（西区）/ 浅間町駅 徒歩2分
seats_text: テーブル席16席（イベント時は14席）
closed_days: 月曜日、毎月第1火曜日（火曜はランチ営業なし）
cultural_comfort_note: Small neighbourhood Italian with Japanese-language menus and staff; no English signage noted, though the atmosphere is casual and welcoming enough that basic pointing/gesturing will get you through.
gf_detail: The menu is built around wheat-based handmade pasta (pappardelle, tagliatelle, ravioli, lasagna, pici, cappelletti, spaghetti) and dishes such as quiche and crostini — wheat is pervasive and central to the kitchen. No gluten-free pasta or dedicated GF options are listed. The restaurant states it can accommodate food allergies upon request, but there is no evidence of celiac-safe protocols, a dedicated fryer, or GF alternatives. Wheat-containing soy sauce use is not documented but standard Italian cooking at this level rarely relies on shoyu; the main celiac risk is the pasta itself and cross-contamination in a small kitchen producing pasta daily.
vegan_detail: The kitchen is strongly oriented toward meat and seafood — venison, wild boar, horse tartare, tripe, octopus, tuna, sardines. A small number of dishes are plant-forward on paper (seasonal vegetable salad ¥1,100, marinated carrots ¥650, marinated olives ¥650, pickles ¥650, minestrone ¥1,400, eggplant-tomato spaghetti ¥1,650), but the pasta contains eggs and the kitchen has no stated vegan protocol. Hidden animal products (butter, dashi, lard in pasta dough) are likely. Vegan diners should call ahead and ask specifically; do not assume any dish is vegan without confirming.

bio.background: Born in Gifu Prefecture in 1982, Okada graduated from the Tsuji Culinary Professional School in Osaka and spent roughly a decade building his Italian craft — beginning at an Italian restaurant in Kobe, then moving to Taberna Visconti in Nagoya. He later staged in Italy's Veneto and Marche regions and served as manager and head chef at THE CUPS café in Nagoya before opening Locanda Okada in February 2019.
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区浅間町（2019年2月開業）
bio.specialty: Gifu-sourced game meat — venison and wild boar from Gero and Gujo — broken down in-house and worked into handmade pastas, terrines, and roasts; squid ink spaghetti is a crowd favourite alongside venison cappelletti and pappardelle al ragù di cervo.
bio.philosophy: 本物の味をカジュアルに楽しめる、温かで優しい店づくり — bringing genuinely Italian flavours to a relaxed, neighbourhood table without the formality of fine dining.

anecdotes.text: Okada personally sources venison and wild boar from two specific hunting areas in Gifu — Gero and Gujo — and butchers the animals himself, a level of commitment to ジビエ unusual even among Italian restaurants in Japan.
The dining room is described as a stylish designer space with a hand-painted mural, giving the small 16-seat room an intimate, almost Florentine trattoria feel tucked into a quiet Nagoya side street.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen produces handmade wheat pasta daily; flour is an active, constant presence. No information about separation of gluten-containing prep areas or utensils is available. Cross-contamination risk for celiac diners must be considered high in the absence of contrary evidence.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce (shoyu) use is mentioned or documented in any source — Italian technique is the foundation. However, the kitchen's allergen handling protocols are not disclosed publicly; anyone with a wheat allergy should ask staff directly.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pasta dough almost certainly contains eggs; the kitchen handles meat and seafood throughout service. No vegan-specific preparation protocols have been disclosed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Tabelog and Hitosara both note 'food allergy accommodations available' (アレルギー対応可), suggesting staff are accustomed to allergy enquiries. The extent of those accommodations — substitution, avoidance, or full protocol — is not documented. Calling ahead (052-887-5579) is strongly recommended.

positives.text: The restaurant explicitly lists food allergy accommodation as a feature, indicating a baseline willingness to discuss dietary needs with guests.

caveats: Hours sourced from Tabelog (verified June 2026 review activity confirms ongoing operations). Seat count varies across sources (16 table seats per Tabelog, 24 total including event standing per Hitosara/HotPepper) — 16 used as the dine-in table figure. No official website found beyond Instagram; official_url set to the verified Instagram account. Chef biography drawn from Hitosara chef profile page. GF and vegan safety ratings are conservative given the pasta-centric, game-meat-heavy menu and absence of any documented celiac or vegan protocol.

neighborhood: 西区上名古屋 (Nishi Ward, Kamimagoya), 浄心駅徒歩8分
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋3-13-30
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch matched 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋三丁目１３番３０号 exactly. 1.19km from Nagoya Castle reference point — within 1.61km radius.
vegan_detail: Dashi is made with Makurazaki katsuobushi (bonito flakes) and Kagoshima kombu. Curry udon broth is chicken-bone based and contains pork. No plant-based broth options known. Not suitable for vegans or vegetarians avoiding fish stock.
gf_detail: All noodles (udon, kishimen, soba, ramen-style) contain wheat. Standard Japanese soy sauce (koikuchi) contains wheat. Tempura is battered in wheat flour. No gluten-free noodle alternatives mentioned anywhere. Cross-contamination is certain given multiple wheat-based noodles produced in-house during breaks. Not suitable for celiac or wheat-allergy diners.
closed_days: 水曜日（年末年始休業あり）
seats_text: 80席。1階はテーブル席と小上がり、2階は座敷。駐車場20台。
cultural_comfort_note: A multigenerational neighborhood udon-ya with no English signage or menu; the staff are warm and service-oriented but communication will be in Japanese.

bio.background: Founded in 1972 (Showa 47) in the Kamimagoya neighborhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward, Kiyohira has been feeding local families for over 50 years. The current proprietor, Oshio Kiyotaka, took over as second-generation owner in his early twenties after training under his father. He now runs the restaurant alongside his mother and wife Takamiko in the same two-story wooden building the shop has always occupied.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture (西区上名古屋, 1972)
bio.specialty: Curry udon — a rich, medium-thick curry broth ladled over hand-made udon; customers are given an apron when they order it. The katsu curry udon (カツカレーうどん), topped with a breaded pork cutlet, is the house favourite.
bio.philosophy: The founder built the restaurant on the principle of listening to customers, and the second generation has carried it forward faithfully. Over five decades, the menu grew from a simple udon shop to over 30 dishes — including miso katsu don, omelette rice, and fried rice — because the owner could never say no to a request. The owner has joked that the relentless requests sometimes make him want to stop, yet he keeps adding dishes out of loyalty to his father's legacy.

anecdotes.text: The owner hand-makes all five noodle varieties — udon, kishimen, soba, Chinese-style, and miso-based — during the restaurant's mid-afternoon break, leaving himself little actual rest time.
When you order curry udon, staff bring a small cloth apron to protect your clothes — a thoughtful house tradition that has become a hallmark of the dining experience.
The menu expanded dramatically because customers kept requesting dishes outside the traditional udon repertoire, and the owner, true to his father's philosophy, couldn't refuse.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All noodles are wheat-based and hand-made on-site across five varieties (udon, kishimen, soba, ramen-style, miso). Cross-contamination with wheat throughout the kitchen is certain.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is used in dashi-based dishes. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternatives are mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi stock is made from Makurazaki katsuobushi (bonito) and Kagoshima kombu. The curry broth contains chicken bone stock and pork. All broth-based dishes contain animal products.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy protocols, or staff allergy-handling procedures are documented on any known source. The restaurant does not appear to have experience catering to strict dietary restrictions. Call 052-531-3387 to inquire directly.

positives.text: The restaurant is highly attentive to hospitality in general — offering aprons for curry dishes and prompt service — suggesting staff are receptive to questions, even if dietary accommodation is limited.

caveats: No official website found. Dashi source (Makurazaki bonito + Kagoshima kombu) confirmed via HotPepper. Founding year appears as both 1969 and 1972 across sources — 1972 (Showa 47) is most commonly cited. Under30 set to 'no' as this is a 50+ year establishment. Distance from Nagoya Castle reference point: 1,192m (within 1.61km).

cuisine: スパイス料理・カレー・インド料理
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区新道1丁目22-13
geocode_note: GSI address search resolved to chō-level (西区新道一丁目); coordinates are centroid of that block, approximately 1.07 km southwest of Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 西区浅間町・新道
seats_text: 15席（カウンター席・座敷席）
hours_raw: 月・水〜日 17:00〜22:00（L.O. 21:30）、火曜定休。営業時間は公式Instagramで要確認。
cultural_comfort_note: Reservations are made via Instagram DM in Japanese; no English interface or signage is indicated, though the young, informal owner is described as welcoming to all.
vegan_detail: Fully omnivore menu. Fish sauce (魚醤) is used as a core seasoning, bonito (鰹) features in at least one curry preparation, and lamb, chicken, and pork all appear on the menu. No vegan dishes or vegan-option labeling have been found across any source. The concept — spice bar with finishing curry — is built around meat and seafood-forward small plates.
gf_detail: Wheat is present throughout: naan is a signature item, and standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is likely used in seasoning. The kitchen operates a tandoor oven and fries items such as cardamom karaage, indicating active use of wheat-flour batters. No gluten-free accommodation or labeling has been mentioned in any review, blog, or listing. Celiac diners should not assume safety and should inquire directly — ideally in Japanese — before visiting.

bio.background: スパイスアワーは間借り営業として吹上のDRUMやゲストハウスよるよなか、アーリーバーズといった場所でカレーポップアップを重ね、カレーの甲子園にも二度出場して支持者を増やした末に、2025年6月8日、名古屋市西区新道の元自転車屋をリノベーションした古民家風の空間に実店舗をオープンさせた。厨房には2名体制で、一方は名古屋の人気カフェ数店で3年の経験を、もう一方はインド料理で知られる「エリックサウス」で2年の経験を積んでいる。
bio.specialty: カルダモン唐揚げ、タンドリーマトンシェクワ・シークケバブ、〆のチキンカレー（シャバシャバ系）
bio.philosophy: 一品ひとつのスパイスの個性を味わわせるシンプルさを軸に、スパイスとお酒の組み合わせを楽しむ「スパイス酒場」として機能する。薬膳的な身体の内側から温まるような味付けを志向し、追いだしや旨味調味料を加えていないのに深みが出ているチキンカレーが評判。
bio.origin: 名古屋市西区新道（旧自転車屋リノベ）

anecdotes.text: オープン前夜の6月7日に「アワーの披露宴」と題したイベントを開催。Bar KanataやBUTTER NOTEをフードゲストに招き、アコースティックライブとDJも行った。
「しっかりとしたタンドールがあり」と常連に指摘されるほど本格的な設備を備える一方、〆カレーとしても機能する間口の広さが特徴。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Naan (wheat flour) is baked in the tandoor oven and served as a core item; the same tandoor is used for marinated meats such as lamb seekh kebab, meaning shared cooking surfaces are in active use.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No confirmation that gluten-free tamari or alternative soy sauce is used. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is the default in this style of fusion spice cooking.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish sauce (魚醤) is used directly in at least one appetizer preparation (mint sauce and fish sauce namero); meat and seafood are cooked throughout the small kitchen, making vegan cross-contact highly probable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information on staff allergy training or allergen labeling has been found in any source. Reservations are handled via Instagram DM, which could provide a channel to raise dietary needs in advance.

positives.text: Small 15-seat independent kitchen means a single team handles all cooking, reducing the complexity of communicating dietary needs — direct conversation with the chef-owner is feasible.

caveats: Hours listed are from the Tabelog listing (as of mid-2025); the restaurant itself advises checking its official Instagram (@spicehour) before visiting as hours may change. Cash only. Reservation via Instagram DM required. No English-language menu or signage confirmed. GF and vegan ratings reflect the absence of any accommodation information — this is an omnivore spice bar and should not be assumed safe for celiac or strictly vegan diners without prior direct inquiry.

name_ja: 和のフードホール hosa（蓬左）
cuisine: Japanese traditional cuisine, wagashi sweets, kaiseki, kaisen-don
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸1-1-1 名古屋能楽堂1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch to 愛知県名古屋市中区三の丸一丁目１番; approximately 450m southwest of Nagoya Castle main keep, within target radius.
neighborhood: 中区三の丸（名古屋城周辺）
seats_text: 112 seats across food hall tables, counter, seven private rooms, and a large banquet hall; Tabelog lists 30 table seats (standing capacity 40) for the main hall area alone.
hours_raw: 10:00–17:00 (last order 16:30), daily; evening service (17:00–21:00) available by reservation for groups of 10+ at ¥8,800/person
cultural_comfort_note: Located in a traditional Noh theatre with a refined, heritage-focused atmosphere; staff are welcoming but the setting is distinctly Japanese cultural — some menu items and signage may be Japanese-only.
vegan_detail: The kitchen is built around fish-based dashi as its philosophical core — 100 bonito fillets are hand-shaved every morning for the kishimen broth. Wagashi sweets use red bean paste, eggs, butter, and dairy. Seafood bowls feature fresh fish and shellfish. No plant-based or vegan dishes are offered, and hidden dashi (katsuo/katsuobushi) is present throughout the menu.
gf_detail: Gluten is pervasive: kishimen is a wheat-flour flat noodle (the signature dish), soy sauce (wheat-containing) is used in dashi preparations and dressings, and tempura dishes appear on the kaiseki menu. No gluten-free labeling, dedicated fryer, or allergy accommodation system has been documented. The afternoon tea set includes wheat-based pastries. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: Hōsa opened March 18, 2022 inside the Nagoya Noh Theatre (名古屋能楽堂) at the main gate of Nagoya Castle. It was conceived as a 食楽堂 (shokurakudō — a food-pleasure hall) with a mission to carry Owari-Nagoya's 400-year culinary heritage into the future by uniting three Edo-period Nagoya institutions under one roof.
bio.origin: Sannomaru, Naka Ward, Nagoya — the historic castle precinct of the former Owari Tokugawa domain
bio.specialty: Kishimen & seafood bowl (きしめんと海鮮丼 half-and-half, ¥1,600) and the Japanese Afternoon Tea Set (和フタヌーンティーセット, ¥4,400) served on a Nagoya Castle-motif three-tiered stand
bio.philosophy: Three heritage brands — the 300-year-old catering house Yaohiko Honten, the Tokugawa clan's official confectioner Kasho Hanakikyo (founded ~400 years ago), and Nagomi Kashiya Nakamata (descended from an Edo-period Atsuta seafood business) — collaborate to present classic Nagoya flavors in a setting worthy of the castle district, while also innovating with items like the 'ふわふわ わぬき' butter dorayaki (sold with a two-minute best-before window, as the butter melts rapidly).

anecdotes.text: Every morning the kitchen hand-shaves 100 sticks of dried bonito (katsuobushi) to make the day's stock — this fresh-drawn dashi is considered the soul of the kishimen broth.
The butter dorayaki 'ふわふわ わぬき' carries an unofficial best-before time of two minutes — the butter melts into the still-warm pancake so quickly that eating it at the counter is essentially required.
Head chef Kondo Toshio is an officially recognized Aichi Prefecture Traditional Craftsman and also leads the restaurant at the Tokugawa Art Museum.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols is publicly available. The kitchen handles kishimen wheat noodles, tempura, and wheat-based soy sauce as daily staples — shared preparation surfaces are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard (wheat-containing) soy sauce is used in dashi preparations, dipping sauces, and the kaiseki courses. No tamari or gluten-free shoyu alternative has been mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (katsuobushi) is central to the kitchen philosophy and present in broths, sauces, and likely wagashi fillings. No vegan-safe preparation line exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No documented allergy-handling policy or allergen chart was found. Phone reservations are available (052-253-8566); contacting ahead of any visit is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

positives.text: All seating is non-smoking throughout the venue.
Wheelchair accessible with nearby dedicated parking (308 regular spaces at the Seimon-mae lot).

caveats: Hours and closed-day data are consistent across Tabelog and multiple blog sources but should be verified directly as the venue is inside a public facility (Nagoya Noh Theatre) that may impose its own closure schedule beyond January 1st. Seat count discrepancy: Tabelog lists 30 table seats for the main food-hall area; the press release total of 112 counts all areas including private rooms and the banquet hall. No allergen documentation is publicly available — contact the restaurant directly before visiting with any dietary restriction.

name_ja: やきとり 風月 名古屋本店
neighborhood: 西区那古野（四間道） / 国際センター駅3分
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-18-13
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-18-13; coordinates [136.890854, 35.174767]; approx 1.44 km from Nagoya Castle centroid — within 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 41 seats total; counter plus multiple private and semi-private rooms seating 2–24 guests.
hours_raw: 月〜土 17:30〜22:30（料理LO 21:30、ドリンクLO 22:00）；日曜・祝日 定休
cultural_comfort_note: Menu is Japanese-only; one TripAdvisor reviewer noted staff were helpful and tried to translate, but Japanese ability will make the experience smoother.
vegan_detail: This is a dedicated yakitori (grilled chicken) restaurant with a kaiseki-style course format built around chicken — Nagoya Cochin and Mikawa red chicken are the centerpiece. Courses include chicken tataki, liver, gizzard, heart, and finishing noodles. Vegetable side dishes (seasonal vegetables with house miso) exist but function as accompaniments. There are no vegan courses and no indication the kitchen can build a vegan meal on request. Hidden animal products are virtually certain throughout.
gf_detail: Yakitori is grilled on binchotan charcoal, which in principle is a wheat-free cooking method, but standard Japanese tare (dipping sauce) and soy sauce almost certainly contain wheat. Ponzu and other dressings used in sides likely contain soy sauce (wheat). Finishing dishes — tantanmen, chicken ramen, dan dan noodles — contain wheat noodles. No gluten-free menu, no allergen notation on Tabelog or HotPepper, and no dedicated fryer information found. A celiac diner would need to inquire carefully in advance; the kitchen may be able to offer salt-seasoned (塩) skewers only, but cross-contamination from tare and shared prep areas cannot be ruled out without direct confirmation.
caveats: Hours confirmed from multiple sources as Mon–Sat 17:30–22:30; LO 21:30 food. Official website has TLS certificate issue and could not be directly verified. Under-30 rating is "no" based on course pricing (¥5,500–¥7,700 per person) and kaiseki-style format indicating an upscale adult dining experience well above a casual budget.

bio.background: The restaurant is operated by 合資会社宮治 (Miyaji Ltd.), a small company behind a small group of Nagoya dining concepts. The honten (main store) occupies a renovated traditional machiya townhouse — reportedly around 100 years old — on a quiet back lane one street removed from the main avenue in the Shikankaidō (四間道) district of Nagono, Nishi Ward.
bio.specialty: Kaiseki-style yakitori using Nagoya Cochin (名古屋コーチン) and Mikawa red chicken (三河赤鶏), grilled over binchotan white charcoal and served one plate at a time in the manner of a formal kaiseki meal.
bio.philosophy: Described by the operator as 'a luxurious yakitori restaurant for adults who want to enjoy delicious things in small portions and variety,' incorporating seasonality into grilled chicken. The hideaway location — tucked off the main road in the historic Shikankaidō streetscape — is intentional, creating a private, calm atmosphere suited to dates and business entertaining.
bio.origin: Nishi Ward, Nagoya; Shikankaidō (四間道) district, a preserved Edo-period merchant townscape near Kokusai Center Station.

anecdotes.text: A reviewer from Hong Kong visiting in 2018 noted that between each skewer course, non-yakitori dishes such as tofu and salad were served — an unexpected kaiseki rhythm — and that the staff 'were very helpful and friendly and tried to translate,' making the experience accessible even without Japanese. The wing tip was singled out as 'very yummy.'

Japanese reviewers cite the contrast of crispy-exterior, juicy-interior skewers as the kitchen's defining technical achievement — attributed to slow binchotan grilling — and highlight the cheese appetizer and Nagoya Cochin tataki as standout non-skewer dishes.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols is publicly available. The kitchen handles wheat-containing tare sauce, soy sauce-based dressings, and wheat noodle finishing dishes in the same space as the grill — cross-contamination risk for celiacs must be assumed until directly confirmed otherwise.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard tare (焼き鳥のたれ) used on skewers almost certainly contains soy sauce brewed with wheat. Sesame-ponzu dishes and other à la carte items also likely use wheat-containing soy sauce. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce option is mentioned anywhere across sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built around chicken and meat; animal products are present in every course element including broths, tare, and finishing noodles. No plant-only preparation area or vegan accommodation is referenced in any source.

staff_allergy_handling.text: One non-Japanese reviewer noted staff attempted to assist with translation and communication at the table, suggesting some goodwill toward guests with special needs. However, no formal allergen protocol or allergy-handling procedure is documented in any public source.

positives.text: Salt-seasoned (塩) yakitori skewers — if available and confirmed wheat-free — could in theory be the lowest-risk option for gluten-sensitive (non-celiac) diners, as plain charcoal grilling of chicken with salt involves no obvious wheat-containing ingredients.

cuisine: インド・ネパール料理、バー
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-8-25 丸の内中野ビル 2F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 名古屋市中区丸の内二丁目８番２５号; approximately 1.10 km from Nagoya Castle target point — within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（丸の内駅2番出口より徒歩4分）
seats_text: カウンター9席、テーブル32席。貸切対応20〜50名
cultural_comfort_note: 日本語環境のインド・ネパール料理店。スパイスレベル0〜5で注文できるなど日本語での説明・対応が中心で、英語対応の明示はない。
vegan_detail: Tabelog上でベジタリアンカレーオプションの言及あり。ただし主力はバターチキンカレー・チキンティッカなど乳製品・肉を使ったメニュー。ナンにも乳製品が含まれる可能性が高く、完全ヴィーガン対応の明示はない。ライス変更でヴィーガン寄りの注文は可能な場合があるが、要確認。
gf_detail: インドカレーのベースは小麦粉不使用が多いが、ナンは小麦グルテンを含む。グルテンフリー対応プログラムや専用メニューの公式記載なし。ライスへの変更で小麦回避の可能性はあるが、厨房内の交差汚染リスクや醤油使用状況（チキンサデコなどネパール料理）は不明。セリアック病の方は来店前に必ず電話確認を。

bio.background: 2025年6月30日にグランドオープンした、名古屋・丸の内のビル2階に構える隠れ家型のインド・ネパール料理店兼バー。昼は本格スパイスカレー、夜はウイスキー・ワイン30種以上を揃えるバータイムという二刀流のコンセプトを掲げる。スパイスレベルは0〜5段階で選べ、辛さが苦手な人にも対応。本場の料理人がインド・ネパールの味を追求しながら、日本人が食べやすいように仕上げている。
bio.specialty: スパイスカレー（ナンおかわり自由）＋炭火焼きチキン（フランゴ）
bio.philosophy: ランチは本格スパイスカレー、夜はしっとりバータイム。一人でも仲間とでも使えるオアシス的空間を目指す。
roles: インド・ネパール料理シェフ

anecdotes.text: グランドオープン当日のInstagram投稿で「ついに本日！TRIVENI(ティリヴェニ)グランドオープンしました」と開店を告知。昼カレー・夜バーの二刀流コンセプトを強調。
ランチのナンは驚きのビッグサイズで、地元メディア（しゃちほこニュース）でも「写真で伝わる？」と紹介されるほどのインパクト。おかわり自由のナンと本場カレーが丸の内ランチの新名物に。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテン（小麦）の交差汚染リスクについて公式情報なし。ナン（小麦粉使用）と同一厨房での調理が想定されるため、セリアック病患者への安全確認は電話問い合わせが必須。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: カレーへの醤油使用は確認されていないが、チキンサデコ等のネパール料理に醤油系調味料が用いられる可能性がある。公式のアレルゲン情報は未開示。

vegan_cross_contact.text: バターチキンカレー等の乳製品を使ったメニューと同一厨房での調理。ヴィーガン向けの分離調理についての記載なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応や食制限への対応について公式サイト・口コミ上に明示的な記載なし。来店前の電話確認（052-253-7874）を推奨。

positives.text: スパイスレベルを0〜5段階で選択可能で、カスタマイズ性は高い。ライスへの変更注文が可能で、小麦（ナン）を避けた選択肢が存在する可能性がある。
ベジタリアンカレーオプションの存在がTabelog上で言及されており、植物性食材を優先する食事の起点となりうる。

caveats: 2025年6月30日開業の新しい店舗で口コミ数が少ない（Tabelog上6件、評価3.07）。グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応の詳細は現時点で公式情報が乏しく、実際の安全性確認には電話問い合わせが必要。公式独立サイトは確認されず、HotPepperとTabelog経由での予約が主。土曜夜は10名以上の予約制。

neighborhood: 名西 / 浄心 (Nishi Ward)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区名西2丁目10番10号-1
geocode_note: GSI block-level geocode places the restaurant at 1,614.6m from the Nagoya Castle reference point — approximately 5m outside the 1.61km radius. This is a block-level (丁目) estimate; the actual building entrance may be marginally closer. Tabelog categorizes this listing under the target area code A230109 (東大手/清水). Flagged for human review.
gf_detail: Standard Edomae sushi; conventional wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu) is used for neta glazing and dipping — a direct gluten source. Dashi in simmered dishes almost certainly uses conventional ingredients. No GF shoyu alternative, dedicated preparation, or allergy accommodation is mentioned anywhere on the official site or third-party listings. Celiac diners should call in advance, though accommodation is unlikely given the fixed omakase format.
vegan_detail: Pure omakase sushi featuring fish, shellfish, and seafood. No plant-based course or substitution is offered. Shari (rice) uses fish-stock-influenced preparation context; dishes include clams and tuna as signature items. Not viable for vegans.
seats_text: 8 counter seats only; maximum party 9
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-only omakase with a strict etiquette code — no strong fragrances, no wrist jewelry at the counter; reservations and communication are primarily in Japanese.

bio.background: Naoki Shimizu trained in Tokyo before returning to Nagoya to open his own counter. The original location in Naka Ward (Shin-Sakae) opened in June 2017; in May 2022 he relocated to the current address in Nishi Ward, creating a quieter, more intimate hidden-gem setting near Joshin Station.
bio.specialty: Edomae omakase course of approximately 10–17 pieces; signature items include a karasumi (bottarga) hand roll and Kuwana simmered clams. The shari uses locally grown Aichi-prefecture rice ('Aichino Kaori') seasoned with a proprietary three-vinegar blend — two types of red vinegar combined with white vinegar.
bio.philosophy: Shimizu wants guests to 'purely enjoy food' without preconception, pursuing flavour beyond conventional boundaries while honouring Edomae tradition. He sources seafood daily from Ise Bay and Mikawa Bay, and premium tuna from specialist wholesaler Yamako.

anecdotes.text: The counter has a strict no-jewellery and no-fragrance rule — Shimizu considers the polished hinoki wood surface and the sensory purity of the meal inseparable.

The restaurant was awarded a Michelin Plate in the Michelin Guide Aichi/Gifu/Mie 2019 Special Edition and won Tabelog Award 2025 Bronze, placing it among the top Edomae sushi counters in the Nagoya region.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination controls. The kitchen handles fish, shellfish, and wheat-based soy sauce throughout service with no stated separation protocols.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is used for neta glaze and accompaniment — a direct and unavoidable gluten source in the omakase format as served.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is fish- and seafood-based; no plant-only preparation stream exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy or dietary accommodation policy is published. The fixed omakase format leaves little room for substitution; guests with serious allergies should contact the restaurant directly before booking.

caveats: Distance caveat: GSI block-level geocode places this address at 1,614.6m from the Nagoya Castle reference — roughly 5m outside the 1.61km threshold. The margin is within geocoding error for a block-level estimate. Tabelog's own area classification (A230109 = 東大手/清水) includes this listing in the target zone. Human review recommended before inclusion. GF caveat: wheat-based soy sauce is integral to the omakase; no GF option is available.

name_ja: 会席料理・ひつまぶし 雅（MASA）
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区泉1-2-17 レジデンス泉1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch for 名古屋市東区泉1-2-17. Coordinates 35.177998, 136.909149 are within the target bounding box and 1.205km from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997), inside the 1.61km radius. The restaurant is in Higashi Ward (東区), not 中区栄 as noted in the target description, but the Hisaya-Odori area straddles both wards and the location is confirmed within range.
neighborhood: 久屋大通・泉エリア（東区）
cuisine: ひつまぶし・会席料理（うなぎ）
vegan_detail: The menu centers on domestic Japanese eel (unagi) grilled over binchotan charcoal, served as hitsumabushi at lunch and as the centerpiece of kaiseki courses at dinner. No plant-based or vegan alternatives are listed. Traditional Japanese dashi (likely made with fish stock/bonito) is standard in kaiseki cuisine, and eel is the defining ingredient throughout the menu. Vegan diners cannot be accommodated here.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or allergy accommodation information is published on Tabelog, the official site, or any aggregator page. Traditional Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is almost certainly used in the eel tare (sauce) and kaiseki preparations. The eel is grilled with a house-made sauce and served with Koshihikari rice, but the sauce composition is not disclosed. No dedicated fryer information available. Celiac-safety cannot be confirmed; wheat exposure via tare/soy sauce and hidden dashi is highly likely. Diners with serious gluten sensitivity should call ahead: 052-228-3128.
seats_text: 18 seats total: 8 counter seats plus two private rooms (one for 4, one for 6 guests)
cultural_comfort_note: A refined kaiseki and eel specialist in a quiet Nagoya side street; staff communication is in Japanese and the dining style — private rooms, counter omakase — assumes familiarity with formal Japanese dining conventions.

bio.background: Opened in April 2024 in a discreet first-floor space in Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a short walk from Hisaya-Odori Station. The restaurant bills itself as a 隠れ家 (kakurega — hidden retreat), blending traditional techniques with a contemporary sensibility in a compact 18-seat room.
bio.specialty: Hitsumabushi — domestic Japanese eel hand-filleted and grilled over binchotan charcoal, served over clay-pot-cooked Koshihikari rice from Niigata's Minamiuonuma region; dinner shifts to seasonal kaiseki courses with charcoal-grilled preparations throughout.
bio.philosophy: Refined yet approachable Japanese cuisine centered on the quality of domestic eel and seasonal ingredients, offered in a setting equally suited to solo counter dining and intimate private-room entertaining.

anecdotes.text: By December 2024 the restaurant marked its half-year anniversary, having quickly become a destination for hitsumabushi and kaiseki in the Hisaya-Odori neighborhood.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No cross-contamination controls are disclosed. The kitchen prepares both lunch eel service and multi-course kaiseki dinners in a compact 18-seat restaurant; shared prep surfaces are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Domestic eel is grilled with a house-made tare (sauce); traditional Japanese tare is soy-sauce based and nearly always contains wheat. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built around unagi (eel) and fish-based kaiseki preparations; there is no plant-based section and no disclosure of vegan-safe handling.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy is published on the official site, Tabelog, Hot Pepper, or any aggregator. Diners with serious allergies should call 052-228-3128 before visiting.

positives.text: The restaurant does state use of domestic Japanese eel (国産うなぎ) only, and binchotan charcoal grilling — simple, whole-ingredient cooking with minimal industrial additives in the core protein.

caveats: Address is 東区泉1-2-17 (Higashi Ward), not 中区栄 as listed in the brief — the Hisaya-Odori district straddles both wards and the spot is confirmed 1.205km from Nagoya Castle, within the 1.61km target radius. National holidays closure pattern is listed as closed on Tabelog but the official site says 祝日も営業 on some pages; verify directly before visiting on a holiday. One-drink minimum per person (age 20+) applies at dinner from October 2025.

cuisine: 手打ちそば（二八・十割）、かもなんそば、そばがき、天ぷら
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区幅下1-1-11
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区幅下一丁目１番１１号. Approx 794m from Nagoya Castle — well within 1.61km radius. Note: the target brief listed 羽栗町 as the neighbourhood, but the Tabelog listing and all sources confirm the address is 幅下1-1-11, a pre-war cotton merchant's warehouse building along the Horikawa river — consistent with the brief's "堀川沿い" description.
neighborhood: 西区幅下、堀川沿い（浅間町駅徒歩7〜9分）
seats_text: 40席。カウンター席あり、座敷あり。全席禁煙。
cultural_comfort_note: Traditional soba-ya atmosphere in a historic warehouse building; no English menu or staff expected, but ordering is simple — point at the laminated menu and indicate nibachi (standard) or juwari (100% buckwheat, +¥110).
vegan_detail: The dashi is made from a proprietary blend of katsuobushi (skipjack), mackerel, and bonito — not vegan. The tsuyu also contains soy sauce (wheat). Vegetable-only small plates (yuba sashimi, seasonal vegetables) may be available but there is no dedicated vegan menu. Guests with strict vegan requirements should call ahead; do not assume any hot dish is animal-free.
gf_detail: The standard soba is 二八 (nibachi) — roughly 80% buckwheat, 20% wheat flour — and is NOT gluten-free. A 十割 (juwari, 100% buckwheat) upgrade is offered for +¥110 and contains no wheat flour by recipe, but the noodles are made in a shared kitchen where wheat flour is handled daily. The tsuyu (dipping sauce and soup base) contains soy sauce made from wheat. Tempura batter contains wheat flour. No dedicated GF preparation has been confirmed by any source. People with celiac disease should be aware of high cross-contamination risk from shared equipment and wheat-containing dashi/tsuyu, even with the juwari noodle option.

bio.background: Owner-chef 谷幸憲 built his craft around an obsessive commitment to soba aroma and freshness. He sources buckwheat flour just once a year and grinds only what is needed the following day using a stone mill — a discipline that keeps the flour at peak fragrance. He personally eats two bowls of soba every day, one hot and one cold, to verify the quality before opening.
bio.specialty: かもなんそば (Kamo Nan Soba) — duck and Welsh onion soba, the house signature. The duck is finished in its own rendered lard with a torch for richness, and the broth is a proprietary blend of light soy, bonito, mackerel, and skipjack. The juwari (100% buckwheat) upgrade produces pale, thin noodles with a delicate aroma, best eaten with a pinch of salt before the fragrance fades.
bio.philosophy: Tani adjusts his flour ratios daily according to weather and humidity, treating each batch of noodles as a fresh decision rather than a fixed formula. He regards maximising the natural aroma of buckwheat — through cold water in the dough, stone-milled same-day flour, and minimal handling — as the central task of the craft.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi. The restaurant occupies a pre-war warehouse that once belonged to a cotton merchant (綿問屋), renovated to preserve the original high ceilings, dark wooden beams, and earthen walls along the Horikawa river near Nagoya Castle.

anecdotes.text: Tani eats 600–700 bowls of soba per year and tastes two bowls — one hot, one cold — every single day before service to calibrate the day's noodles.
He uses cold water rather than hot water when making ten-soba (100% buckwheat dough), an unconventional choice he maintains deliberately to preserve aroma.
The kamo nan soba was singled out by top chefs as a standout dish in Nagoya.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat flour is used daily in the kitchen for standard nibachi (二八) noodles and tempura batter, creating a shared-environment cross-contamination risk even when the juwari (十割) noodle option is ordered. No source confirms dedicated equipment or separate preparation areas for buckwheat-only orders.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The tsuyu (dipping broth and hot soup base) is described as containing soy sauce (醤油) blended with bonito, mackerel, and skipjack dashi. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternative has been mentioned by any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The dashi used in all soba broths is an animal-based blend (katsuobushi/skipjack, mackerel, bonito). Even the dashi-maki tamago (rolled omelet) is described as having a strong dashi flavour. There is no plant-based broth option confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information about staff allergy training or allergy-handling procedures has been found in any source. Guests with celiac disease or severe wheat allergy should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

positives.text: A 十割 (juwari, 100% buckwheat flour) noodle option is available for +¥110. By recipe the noodles contain no added wheat flour, which may suit those with mild wheat sensitivity, though celiac-level safety cannot be confirmed given the shared kitchen environment.

caveats: Hours note: the restaurant closes when soba sells out, which can happen before the posted 21:30 closing time. Closed days are Sunday every week plus the 4th Monday of each month — verify before visiting as special closures (holidays, Golden Week adjustments) have been noted. The address in the target brief listed 羽栗町 but all authoritative sources confirm 幅下1-1-11; the building is on the Horikawa riverbank as described. GF safety: the juwari upgrade makes the noodle itself wheat-free by recipe, but shared kitchen and wheat-containing tsuyu/soy sauce mean this is NOT safe for celiac diners without direct confirmation from the restaurant.

name_ja: レストラン ペルージュ
cuisine: フランス料理（オーガニック野菜使用）
neighborhood: 那古野（西区）/ 国際センター駅 徒歩3〜5分
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野1-23-9 町家1F
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch matched 愛知県名古屋市西区那古野一丁目２３番９号 → [136.891434, 35.174988]. 1,399m from Nagoya Castle target center, within 1.61km radius.
seats_text: 30席（1F 16席、2F 14席・半個室4席あり）
cultural_comfort_note: French-style fine dining operated by a Japanese chef-couple; menu and communication are in Japanese, but the Western dining format is familiar and the staff are experienced handling reservation-time allergy notifications.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu. Classic French kitchen: all stocks (fond de veau, consommé) made in-house from scratch, and house-baked bread is served — both are standard wheat risk points. Soy sauce is unlikely in this French kitchen, but cross-contamination from bread and flour-thickened sauces is a real concern. The restaurant explicitly asks guests to disclose allergies at time of reservation; same-day requests cannot be accommodated. A celiac diner should call ahead and have a detailed conversation before booking.
vegan_detail: Not a vegan-friendly restaurant. The kitchen features pigeon rôti, foie gras and rabbit pâté, veal, and wild game (gibier) as signature proteins. Organic and pesticide-free vegetables are a proud focus (~80–90% of produce), but they are used as accompaniments in a fully omnivorous French menu. No vegan course or substitution is listed or implied.

bio.chef_name: 今泉智成（いまいずみ としなり）
bio.background: 1977年大阪生まれ。祖母が料理店を営んでいたことに影響を受け料理の道へ。調理師学校卒業後、名古屋市内のホテルや料理店で和食・フランス料理の修業を積み、2008年に那古野に独立開業。
bio.origin: 2008年開業。那古野の古い町家（旧電気店）を改装した隠れ家的な2フロア構造。
bio.specialty: ランド産鳩のロティやフォアグラ・ジビエを軸とした本格フランス料理コース。無農薬野菜・天然魚介・抗生物質不使用の畜肉を使用。
bio.philosophy: 「レストランの食事は『美味しく』『楽しく』『優雅で』『身も心も癒されるようなひと時』でなければならない」。素材の美味しさを最大限に引き出すため、フォン・ド・ヴォーやコンソメなどの基礎出汁はすべて一から仕込む。オーガニック野菜との出会いはナチュラルワインへの興味から始まり、現在は使用野菜の約80〜90%が有機栽培品。

anecdotes.text: シェフは「素材がオーガニックだと最初にアピールするより、まず料理を食べて美味しいと感じてもらい、後でそれが有機野菜だったと知ってもらいたい」という姿勢を持つ。有機野菜との向き合い方はナチュラルワインを好むようになったことがきっかけで、自然な農法と自然なワイン造りの共通点に気づいたと語る。

gf_cross_contamination.text: House-baked bread is served as part of the meal, meaning wheat flour is actively used in the kitchen. Flour-thickened sauces are standard in classical French cooking. No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or protocol is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: This is a French kitchen; soy sauce is not a listed ingredient. However, no explicit confirmation that soy sauce or wheat-based condiments are absent from all preparations.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Animal proteins (pigeon, veal, foie gras, rabbit, wild game) are central to the menu. Complete separation from vegan dishes is not possible in this kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Tabelog listing explicitly states: 苦手な食材、アレルギー等はご予約時にお伝えください。当日ですと対応しかねる場合がございます。（Please inform us of food dislikes and allergies at time of reservation; same-day requests may not be accommodable.）

positives.text: All stocks and sauces made in-house from scratch by the chef, giving the kitchen full control over ingredients — facilitating custom preparation when notified in advance.
80–90% of vegetables used are certified organic or pesticide-free, sourced directly from named farms; antibiotic-free meats and wild-caught seafood used throughout.

caveats: Hours sourced from Tabelog (Mon/Thu–Sun split service); verify seasonal changes. Hitosara lists slightly wider hours (lunch to 15:00, dinner to 23:00) which may reflect an older listing — Tabelog's L.O. times are used as primary. Price range varies by source (¥3,300–¥12,000 depending on course); Tabelog mid-range used. No English-language menu confirmed; French/Japanese only. GF and vegan assessments based on menu composition and kitchen style — no direct staff interview conducted.

name_ja: 和風ラザニア専門店 Y
name_en: Wafu Razania Senmonten Wai (Japanese-Style Lasagna Specialty Shop Y)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区二の丸1-3 金シャチ横丁 宗春ゾーン
geocode_note: GSI address search for 名古屋市中区二の丸１番 returned [136.90155, 35.183643]. Distance from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) is approximately 275m — well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区二の丸 金シャチ横丁宗春ゾーン
cuisine: イタリアン・和風フュージョン
vegan_detail: No vegan options documented. The signature dish is lasagna layered with cheese-based béchamel (white sauce), tomato sauce, and Japanese condiments including Hatchomiso and soy sauce — dairy is integral. Menu also includes items like shrimp fry doria and beef tongue lasagna. No plant-based or dairy-free alternatives are mentioned on the official site, Tabelog listing, or any third-party reviews.
gf_detail: This is a wheat-pasta lasagna specialty shop — gluten is the core ingredient. All lasagna varieties are built on standard wheat pasta sheets. Soy sauce (wheat-based) is incorporated directly into the sauce blend alongside Hatchomiso. No gluten-free pasta alternative is mentioned anywhere. No dedicated fryer information found. Not safe for celiac diners.
seats_text: 88 seats total: approximately 60 indoor table seats plus 28 covered terrace seats. Private group bookings available for parties of 20–50+.
cultural_comfort_note: English menus are available; the venue sits inside Kinshachi Yokocho, a tourist-oriented food complex adjacent to Nagoya Castle, designed for broad accessibility including international visitors.

bio.background: Chef Shinohara Hidekazu spent 18 years in Milan, where he trained under Nobuyuki Matsuhisa (NOBU) at NOBU Armani and went on to open Zero Milano — a creative sushi restaurant whose clientele included soccer legend Roberto Baggio. His Milan-era work earned him a place in Who's Who: Chefs, a US publication that ranks the world's top 100 culinary professionals. He later reverse-imported his fusion concept to Japan with Zero Tokyo in Ginza (2016), before launching this lasagna-focused concept at Kinshachi Yokocho in July 2023.
bio.specialty: 和風ラザニア (Japanese-style lasagna) — six-layer wheat pasta sheets with tomato sauce, béchamel, Hatchomiso, and soy sauce, in rotating varieties such as Salmon & Avocado, Mixed Mushroom, Beef Tongue & Olive, and Whitebait & Lemon Pepper from Shikoji Island (Aichi Prefecture)
bio.philosophy: Shinohara built the concept on a conviction that Italian and Japanese culinary sensibilities share deep common ground — both prize seasonality, craftsmanship, and umami. The shop name Y encodes the fusion: 和 (wa, Japanese) + 伊 (i, Italian) = Y. He positions lasagna not as foreign import but as a form that Japanese ingredients inhabit naturally.

anecdotes.text: Roberto Baggio was among the celebrity regulars at Shinohara's Zero Milano in Italy, drawn by his Japanese-Italian fusion cuisine long before the concept returned to Japan.
The shop name Y is a visual shorthand for the fusion: combining the kanji 和 (wa = Japanese) and 伊 (i = Italian) produces the letter Y — a bilingual pun encoded in a single character.

Kinshachi Yokocho opened the Muneharuzone in 2019 adjacent to Nagoya Castle's Ninomaru grounds; 和風ラザニア専門店 Y joined the complex at its July 2023 opening, part of a wave of concept restaurants targeting castle-area tourists.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat pasta is the foundational ingredient of every dish; cross-contamination throughout the kitchen is near-certain. No separate preparation areas for allergen-free cooking are documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce (which contains wheat) is used directly in the lasagna sauce blend alongside Hatchomiso. Wheat is present in both the pasta layers and the sauce itself — there is no wheat-free version of any menu item.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy (béchamel/white sauce, cheese) and meat proteins are core to the menu; no vegan preparation track exists. Cross-contact with animal products is unavoidable.

caveats: Hours subject to irregular closures (不定休) — confirm before visiting. The independent flag is set to false because the shop operates within the Kinshachi Yokocho managed food complex, not as a standalone independent establishment. Chef Shinohara's name appeared on Tabelog as 篠原秀和 but the Lemon8 source refers to the Milan restaurant as "Zero Milano" while Tabelog originally mentioned "NOBU Milano" — these are reconciled: Shinohara trained at NOBU Armani Milan then opened his own Zero Milano. Seating count varies slightly across sources (88 vs 90); 88 used as Tabelog figure.

cuisine: 日本料理・京料理 (Japanese / Kyoto Kaiseki)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市東区東片端町24 ハセガワビル1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 東片端町24番地; 1.43 km from Nagoya Castle, within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: 東区 東片端町、高岳駅徒歩8分
seats_text: 7-seat counter plus a small number of table seats; half-private seating available; no full private rooms
cultural_comfort_note: Intimate counter-style kaiseki restaurant oriented toward Japanese business entertaining; the chef interacts with guests in Japanese, and the menu is entirely in Japanese with no English materials noted.
vegan_detail: Classic Kyoto kaiseki built around dashi (kombu and katsuobushi), seasonal fish, Nagoya Cochin poultry, and soft-shell turtle. Kyoto vegetables and obanzai-style simmered dishes feature prominently but are cooked in fish-based broths. No vegan course exists; advance inquiry is essential and outcomes are uncertain.
gf_detail: Wheat-containing soy sauce (koikuchi shoyu) is a staple of Kyoto kaiseki and almost certainly present in marinades, braises, and dipping sauces. Tempura (wheat batter) appears on the à la carte menu. No gluten-free claims, tamari use, or allergen menu exist on the official site or review pages. Celiac diners should inquire directly by phone before booking; outcomes are unverified.

bio.background: Chisoan Hijiri is an intimate Kyoto-style kaiseki restaurant in Nagoya's Higashi Ward, steps from the Higashikatahata intersection. The chef trained in Kyoto before opening here, bringing authentic techniques and a personal procurement network that sources Kyoto vegetables, seasonal fish, and specialty items directly.
bio.specialty: Soft-shell turtle (suppon) — available year-round in multiple preparations including sashimi, chawanmushi, karaage, and hot pot — alongside a rotating kaiseki menu built on whatever the chef sources each day.
bio.philosophy: Seasonal, ingredient-led cooking in a calm atmosphere modelled on Kyoto aesthetics: the interior uses lattice woodwork imported from Kyoto. The menu changes daily based on procurement, and the chef occasionally offers surprise off-menu dishes to counter guests.
bio.origin: Kyoto culinary tradition transplanted to Nagoya

anecdotes.text: Fellow chefs praise the restaurant's Kyoto training lineage and describe the plating as 'distinctively creative' (盛り付けがとてもきれいで独創性があり).
The restaurant has been selected as a Tabelog Japanese Cuisine EAST 百名店 (Top 100) in 2021, 2023, and 2025 — an unusually consistent run for a small counter in a provincial city.
A food blogger describes sourcing wild ayu (sweetfish) from the Wara River in Gifu Prefecture, calling it 'Japan's finest ayu', reflecting the chef's reach beyond standard market channels.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on cross-contamination controls; the kitchen prepares wheat-battered tempura and standard soy-sauce-based dishes.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat-based soy sauce is standard throughout Kyoto kaiseki cooking and appears inherent to the braises, simmered dishes, and seasoning at this restaurant; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is noted anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi made from katsuobushi (bonito flakes) is the backbone of Kyoto kaiseki; fish and poultry are central to the menu, making vegan cross-contact virtually certain without specific advance arrangement.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy-handling policy, or gluten-free accommodation is mentioned on the official site or any reviewed source; the restaurant takes reservations by phone (052-963-0073) and dietary needs should be communicated at the time of booking.

positives.text: Courses are prepared to order in small batches at a 7-seat counter, giving the chef direct visibility over every dish and some flexibility to accommodate requests if communicated well in advance.

caveats: Hours confirmed as 17:00-23:00 (LO 22:30) from the official site and blog sources; the Tabelog reservation calendar showed a broader 10:00-24:00 window which likely reflects the booking system's range, not actual opening hours. Seat count of 7 is from a blog review and may not reflect the total capacity including table seating. Chef name not publicly disclosed on any source found.

name_ja: 肉うどん さんすけ
neighborhood: 丸の内
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-8-30 竹中ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI address-search on 丸の内2-8-30; coordinates fall within the target box and approximately 1.05 km from Nagoya Castle — within the 1.61 km radius.
seats_text: 15席（カウンターあり）
closed_days: 年末年始（月曜定休なし、年中無休に近い）
cultural_comfort_note: Cash-only counter shop with a local-regular feel; staff are described as attentive and happy to explain the menu, but all signage and interaction is in Japanese.
vegan_detail: Broth is made with pork bone, chicken oil (chiyu), and animal-based dashi — no plant-based option available. Toppings include chashu pork as the centerpiece. Nothing on the menu is suitable for vegans or vegetarians.
gf_detail: The restaurant's core product is handmade wheat udon noodles. The broth uses Imokei tamari soy sauce (from Gifu Prefecture), which is often lower-gluten than standard shoyu, but the shop is entirely built around wheat noodles and there is no gluten-free alternative offered. Cross-contamination is certain. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: Owner Katō spent years training in ramen rather than udon — he worked at the legendary Ramen Jiro Mita main branch, then ran the Ramen Jiro Ōmiya offshoot for eight years before pivoting to udon in Nagoya. He opened this counter in October 2017 at age 29, channeling his ramen pedigree into a bowl that straddles both worlds.
bio.specialty: さんすけうどん — handmade thick flat noodles in a rich pork-and-tamari-soy broth topped with chashu, spinach, nori, and self-serve ginger and green onion
bio.philosophy: Always satisfy customers' appetites (お客様のお腹を常に満たす). Katō applies ramen-kitchen discipline — three-day marinading and low-temperature pork cooking — to an udon format, producing generous portions that reviewers describe as addictive despite their richness.

anecdotes.text: The shop's name, さんすけ, references Oda Nobunaga's childhood name (三介), a nod to the historical Nagoya connection — a playful local anchor for what is otherwise a very non-traditional udon.
A food blogger visiting in summer 2025 noted customers eating with smiles and laughter — a warm contrast to the tense, almost martial atmosphere of the Ramen Jiro style the owner came from.
The broth prompted one writer to ask 'Is this just Yokohama ie-kei ramen?' — the tamari-soy, pork-bone, chicken-oil combination reads unmistakably ramen to veteran noodle-hunters.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat noodles are the restaurant's entire product; flour is present throughout the kitchen. No gluten-free alternative exists and cross-contamination is unavoidable.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The broth uses Imokei tamari soy sauce from Gifu Prefecture. Tamari is typically made with little or no wheat, but this has not been confirmed as certified gluten-free and is served in a wheat-noodle kitchen.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pork chashu, chicken oil, and animal dashi are core broth components. There is no vegan path through the menu.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Staff are noted as patient and thorough in explaining the menu to first-time visitors, but no allergy or dietary accommodation protocols have been documented by any source.

caveats: Hours vary by day of week with different start times (8:00 vs 11:00); confirm before visiting as a 2025 blog noted Sunday is lunch-only. Cash only — no cards, IC, or QR. No reservations taken.

name_ja: グラッツィエ・ディ・クオーレ
neighborhood: 浄心駅 徒歩5分 (西区上名古屋)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋2-3-7
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 愛知県名古屋市西区上名古屋2-3-7 returned [136.893143, 35.192459]. Approx 0.97km from Nagoya Castle (35.1856, 136.8997) — within the 1.61km target radius.
seats_text: Seating capacity not published; private room available (extra fee). Online and phone reservations accepted.
cultural_comfort_note: A neighborhood Italian with local regulars and no evident English signage or menu; basic Japanese phrases will get you far, and staff are described as friendly and welcoming.
vegan_detail: No vegan designation. The menu is built around meat, cheese, seafood, and egg-enriched pasta (carbonara uses Nagoya Cochin egg). Organic vegetables appear throughout — salads, roasted vegetables, and sides — but no dish is explicitly vegan. The restaurant notes it accommodates ingredient-substitution requests at time of visit, so it is worth asking, but no vegan commitment is made.
gf_detail: No gluten-free offering is listed or advertised. All pizzas and fresh pasta are wheat-based; the restaurant's own website highlights Italian wheat flour (イタリア産小麦) as a point of pride in its pizza dough. There is no dedicated GF kitchen, no mention of GF flour alternatives, and no allergen menu. Cross-contamination from flour-heavy cooking is a real concern. The restaurant does accept allergy-substitution requests verbally, but suitability for celiac diners has not been established.

bio.background: Born in Tokyo in 1982, Terasawa Takashi discovered a love of cooking during part-time work at a family restaurant chain, where he stayed for eight years. He then spent three years at a pizza specialty shop, followed by three years at an Italian restaurant, before being entrusted with the kitchen at Grazie di Cuore.
bio.specialty: Hand-made pizza on Italian wheat dough rested for half a day, fired to a crisp exterior and chewy interior; house-made roast beef served alongside mashed potato.
bio.philosophy: Simple, never-tiring cuisine — シンプルで飽きのこない料理 — adapted so that Japanese diners of all ages feel at home with Italian food.
bio.origin: Opened November 2018 in Joshin, Nishi-ku, Nagoya.

anecdotes.text: Terasawa's pizzas became so sought-after by fellow restaurateurs that the shop launched a frozen-pizza wholesale line — now supplying Italian restaurants, cafes, izakayas, and bars across the region.

The chef's route into professional cooking began entirely through family-restaurant part-time work; his eight years there gave him the fundamentals before he moved to specialist pizza and Italian training.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen works extensively with Italian wheat flour for fresh pasta and pizza dough; no GF-separated preparation area or equipment is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce-based dishes are evident on the Italian menu, but standard wheat-containing Italian ingredients (pasta dough, pizza dough) are used throughout.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Cheese, prosciutto, and animal-based ingredients appear in nearly every menu section; no vegan-safe prep line is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant states it accepts ingredient-substitution requests for allergies or dietary preferences at the time of visit (要相談).

positives.text: Organic vegetables (有機野菜) are used throughout the menu in salads, pasta, and side dishes.

caveats: Hours are confirmed from multiple sources as of mid-2025; Thursday closure is consistent. Seating count is not published. GF and vegan status are low-confidence: this is a standard Italian trattoria with no dietary-focus marketing. The chef name (寺澤昴志) comes from a single trade-equipment profile page and could not be independently corroborated on the restaurant's own site.

address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内3-7-25 中文ビル1F
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch matched 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内三丁目７番２５号; approximately 1,224m from Nagoya Castle reference point, within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内・久屋大通
seats_text: 31 seats total: 7 counter seats and approximately 24 table seats across four table areas
closed_days: 日曜・祝日・祝日の翌月曜（年始1月1-3日も休み）
cultural_comfort_note: A quiet, polished soba-ya in a business district with no English signage noted; an English menu is reportedly available, but interaction will be primarily in Japanese.
vegan_detail: Soba noodles themselves can be ordered without animal products, but the tsuyu (dipping broth) is almost certainly based on katsuobushi (bonito) and/or conventional Japanese dashi — no vegan broth option is advertised. Signature dishes include kamo-nanban (duck broth) and pork dipping soba; tempura is on the menu. Vegans should call ahead to ask whether a plain cold soba with no dipping sauce or a substitute is possible.
gf_detail: The restaurant specialises in 十割そば (juwari soba) — 100% buckwheat noodles, which contain no wheat flour. This is a genuine option for gluten-avoiders. However, the standard offering also includes 二八そば (niwari, 20% wheat) and 更科そば (sarashina, also wheat-blended), so you must explicitly request the 十割. The tsuyu is made with a 20-year-old kaeshi base using shoyu from Shinshu (Nagano) — standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat. Cross-contamination risk exists because wheat-containing soba is kneaded and cut in the same kitchen. No dedicated gluten-free kitchen is claimed. Tempura is served, meaning wheat flour and shared frying oil are present. Suitable for gluten-preference only, not celiac safety.

bio.background: Opened on 27 March 2006 in a ground-floor space of the Nakabun Building in Nagoya's Marunouchi office district. The owner-operator hand-rolls every portion of soba each morning using stone-milled buckwheat ground fresh daily. The shop's kaeshi (soy-sauce reduction that forms the tsuyu base) has been continuously replenished since opening and is now over 20 years old.
bio.specialty: Juwari soba (十割そば) — 100% buckwheat noodles; also kamo-nanban (duck broth soba) and seasonal tempura courses
bio.philosophy: The owner selects buckwheat from multiple origins, balancing hulled and unhulled grain ratios and adjusting the stone-mill technique to suit each harvest's characteristics. The guiding idea is to coax the maximum aroma and flavour from the grain itself, with a tsuyu built on a single carefully chosen Shinshu shoyu that has not changed since the shop opened.

anecdotes.text: The shop's kaeshi has been continuously replenished since the 2006 opening and is now over 20 years old — a hallmark of a traditional soba-ya.
An early food-blog review noted that the 100% buckwheat soba was available as a small upcharge option even in the shop's early years, with pork and duck dipping broths as the signature warm options.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat-containing niwari (80% buckwheat / 20% wheat) and sarashina soba are made in the same kitchen as the juwari (100% buckwheat) soba; shared surfaces and equipment are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The tsuyu is built on a 20-year-old kaeshi made with Shinshu shoyu — standard Japanese soy sauce brewed with wheat. All broth-based and dipping dishes will contain wheat-derived soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Tempura is on the menu, meaning wheat-batter frying takes place in the kitchen. Duck (kamo) and pork dipping broths are signature items, so animal products are central to the cooking environment.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy statement, or staff allergy-handling protocol has been published on any source reviewed. Guests with serious allergies should call 052-951-1226 before visiting.

positives.text: Juwari soba (十割そば, 100% buckwheat, no wheat flour in the noodle itself) is a standing menu option and represents a genuine wheat-free noodle choice for those avoiding gluten by preference.

caveats: Official website (soba-sougen.com) was unreachable (ECONNREFUSED) at time of research; content from the /about page was retrieved via Google's cached description in search snippets. Hours and payment confirmed across multiple aggregators (Tabelog, HotPepper, Gurunavi). No allergy or dietary accommodation information has been published by the restaurant on any platform. Juwari soba availability confirmed but celiac safety cannot be assured given shared kitchen environment.

neighborhood: 浅間町 / 押切 (Nishi Ward)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市西区押切1-3-1 第二伊藤ビル 1F
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI address search for 愛知県名古屋市西区押切一丁目３番１号. Coordinates place the restaurant approximately 1.34 km west of Nagoya Castle, within the 1.61 km target radius.
gf_detail: The menu includes both 二八そば (nihachi: 80% buckwheat, 20% wheat — not gluten-free) and 十割そば (juwari: 100% buckwheat — wheat-free as a flour). However, this is a general soba kitchen with no dedicated gluten-free protocol confirmed. The dashi broth uses bonito (likely with standard soy sauce containing wheat). Tempura is also on the menu, introducing wheat batter and shared-fryer risk. Celiacs should call ahead; the juwari soba is the only potentially lower-wheat option and cross-contamination cannot be ruled out.
vegan_detail: The dashi stock is bonito-based (katsuo dashi), making the standard soba broth non-vegan. The tsuyu served with cold seiro soba is also fish-based. Duck dishes are on the menu. Vegetable tempura options exist but share the kitchen. No plant-based broth alternative is mentioned anywhere. Vegans should ask if a konbu-only dashi is available; assume not unless confirmed.
seats_text: 24 seats: four 4-seat tables and two 4-seat raised tatami platforms
hours_raw: 月火木金土日 11:00-14:30 / 18:00-21:00、水曜定休、毎月第3木曜日休み
cultural_comfort_note: A quiet neighborhood soba-ya with no English signage or menu noted; a few Japanese phrases for ordering or pointing at the menu is enough to get by.

bio.specialty: Hand-rolled nihachi (80/20 buckwheat-wheat) and juwari (100% buckwheat) seiro soba; duck nanban seiro and tempura seiro are the standout dishes.
bio.philosophy: A calm, unhurried neighborhood soba shop where the noodles are rolled fresh daily and the atmosphere encourages lingering over soba-yu at the end of the meal.

anecdotes.text: Despite being a small shop, diners line up at the door by the 6 pm dinner opening — a reliable sign of neighborhood loyalty.
A soba researcher visiting in 2024 noted the mild, pale-coloured broth and hand-rolled noodles approximately 130–140 g per serving, and left wishing they had asked about the buckwheat's origin.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area is documented. The kitchen handles both wheat-containing nihachi noodles and tempura batter alongside the 100% buckwheat juwari soba, making cross-contamination a realistic concern for celiacs.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The tsuyu dipping broth and kakejiru hot broth are almost certainly made with standard soy sauce (which contains wheat) and bonito dashi — no wheat-free tamari alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito-based dashi is the standard broth throughout; duck dishes are prepared in the same kitchen. No plant-based alternative broth has been confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen handling procedures or staff allergy awareness information has been published for this restaurant. Phone ahead (052-522-5660) for any dietary requirements.

positives.text: The juwari (十割) seiro uses 100% buckwheat flour with no wheat added to the noodle itself — the safest option on the menu for those avoiding wheat in the noodle strand, subject to kitchen cross-contamination caveats.

caveats: Hours include an irregular closure on the third Thursday of each month in addition to the regular Wednesday closure — verify before visiting. No official website exists; Tabelog is the most reliable source for current hours. All dietary safety assessments are based on publicly available review information; no direct staff confirmation was possible.

name_ja: 濃厚中華そば 佐とう
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内2-8-25
geocode_note: GSI geocode matched to 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内二丁目８番２５号; approximately 1.1km from Nagoya Castle reference point, within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区 丸の内 / 丸の内駅から徒歩3分
seats_text: 10 counter seats, 6 table seats; groups of 3+ seated at tables, 1-2 at counter
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-style ramen shop serving a Japanese business-district crowd; no English menu or staff noted, but ordering is straightforward with a short menu.
