neighborhood: 中区 本丸 (Nakaku, Honmaru — inside Nagoya Castle)
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区本丸1番1号 名古屋城内
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市中区本丸１番. Coordinates place the spot inside Nagoya Castle grounds, approximately 0.23 km from the target pin — well within the 1.61 km threshold.
hours_raw: 9:00–16:45 (last order 16:30); Gold Shachi Morning 9:00–11:00 limited qty daily; on the 8th of each month a formal tea ceremony is held and morning service is unavailable that day
closed_days: 年末年始 (year-end and New Year holidays, approx. Dec 29–Jan 1)
seats_text: Approximately 24 seats in a traditional tatami-adjacent tea room setting overlooking the Ninomaru Garden; no private rooms
cultural_comfort_note: Nagoya Castle draws international visitors and has basic English signage, but the tearoom itself is a quiet, traditional Japanese space where ordering is done simply by pointing at the menu; minimal English interaction expected from staff.
vegan_detail: Traditional wagashi (nerikiri, mitsumame) and plain matcha are typically free of meat, fish, and dairy — making them incidentally vegan in most cases. Mitsumame contains agar jelly, sweet azuki beans, and fruit, all plant-based. However, no vegan labeling exists, no confirmation that dashi or animal-derived additives are absent from sweets, and the Gold Shachi Morning toast almost certainly contains butter/milk. Ask about specific items before ordering.
gf_detail: Pure matcha and most traditional wagashi (nerikiri mochi, mitsumame bean jelly) are rice- and bean-based and typically contain no wheat. However, the Gold Shachi Morning set includes toast (明確に小麦含む). No dedicated gluten-free kitchen, no allergen labeling, no staff allergy training confirmed. Cross-contamination risk is unknown. Celiac diners should stick to matcha with mitsumame or nerikiri and confirm ingredients on the day — avoid the morning toast set entirely.

bio.background: 二の丸茶亭 is a traditional tearoom operated by the Nagoya Castle Promotion Company (名古屋城振興株式会社) inside the grounds of Nagoya Castle, situated beside the designated scenic Ninomaru Garden — historically the garden of the Owari Tokugawa lords, the largest castle-lord residential garden in Japan at roughly 30,000 square meters. The tearoom has been serving visitors for many decades as an integrated rest stop within one of Japan's most-visited castle complexes.
bio.origin: 名古屋城内 二之丸庭園 (Ninomaru Garden, Nagoya Castle)
bio.specialty: Matcha tea served with seasonal wagashi confections; gold-leaf matcha (金箔抹茶) is the premium signature offering
bio.philosophy: A place to slow down mid-castle-visit and experience a moment of traditional tea culture, framed by a nationally designated scenic garden — the tearoom's ethos is quiet appreciation of season and setting rather than culinary ambition.
roles: City-affiliated tearoom (名古屋城振興株式会社 operation)

anecdotes.text: A replica of the legendary golden tea kettle (黄金の茶釜の写し) — said to be modeled after one cast from melted gold shachihoko roof ornaments destroyed during wartime — is displayed inside the tearoom. On the 8th of each month a formal tea ceremony is held using this kettle, and every Friday matcha is brewed from it for regular guests.

The Gold Shachi Morning (金シャチモーニング), offered daily 9:00–11:00 in limited quantities, pairs matcha, coffee, or orange juice with an original gold-shachi-shaped sweet toast topped with real gold leaf — a playful nod to Nagoya Castle's iconic gilded roof ornaments.

Despite its prime tourist location, 4travel reviewers note the tearoom is consistently less crowded than the main castle area, making it a peaceful escape — described as offering a '風流な雰囲気' (refined, cultivated atmosphere) with traditional red cloth seating and decorative red umbrellas.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about kitchen separation or cross-contamination controls is publicly available. The tearoom is a small traditional operation with no allergen documentation found online.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce-based dishes appear on the menu. The menu is limited to matcha, wagashi sweets, and mitsumame — soy sauce is not a standard ingredient in any of these. However, wagashi recipes are not disclosed and hidden wheat starch in confections cannot be ruled out.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No dairy or egg dishes visible on the menu alongside the matcha and wagashi offerings, but no formal separation protocol confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information found regarding staff allergy training or allergy-aware ordering procedures. The tearoom is a compact, city-run facility without documented allergy protocols.

positives.text: The core menu — plain matcha and mitsumame (agar jelly, sweet azuki beans, fruit) — uses ingredients that are traditionally wheat-free, dairy-free, and meat-free, making incidental safety plausible for those with moderate sensitivities.
Menu is short and simple (matcha, wagashi, mitsumame), reducing the number of unknowns compared to a full kitchen restaurant.

caveats: Hours vary across sources (Tabelog says 09:00-16:00 LO 15:30; official nagoyajo.jp says 09:00-16:45 LO 16:30; Nagoya tourist concierge says 09:00-16:30). The official castle website figure (16:45 close, 16:30 LO) is used as most authoritative but verify on-site, especially as hours may shift seasonally. Entry to the tearoom requires purchasing a Nagoya Castle admission ticket (adults ¥500 as of last check). The Gold Shachi Morning sells out quickly. On the 8th of each month, morning service is replaced by a formal tea ceremony. Cash only — no cards, IC cards, or QR payment accepted.

cuisine: ブラジル料理・オーガニック・カフェ
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内1-8-39 HP丸の内ビル 1F
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 丸の内一丁目8番39号. Distance from Nagoya Castle ~1.15km, within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 中区丸の内（地下鉄丸の内駅8番出口より徒歩4分）
seats_text: カウンター5席、テーブル24席（最大32名貸切可）
cultural_comfort_note: Welcoming solo-diner layout and online reservation via HotPepper suggest a moderate degree of accessibility, though the menu and signage are Japanese-only.
vegan_detail: The menu centers on Brazilian meat dishes (picanha steak, moqueca seafood stew, beef-tongue curry) with organic vegetables on the side. No dedicated vegan dishes are listed. Hidden animal-based broths and animal proteins are likely throughout. Ask staff before ordering.
gf_detail: The restaurant emphasizes organic, additive-free ingredients and avoids chemical seasonings, but makes no gluten-free claims. Brazilian-style stews and grills may involve wheat-containing soy sauce or marinades. No dedicated GF menu, no allergen labeling found online. Cross-contamination risk is unknown. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

bio.background: カバッチは2026年3月に名古屋・丸の内のHP丸の内ビル1階にオープンしたブラジル料理カフェ。農薬不使用の地元農家直送野菜とブラジル伝統料理を組み合わせたコンセプトを掲げ、女性一人でも入りやすいレトロでかわいらしい内装が特徴。
bio.specialty: ピカーニャ（ブラジル式300gステーキ）、ムケッカ（ブラジル伝統のコクミルクベースシーフードシチュー）、牛タンカレーランチ
bio.philosophy: 化学調味料に頼らず素材本来の旨みを大切に。地元農家から届くオーガニック野菜と南米の家庭料理の知恵を融合し、体に寄り添う食事を提供する。

caveats: Very recently opened (March 2026); Tabelog reviews may be sparse. No GF or vegan certifications found. Organic-focused but not allergy-safe by design. Menu is meat- and seafood-centric; vegan and celiac diners face high uncertainty.

cuisine: Organic vegetable cafe, chopped salad, seafood bar
address_ja: 愛知県名古屋市中区二の丸1-2 金シャチ横丁 宗春ゾーン
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 愛知県名古屋市中区二の丸1番; result resolved to chō level. Coordinates place the restaurant approximately 274m from Nagoya Castle reference point (35.1856, 136.8997), well within the 1.61km radius. The restaurant is physically within Kinshachi Yokocho which is immediately adjacent to Nagoya Castle grounds.
neighborhood: 中区二の丸, 金シャチ横丁宗春ゾーン（Nagoya Castle Kinshachi Yokocho）
closed_days: Follows Nagoya Castle closure schedule (名古屋城に準ずる); irregular closures
seats_text: 54 seats total: 16 table seats, 12 sofa seats, 11 counter seats, 15 terrace seats
cultural_comfort_note: A Japanese-language environment inside a popular tourist-facing complex at Nagoya Castle; staff serve international visitors regularly but menus and signage are primarily in Japanese.
vegan_detail: The menu is vegetable-forward but definitively omnivorous. Lunch-focused chopped salads can be customized with vegetable toppings, and a vegetable curry is listed. However, the menu also includes tebasaki (chicken wings), oysters, wagyu beef, seafood, and Nagoya specialty meat dishes. No dishes are labeled vegan, and hidden animal-derived ingredients (dashi, bonito, cheese) are likely present in many preparations. Vegans should inquire carefully before ordering.
gf_detail: No gluten-free labeling or dedicated GF menu exists. The menu includes multiple wheat-containing items: burgers with buns, tempura (red snapper tempura listed), curry (roux likely contains wheat), and standard Japanese soy sauce used throughout. Soy sauce in Japan typically contains wheat. No dedicated fryer information found. Cross-contamination risk is high in a busy kitchen that fries tempura and uses wheat-based sauces. Celiacs should not eat here without direct staff consultation and should be prepared for limited safe options.
caveats: Hours conflict across sources: the official Kinshachi Yokocho listing and aichinagoya.mediajapan.jp agree on 10:30-22:30 (Sun 10:30-20:00), while Tabelog and HotPepper show evening-only hours, which may reflect an outdated or alternate mode. The official yokocho page is treated as most authoritative. Address shows as either 二の丸1-2 or 1-3 depending on source; 1-2 used per HotPepper and Tabelog. The official website (nagoyajo-saien.com) had TLS certificate errors and could not be fetched directly.

bio.background: An independent dining concept opened within Kinshachi Yokocho, the food and souvenir complex built in 2018 alongside Nagoya Castle as part of a city-led revitalization project. The Soharu Zone where saien sits is themed around Tokugawa Munenari, one of the castle's historical lords.
bio.specialty: Chopped salad bowls (lunch) made with own-farm organic vegetables; soft-shell crab burger
bio.philosophy: The name 'saien' fuses two Japanese ideas: 菜園 (saien, vegetable garden) and 野菜で宴 (yasai de utage, a feast with vegetables). Owner Muto-san states the kitchen's commitment is to vegetables with vibrant colors used abundantly, sourced from the restaurant's own farm, paired with fresh seafood brought in daily from the market.
bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture; opened as part of the 2018 Kinshachi Yokocho development

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's concept draws on New York salad culture — the idea of building a complete, satisfying meal around a composed salad — and transplants it to the shadow of Nagoya Castle, combining it with local seafood and Nagoya specialty dishes like tebasaki and dote-ni.

A collaboration menu tied to Nagoya's three great unifiers (the three busho: Nobunaga, Hideyoshi, Ieyasu) featured a roasted miso vegetable platter, abalone risotto with raclette, and red snapper tempura with matcha salt, each priced at ¥999 as a nod to the castle grounds' cultural history.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is on the menu (red snapper tempura listed in collaboration menus), indicating wheat-flour frying occurs in the kitchen. No information about a dedicated fryer or separate frying station was found.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned anywhere in the menu or restaurant descriptions. Standard Japanese soy sauce (which contains wheat) is expected to be used in seasoning.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen prepares meat (tebasaki chicken wings, wagyu steak, pork), dairy (raclette cheese dishes), and seafood alongside vegetable dishes on the same line; no separation policy is documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy, allergy menu, or staff allergy training information was found across any source reviewed (official site, Tabelog, HotPepper, Retty, or blog coverage).

positives.text: The restaurant's core identity is vegetable-forward: it grows its own organic vegetables on a dedicated farm and sources fresh seafood daily, meaning the produce quality is high and plant-based dishes are a genuine focus rather than an afterthought.
